- **ID:** D-0925
  - **Title:** NA-0469 qsc CLI identity rotation provider RNG failure test seam implementation
  - **Status:** Accepted
  - **Date:** 2026-06-13
  - **Goals:** G1, G2, G3, G4, G5
  - **Decision:** NA-0469 consumes NA-0468 and implements the bounded qsc CLI identity rotation provider RNG failure test seam selected by D-0923 and restored by D-0924. The seam is compiled only under `--cfg qsc_rng_failure_test_seam`, uses separate labels `QSC.IDENTITY.ROTATE.KEM_KEYPAIR` and `QSC.IDENTITY.ROTATE.SIG_KEYPAIR`, and leaves normal no-cfg production semantics unchanged.
  - **Exact changed implementation paths:** `qsl/qsl-client/qsc/src/main.rs`; `qsl/qsl-client/qsc/src/identity/mod.rs`.
  - **Exact test file:** `qsl/qsl-client/qsc/tests/cli_identity_rotation_provider_rng_failure.rs`.
  - **Forced CLI rotation KEM provider failure evidence:** Forced `QSC.IDENTITY.ROTATE.KEM_KEYPAIR` returns sanitized `identity_secret_unavailable` / `rng_failure_forced` before any durable CLI rotation write. Markers: `NA0469_CLI_ROTATE_KEM_RNG_FAILURE_FORCED_OK` and `NA0469_CLI_ROTATE_KEM_RNG_FAILURE_NO_PARTIAL_ROTATION_STATE_OK`.
  - **Forced CLI rotation signature provider failure evidence:** Forced `QSC.IDENTITY.ROTATE.SIG_KEYPAIR` returns sanitized `identity_secret_unavailable` / `rng_failure_forced` after KEM generation but before vault, public-record, or peer-reset writes. Markers: `NA0469_CLI_ROTATE_SIG_RNG_FAILURE_FORCED_OK` and `NA0469_CLI_ROTATE_SIG_RNG_FAILURE_NO_PARTIAL_ROTATION_STATE_OK`.
  - **Selected identity stability evidence:** The cfg tests prove the selected Alice identity fingerprint and `identities/self_alice.json` remain byte-for-byte unchanged after forced failure. Marker: `NA0469_CLI_ROTATE_SELECTED_IDENTITY_STABLE_OK`.
  - **No new or partial KEM/signature identity secret write evidence:** The cfg tests decrypt the temporary mock vault and prove `identity.kem_sk.alice`, `identity.sig_sk.alice`, and `vault.qsv` remain unchanged after forced KEM and signature failures. Markers: `NA0469_CLI_ROTATE_NO_PARTIAL_KEM_SECRET_WRITE_OK` and `NA0469_CLI_ROTATE_NO_PARTIAL_SIG_SECRET_WRITE_OK`.
  - **No partial self public-record write/update evidence:** The cfg tests prove `identities/self_alice.json` remains byte-for-byte unchanged after forced failures. Marker: `NA0469_CLI_ROTATE_NO_PARTIAL_PUBLIC_RECORD_WRITE_OK`.
  - **Peer-reset/contact state unchanged evidence:** The cfg tests invoke CLI rotation with `--reset-peers` and prove contact list output plus a seeded legacy `peer_bob.fp` file remain unchanged, so the peer-reset/contact cleanup path is not reached. Marker: `NA0469_CLI_ROTATE_PEER_RESET_STATE_UNCHANGED_OK`.
  - **No dependent handshake/session output evidence:** The cfg tests prove no `handshake_send`, no `handshake_complete`, no `qsp_session_store`, no pending handshake vault secret, no legacy pending file, and no session blob are produced. Marker: `NA0469_CLI_ROTATE_NO_DEPENDENT_HANDSHAKE_OUTPUT_OK`.
  - **Production semantics unchanged without cfg:** The no-cfg test sets `QSC_RNG_FAILURE_TEST_SEAM=QSC.IDENTITY.ROTATE.KEM_KEYPAIR` and proves normal CLI identity rotation succeeds, writes identity public record and KEM/signature identity secrets, emits `identity_fp=...`, and does not emit forced-failure output. Marker: `NA0469_PRODUCTION_SEMANTICS_UNCHANGED_OK`.
  - **Lazy identity background preserved:** cfg/no-cfg `lazy_identity_provider_rng_failure` remains green. Lazy identity evidence remains bounded background only.
  - **Legacy/public-record background preserved:** cfg/no-cfg `legacy_identity_public_record_provider_rng_failure` remains green. Legacy/public-record evidence remains bounded background only.
  - **TUI account bootstrap deferred:** TUI account bootstrap identity generation remains separate and outside NA-0469.
  - **X25519 deferred:** X25519 / ephemeral generation remains residual and handshake source is not mutated by NA-0469.
  - **refimpl provider RNG deferred:** refimpl provider RNG remains residual and refimpl is not mutated by NA-0469.
  - **A2/B1/KEM background preserved:** cfg/no-cfg A2 signature, B1 signature, and KEM provider RNG tests remain green. These are bounded background checks only.
  - **No qshield-cli mutation:** qshield-cli remains a demo-local residual and is not mutated by NA-0469.
  - **No refimpl mutation:** refimpl source is not mutated by NA-0469.
  - **No dependency/Cargo/lockfile/workflow mutation:** NA-0469 does not mutate dependencies, Cargo manifests, lockfiles, workflows, fuzz targets, vectors, formal models, qsl-server, qsl-attachments, qshield runtime, website, public docs, README, START_HERE, qwork/qstart/qresume/qshell, backup/restore/local-ops paths, qsl-backup, backup status files, backup plan files, rollback subtree paths, or backup tree paths.
  - **No backup/restore:** Codex did not run backup or restore. Codex did not run sudo. Codex did not mutate qsl-backup, backup status files, backup plan files, rollback subtree paths, timers, fstab, source lists, retention, backup scripts, or `/backup/qsl`.
  - **Public claim boundary:** No public crypto-complete claim is made. No signature-complete claim is made. No identity-complete claim is made. No RNG-failure-complete claim is made. No provider-RNG-complete claim is made. No side-channel-free claim is made. No vulnerability-free claim is made. No bug-free claim is made. No perfect-crypto claim is made. Cargo audit green remains dependency-health evidence only.
  - **Selected successor:** `NA-0470 -- QSL qsc TUI Account Bootstrap Identity Provider RNG Failure Scope Authorization Plan`.
  - **Required behavior:**
    - NA-0469 evidence must remain CLI-rotation-only internal qsc evidence.
    - Lazy identity, legacy/public-record, A2, B1, KEM, route/contact/attachment, base RNG seam, key lifecycle, and provider-error evidence remain bounded background checks only.
    - TUI account bootstrap identity generation, X25519 / ephemeral generation, refimpl provider RNG, qshield-cli demo RNG, formal/model RNG, and fuzz/vector RNG remain residual unless a later exact directive authorizes them.
    - Optional closeout must not implement NA-0470.
    - Exactly one READY item remains mandatory.
  - **Must never happen:**
    - NA-0469 evidence is represented as all identity-provider RNG coverage.
    - NA-0469 evidence is represented as identity completion, signature completion, RNG-failure completion, provider-RNG completion, or crypto completion.
    - Cargo audit output must not be used as public-readiness, production-readiness, public-internet-readiness, external-review-complete, crypto-complete, identity-complete, RNG-failure-complete, provider-RNG-complete, vulnerability-free, bug-free, perfect-crypto, or side-channel-free proof.
    - more than one READY item remains.

- **ID:** D-0928
  - **Title:** NA-0470 qsc TUI account bootstrap identity provider RNG failure scope authorization
  - **Status:** Accepted
  - **Date:** 2026-06-13
  - **Goals:** G1, G2, G3, G4, G5
  - **Decision:** NA-0470 consumes D-0927 and NA-0469, inventories qsc TUI account bootstrap identity provider RNG scope, and classifies the surface as `TUI_BOOTSTRAP_REQUIRES_DESIGN_CHANGE`. Read-only review shows TUI bootstrap generates both KEM and signature identity keypairs in `qsl/qsl-client/qsc/src/tui/controller/commands/locked.rs`, but only after vault creation, alias/default setting writes, verification seed generation, relay default fields, and TUI relay inbox token state are already written. A simple cfg-only identity provider RNG seam could prove no partial identity/public-record write, but cannot truthfully prove no partial account/bootstrap state under current ordering.
  - **D0927 assurance recovery consumed:** D-0927 is consumed before NA-0470 execution. Its assurance sections remain active and do not widen NA-0469 beyond bounded CLI rotation evidence.
  - **NA-0469 consumed:** NA-0469 CLI rotation provider RNG evidence is consumed as bounded background only. CLI rotation labels `QSC.IDENTITY.ROTATE.KEM_KEYPAIR` and `QSC.IDENTITY.ROTATE.SIG_KEYPAIR` remain complete only for the CLI rotation path.
  - **Selected NA-0471 successor:** `NA-0471 -- QSL qsc TUI Account Bootstrap Transactionality Design Authorization Plan`.
  - **Exact future design scope:** Future NA-0471 may mutate only `docs/governance/evidence/NA-0471_qsl_qsc_tui_account_bootstrap_transactionality_design_authorization_plan.md`, `tests/NA-0471_qsl_qsc_tui_account_bootstrap_transactionality_design_authorization_testplan.md`, `DECISIONS.md`, `TRACEABILITY.md`, and `docs/ops/ROLLING_OPERATIONS_JOURNAL.md`, with read-only inspection of `qsl/qsl-client/qsc/src/tui/controller/commands/locked.rs`, `qsl/qsl-client/qsc/src/identity/mod.rs`, `qsl/qsl-client/qsc/src/vault/mod.rs`, qsc tests, and NA-0470 evidence. It must decide whether to pre-generate identity material before durable account/default writes, roll back durable state after identity provider failure, narrow the invariant to identity-only evidence, or defer to Assurance Gap Review.
  - **Assurance gap trigger classification:** `HIGHER_PRIORITY_RESIDUAL_SUPERSEDES_ASSURANCE_REVIEW`. Assurance Gap Review remains required after this direct residual chain unless a later directive proves another higher-priority residual.
  - **No implementation mutation:** NA-0470 does not implement a TUI bootstrap seam and does not mutate runtime code, crypto code, qsc source, qshield-cli source, refimpl source, executable tests, fuzz targets, vectors, or formal models.
  - **No dependency/Cargo/lockfile/workflow mutation:** NA-0470 does not mutate dependencies, Cargo manifests, lockfiles, workflows, qsl-server, qsl-attachments, qshield runtime, website, public docs, README, START_HERE, qwork/qstart/qresume/qshell, backup/restore/local-ops paths, qsl-backup, backup status files, backup plan files, rollback subtree paths, or backup tree paths.
  - **No backup/restore:** Codex did not run backup or restore. Codex did not run sudo. Codex did not mutate qsl-backup, backup status files, backup plan files, rollback subtree paths, timers, fstab, source lists, retention, backup scripts, or `/backup/qsl`.
  - **Public claim boundary:** No public crypto-complete claim is made. No KEM-complete claim is made. No signature-complete claim is made. No identity-complete claim is made. No RNG-failure-complete claim is made. No provider-RNG-complete claim is made. No side-channel-free claim is made. No vulnerability-free claim is made. No bug-free claim is made. No perfect-crypto claim is made. No public-readiness claim is made. No production-readiness claim is made. No external-review-complete claim is made. It classifies TUI account bootstrap as `TUI_BOOTSTRAP_REQUIRES_DESIGN_CHANGE` because current bootstrap ordering writes vault/account/default/verification/relay state before KEM and signature identity keypair generation. It does not implement a TUI bootstrap seam.
  - **NA-0471 restored scope:** NA-0471 is governance-only transactionality design authorization. It may mutate only `docs/governance/evidence/NA-0471_qsl_qsc_tui_account_bootstrap_transactionality_design_authorization_plan.md`, `tests/NA-0471_qsl_qsc_tui_account_bootstrap_transactionality_design_authorization_testplan.md`, `DECISIONS.md`, `TRACEABILITY.md`, and `docs/ops/ROLLING_OPERATIONS_JOURNAL.md`, with read-only inspection of `qsl/qsl-client/qsc/src/tui/controller/commands/locked.rs`, `qsl/qsl-client/qsc/src/identity/mod.rs`, `qsl/qsl-client/qsc/src/vault/mod.rs`, qsc tests, and NA-0470 evidence.
  - **Design questions for NA-0471:** NA-0471 must decide whether future bootstrap behavior should pre-generate identity material before durable account/default writes, roll back durable bootstrap state after identity provider failure, narrow future evidence to identity/public-record-only invariants, or defer TUI bootstrap implementation to Assurance Gap Review.
  - **Assurance gap trigger status:** D-0928 classified the trigger as `HIGHER_PRIORITY_RESIDUAL_SUPERSEDES_ASSURANCE_REVIEW`. Assurance Gap Review remains required after this direct residual chain unless a later directive proves another higher-priority residual.
  - **No implementation mutation by closeout:** This closeout does not mutate qsc source, executable qsc tests, runtime behavior, crypto behavior, dependencies, Cargo manifests, lockfiles, workflows, fuzz targets, vectors, formal models, refimpl, qsl-server, qsl-attachments, qshield runtime, qshield-cli, website, public docs, README, START_HERE, qwork/qstart/qresume/qshell, backup/restore/local-ops paths, qsl-backup, backup status files, backup plan files, rollback subtree paths, or backup tree paths.

  - **Public claim boundary:** No public-readiness claim is made. No production-readiness claim is made. No public-internet-readiness claim is made. No external-review-complete claim is made. No crypto-complete claim is made. No KEM-complete claim is made. No signature-complete claim is made. No identity-complete claim is made. No RNG-failure-complete claim is made. No provider-RNG-complete claim is made. No secret-material-complete claim is made. No side-channel-free claim is made. No vulnerability-free claim is made. No bug-free claim is made. No perfect-crypto claim is made. Cargo audit green remains dependency-health evidence only.

- **ID:** D-0930
  - **Title:** NA-0471 qsc TUI account bootstrap transactionality design authorization
  - **Status:** Accepted
  - **Date:** 2026-06-13
  - **Goals:** G1, G2, G3, G4, G5
  - **Decision:** NA-0471 consumes NA-0470, consumes D-0927 assurance recovery context, classifies current qsc TUI account bootstrap write order, and selects `TUI_BOOTSTRAP_PREGENERATION_IMPLEMENTATION_READY`. Future TUI account bootstrap should generate identity KEM/signature material before durable vault/account/default writes so forced identity provider RNG failure can occur before vault creation, profile alias storage, account defaults, verification seed, relay endpoint/token fields, TUI relay inbox token state, identity secret writes, self public-record writes, and setup-success output. The selected successor is `NA-0472 -- QSL qsc TUI Account Bootstrap Pre-Generation Transactionality Implementation Harness`.
  - **NA-0470 consumed:** NA-0470's `TUI_BOOTSTRAP_REQUIRES_DESIGN_CHANGE` classification is consumed. Its write-order finding remains controlling: current TUI `/init` and init wizard call `tui_try_vault_init`, then `initialize_account_after_init`, which stores alias/defaults before `init_identity_with_passphrase` generates KEM/signature identity keypairs and writes identity secrets plus the self public record.
  - **D0927 assurance recovery context consumed:** D-0927's Level-1 stewardship and assurance requirements remain active. NA-0471 preserves the D-0927 distinction between internal governance evidence and external/public claim evidence.
  - **Transactionality option selected:** Pre-generation is selected over staged commit, rollback, identity-only invariant, path-specific split, documentation-only, and immediate Assurance Gap Review. Pre-generation is the best-known bounded design because it prevents identity-provider failure from happening after durable account/default writes. It also avoids rollback deletion risk and avoids inventing a vault-wide transaction abstraction in this lane.
  - **Exact future scope:** Future NA-0472 may mutate `qsl/qsl-client/qsc/src/tui/controller/commands/locked.rs`, `qsl/qsl-client/qsc/src/identity/mod.rs`, `qsl/qsl-client/qsc/tests/tui_account_bootstrap_transactionality.rs`, `docs/governance/evidence/NA-0472_qsl_qsc_tui_account_bootstrap_pre_generation_transactionality_implementation_harness.md`, `tests/NA-0472_qsl_qsc_tui_account_bootstrap_pre_generation_transactionality_implementation_testplan.md`, `DECISIONS.md`, `TRACEABILITY.md`, and `docs/ops/ROLLING_OPERATIONS_JOURNAL.md`, unless a later exact directive narrows or changes scope.
  - **Future implementation boundary:** NA-0472 should not require dependency, Cargo, lockfile, workflow, refimpl, qshield-cli, qsl-server, qsl-attachments, website, public-doc, vector, fuzz-target, formal-model, backup, restore, qsl-backup, backup status, backup plan, rollback subtree, `/backup/qsl`, or public technical paper mutation. NA-0472 should not require vault transactionality, new staging infrastructure, new temp-file protocols, or state-machine redesign for the selected identity-provider failure invariant.
  - **Assurance gap trigger classification:** `HIGHER_PRIORITY_RESIDUAL_SUPERSEDES_ASSURANCE_REVIEW`. Assurance Gap Review remains the default after NA-0472 closes unless NA-0472 proves another higher-priority residual.
  - **No implementation mutation:** NA-0471 does not implement TUI bootstrap pre-generation and does not mutate qsc source, runtime code, crypto code, executable tests, fuzz targets, vectors, formal models, refimpl, qsl-server, qsl-attachments, qshield runtime, qshield-cli, website, public docs, README, START_HERE, qwork/qstart/qresume/qshell, backup/restore/local-ops paths, qsl-backup, backup status files, backup plan files, rollback subtree paths, or `/backup/qsl`.
  - **No dependency/Cargo/lockfile/workflow mutation:** NA-0471 does not mutate dependencies, Cargo manifests, lockfiles, or workflows.
  - **No backup/restore:** Codex did not run backup or restore. Codex did not mutate qsl-backup, backup status files, backup plan files, rollback subtree paths, timers, fstab, source lists, retention, backup scripts, or `/backup/qsl`.
  - **Public claim boundary:** No public crypto-complete claim is made. No KEM-complete claim is made. No signature-complete claim is made. No identity-complete claim is made. No RNG-failure-complete claim is made. No provider-RNG-complete claim is made. No side-channel-free claim is made. No vulnerability-free claim is made. No bug-free claim is made. No perfect-crypto claim is made. No public-readiness claim is made. No production-readiness claim is made. No public-internet-readiness claim is made. No external-review-complete claim is made. Cargo audit green remains dependency-health evidence only.
  - **Steward and assurance reviews used:** Level-1 stewardship, best-known-method, hostile cryptographer, red-team, production SRE, side-channel, formal-model mapping residual, external-review readiness, release-claim boundary, and assurance-gap trigger reviews were used. Level 2 and Level 3 remain future-gated. No separate Directors, independent READY promotion, or independent merge authority is created.
  - **Required behavior:**
    - NA-0471 evidence remains internal governance design authorization only.
    - Future NA-0472 must prove selected pre-generation behavior and no-cfg production success behavior before any implementation claim.
    - Exactly one READY item remains mandatory.
  - **Must never happen:**
    - NA-0471 is represented as a TUI bootstrap implementation.
    - No future evidence may represent TUI bootstrap pre-generation authorization as public readiness, production readiness, external review completion, crypto completion, KEM completion, signature completion, identity completion, RNG failure completion, provider RNG completion, side-channel freedom, vulnerability freedom, bug freedom, or perfect crypto.
    - No future evidence may represent Cargo audit dependency-health evidence as public-readiness, production-readiness, or vulnerability-free evidence.
    - more than one READY item remains.
  - **References:** NA-0471; NA-0470; NA-0472; D-0930; D-0929; D-0928; D-0927; `docs/governance/evidence/NA-0471_qsl_qsc_tui_account_bootstrap_transactionality_design_authorization_plan.md`; `tests/NA-0471_qsl_qsc_tui_account_bootstrap_transactionality_design_authorization_testplan.md`; `qsl/qsl-client/qsc/src/tui/controller/commands/locked.rs`; `qsl/qsl-client/qsc/src/identity/mod.rs`; `qsl/qsl-client/qsc/src/vault/mod.rs`; `TRACEABILITY.md`; `docs/ops/ROLLING_OPERATIONS_JOURNAL.md`

D-0930 records the bounded qsc TUI account bootstrap transactionality design authorization, selected `TUI_BOOTSTRAP_PREGENERATION_IMPLEMENTATION_READY`, and selected `NA-0472 -- QSL qsc TUI Account Bootstrap Pre-Generation Transactionality Implementation Harness` as the successor. - **ID:** D-0932
  - **Title:** NA-0472 qsc TUI account bootstrap pre-generation transactionality implementation
  - **Status:** Accepted
  - **Date:** 2026-06-13
  - **Goals:** G1, G2, G3, G4, G5
  - **Decision:** NA-0472 consumes NA-0471 and implements the selected qsc TUI account bootstrap pre-generation transactionality invariant. TUI `/init` and the init wizard now pre-generate bootstrap identity KEM/signature material before `tui_try_vault_init` and before durable account/default writes, so forced TUI bootstrap identity provider RNG failure occurs before vault, account/default, identity secret, and self public-record writes.
  - **NA-0471 consumed:** D-0930 selected `TUI_BOOTSTRAP_PREGENERATION_IMPLEMENTATION_READY`; D-0931 restored NA-0472 as READY. NA-0472 implements that selected direct residual and does not widen into vault transactionality, rollback cleanup, staged commit infrastructure, refimpl work, qshield-cli work, dependency work, workflow work, fuzz/vector work, or formal model mutation.
  - **Exact changed implementation paths:** `qsl/qsl-client/qsc/src/tui/controller/commands/locked.rs`; `qsl/qsl-client/qsc/src/identity/mod.rs`.
  - **Exact test file:** `qsl/qsl-client/qsc/tests/tui_account_bootstrap_transactionality.rs`.
  - **cfg-only seam labels:** `QSC.TUI.BOOTSTRAP.IDENTITY.KEM_KEYPAIR`; `QSC.TUI.BOOTSTRAP.IDENTITY.SIG_KEYPAIR`.
  - **Forced TUI bootstrap KEM provider failure evidence:** cfg test `tui_bootstrap_kem_rng_failure_writes_no_partial_account_state` forces `QSC.TUI.BOOTSTRAP.IDENTITY.KEM_KEYPAIR`, emits sanitized `identity_secret_unavailable` / `rng_failure_forced`, and emits marker `NA0472_TUI_BOOTSTRAP_KEM_RNG_FAILURE_FORCED_OK`.
  - **Forced TUI bootstrap signature provider failure evidence:** cfg test `tui_bootstrap_sig_rng_failure_writes_no_partial_account_state` forces `QSC.TUI.BOOTSTRAP.IDENTITY.SIG_KEYPAIR`, emits sanitized `identity_secret_unavailable` / `rng_failure_forced`, and emits marker `NA0472_TUI_BOOTSTRAP_SIG_RNG_FAILURE_FORCED_OK`.
  - **No partial account/default state evidence:** forced KEM and signature failures occur before `tui_try_vault_init`, leave no `vault.qsv` or `vault.qsv.tmp`, and therefore leave no vault-backed `profile_alias`, TUI defaults, verification seed, relay endpoint/token, or relay inbox token state. Markers: `NA0472_TUI_BOOTSTRAP_NO_PARTIAL_ACCOUNT_STATE_OK`; `NA0472_TUI_BOOTSTRAP_NO_PARTIAL_DEFAULT_CONFIG_OK`.
  - **No partial identity state evidence:** forced KEM and signature failures write no `identity.kem_sk.self`, no `identity.sig_sk.self`, and no `identities/self_self.json`; any TUI-created `identities/` directory remains absent or empty. Markers: `NA0472_TUI_BOOTSTRAP_NO_PARTIAL_IDENTITY_STATE_OK`; `NA0472_TUI_BOOTSTRAP_NO_IDENTITY_KEM_SECRET_WRITE_OK`; `NA0472_TUI_BOOTSTRAP_NO_IDENTITY_SIG_SECRET_WRITE_OK`; `NA0472_TUI_BOOTSTRAP_NO_SELF_PUBLIC_RECORD_WRITE_OK`.
  - **No misleading setup-success output evidence:** forced KEM and signature failures emit `event=tui_init_reject` with `reason=identity_init_failed`, do not emit `event=tui_init ok=true`, do not emit `alias=stored_local_only`, do not emit `identity_fp=`, and do not expose identity secret names or the TUI passphrase. Marker: `NA0472_TUI_BOOTSTRAP_NO_MISLEADING_SUCCESS_OUTPUT_OK`.
  - **Production semantics unchanged without cfg:** no-cfg test `tui_bootstrap_pregeneration_seam_inactive_without_cfg` sets `QSC_RNG_FAILURE_TEST_SEAM` to the TUI KEM label and proves normal TUI bootstrap succeeds, writes the vault, account defaults, identity secrets, and self public record, and emits marker `NA0472_PRODUCTION_SEMANTICS_UNCHANGED_OK`.
  - **In-memory secret lifetime caveat preserved:** pre-generation increases in-memory lifetime for generated bootstrap identity secrets during the durable commit sequence. The implementation zeroizes newly pre-generated secret vectors and temporary hex strings at the new storage boundary where practical, but no side-channel-free claim, no memory-erasure completeness claim, and no secret-material-complete claim is made. Marker: `NA0472_IN_MEMORY_SECRET_LIFETIME_CAVEAT_PRESERVED_OK`.
  - **CLI/lazy/legacy/A2/B1/KEM background preserved:** post-implementation cfg/no-cfg inherited qsc tests for CLI rotation, lazy identity, legacy/public-record, A2 signature, B1 signature, and KEM provider RNG remained green. These remain bounded background checks only.
  - **X25519 deferred:** X25519 / ephemeral generation remains residual and handshake source is not mutated by NA-0472.
  - **refimpl provider RNG deferred:** refimpl provider RNG remains residual and refimpl is not mutated by NA-0472.
  - **No qshield-cli mutation:** qshield-cli remains a demo-local residual and is not mutated by NA-0472.
  - **No dependency/Cargo/lockfile/workflow mutation:** NA-0472 does not mutate dependencies, Cargo manifests, lockfiles, workflows, fuzz targets, vectors, formal models, qsl-server, qsl-attachments, qshield runtime, website, public docs, README, START_HERE, qwork/qstart/qresume/qshell, backup/restore/local-ops paths, qsl-backup, backup status files, backup plan files, rollback subtree paths, or `/backup/qsl`.
  - **No backup/restore:** Codex did not run backup or restore. Codex did not run sudo. Codex did not mutate qsl-backup, backup status files, backup plan files, rollback subtree paths, timers, fstab, source lists, retention, backup scripts, or `/backup/qsl`.
  - **Validation:** `cargo fmt --check`, cfg/no-cfg `tui_account_bootstrap_transactionality`, inherited qsc provider RNG tests, qsc `key_lifecycle_zeroization`, qsc `handshake_provider_error_no_mutation`, stable qsc `send_commit`, refimpl `pqkem768`, root cargo audit, nested qsc fuzz lock audit, qsc adversarial script syntax, dependency probes, and formal model scripts passed locally before PR.
  - **Assurance gap trigger classification:** `ASSURANCE_GAP_REVIEW_REQUIRED_AFTER_CURRENT_CHAIN`. NA-0472 completes the direct TUI bootstrap pre-generation residual selected by NA-0471; Assurance Gap Review is the default successor unless post-merge evidence proves a higher-priority residual.
  - **Selected successor:** `NA-0473 -- QSL Identity / Provider RNG Assurance Gap Review Plan`.
  - **Public claim boundary:** No public crypto-complete claim is made. No KEM-complete claim is made. No signature-complete claim is made. No identity-complete claim is made. No RNG-failure-complete claim is made. No provider-RNG-complete claim is made. No side-channel-free claim is made. No vulnerability-free claim is made. No bug-free claim is made. No perfect-crypto claim is made. No public-readiness claim is made. No production-readiness claim is made. No public-internet-readiness claim is made. No external-review-complete claim is made. Cargo audit green remains dependency-health evidence only.
  - **Required behavior:**
    - NA-0472 evidence remains bounded qsc TUI bootstrap internal evidence.
    - Normal no-cfg TUI bootstrap behavior must keep ignoring the seam selector.
    - The in-memory secret lifetime caveat remains active.
    - Optional closeout must not implement NA-0473.
    - Exactly one READY item remains mandatory.
  - **Must never happen:**
    - NA-0472 evidence is represented as identity completion, signature completion, RNG-failure completion, provider-RNG completion, crypto completion, side-channel freedom, vulnerability freedom, bug freedom, or perfect crypto.
    - No Cargo audit output may be represented as public-readiness, production-readiness, public-internet-readiness, external-review-complete, crypto-complete, vulnerability-free, bug-free, perfect-crypto, or side-channel-free proof.
    - more than one READY item remains.
  - **References:** NA-0472; NA-0471; NA-0473; D-0932; D-0931; D-0930; `docs/governance/evidence/NA-0472_qsl_qsc_tui_account_bootstrap_pre_generation_transactionality_implementation_harness.md`; `tests/NA-0472_qsl_qsc_tui_account_bootstrap_pre_generation_transactionality_implementation_testplan.md`; `qsl/qsl-client/qsc/src/tui/controller/commands/locked.rs`; `qsl/qsl-client/qsc/src/identity/mod.rs`; `qsl/qsl-client/qsc/tests/tui_account_bootstrap_transactionality.rs`; `TRACEABILITY.md`; `docs/ops/ROLLING_OPERATIONS_JOURNAL.md`

- **ID:** D-0936
  - **Title:** NA-0474 KEM signature transcript binding read-only audit
  - **Status:** Accepted
  - **Date:** 2026-06-13
  - **Goals:** G1, G2, G3, G4, G5
  - **Decision:** NA-0474 consumes NA-0473 and completes a governance-only, read-only audit of qsc/refimpl KEM, signature, transcript, identity, public-record, suite, replay, downgrade, and state-transition binding evidence. The audit finds meaningful direct and supporting qsc binding evidence, but selects `BINDING_NEGATIVE_TEST_SCOPE_NEXT` because multiple exact negative-test gaps remain across KEM public keys/ciphertexts, signatures, transcript mutation/replay, stale public records, identity rollback, downgrade, suite confusion, and cross-role/cross-message cases.
  - **NA-0473 consumed:** D-0934 completed the identity/provider RNG assurance gap review and selected `CRYPTOGRAPHER_REVIEW_TRANSCRIPT_BINDING_NEXT`; D-0935 closed NA-0473 and restored NA-0474. NA-0474 accepts that evidence as bounded internal governance evidence and does not widen it into identity completion, provider-RNG completion, signature completion, KEM completion, transcript completion, side-channel freedom, external-review completion, public readiness, or vulnerability freedom.
  - **KEM binding reviewed:** qsc A1 carries suite context, session ID, KEM public key, signature public key, and DH public key; responder-side identity pinning checks the A1 KEM public key fingerprint before encapsulation; B1 carries KEM ciphertext; KEM shared secret derivation includes session ID and active suite context. Existing evidence is supporting and partially direct, but swapped/stale/wrong KEM public-key and replayed-ciphertext negative-test scope remains next.
  - **Signature binding reviewed:** qsc B1 and A2 signatures use explicit label-prefixed messages with session ID and transcript hash, and A2 additionally covers the confirmation MAC. Existing evidence covers B1/A2 signature tamper and selected suite/transcript cases, but cross-message replay, wrong identity key, wrong role, wrong suite, stale public-record signature, and domain-separation negative-test scope remains next.
  - **Transcript/KDF/confirmation binding reviewed:** qsc transcript MAC/hash covers A1 and unauthenticated B1 bytes with session ID and active suite context; confirmation key/MAC derivation binds session ID, transcript hash, suite context, and role-specific A2 material. Existing suite-id and transcript mismatch tests are direct for selected cases, but broader mutation, truncation, replay, and mixed-context negative-test scope remains next.
  - **Identity/public-record stale-record reviewed:** qsc identity records bind KEM and signature public keys to peer/contact state through primary KEM fingerprint and optional signature fingerprint checks. Existing identity-binding and provider-RNG evidence is supporting, but stale public record, identity rollback, local rollback, peer/contact stale state, and rotation race negative-test scope remains next.
  - **Downgrade/replay/suite-confusion reviewed:** qsc suite context is encoded into handshake headers and derivations, and suite-id tests cover selected downgrade, stripped, mismatch, duplicate, replay, and no-mutation cases. Broader downgrade/replay/suite-confusion negative-test scope remains next.
  - **qsc/refimpl mapping reviewed:** refimpl `StdCrypto` provides ML-KEM, ML-DSA, KMAC, and Suite-2 supporting evidence, including `pqkem768` behavior. qsc binding assumptions remain the higher-priority direct test target; qsc/refimpl mapping remains supporting-only residual.
  - **Formal/vector/fuzz coverage reviewed:** existing formal models support bounded SCKA, Suite-2 negotiation, and future qsc suite-id fail-closed/no-mutation properties, but do not directly model full KEM/signature/transcript/identity binding. Existing vectors/fuzz are supporting-only for this lane.
  - **Threat scenarios reviewed:** stale public record replay, wrong peer signature public key, wrong peer KEM public key, cross-role replay, cross-suite downgrade, replayed A1/B1/A2, mixed transcript mutation, public-record rollback, identity rotation race, relay/server stale delivery, local storage rollback, and peer/contact stale state were reviewed and ranked in the NA-0474 findings matrix.
  - **Findings matrix accepted:** The accepted matrix ranks KEM binding negative tests, signature binding negative tests, transcript mutation/replay tests, stale public-record/identity rollback tests, and downgrade/suite-confusion tests as P1 next work; qsc/refimpl mapping, formal mapping, negative vectors, fuzz binding, side-channel, and production/SRE residuals remain future or caveat items.
  - **Primary classification selected:** `BINDING_NEGATIVE_TEST_SCOPE_NEXT`.
  - **Selected NA-0475 successor:** `NA-0475 -- QSL qsc KEM / Signature / Transcript Binding Negative Test Scope Authorization Plan`.
  - **No implementation mutation:** NA-0474 does not implement NA-0475 and does not mutate runtime behavior.
  - **No runtime/crypto/dependency/Cargo/lockfile/workflow/test/fuzz/vector/formal mutation:** NA-0474 does not mutate runtime code, crypto code, dependencies, Cargo manifests, lockfiles, workflows, executable tests, fuzz targets, vectors, formal models, refimpl, qsl-server, qsl-attachments, qshield runtime, qshield-cli, website, public docs, README, START_HERE, qwork/qstart/qresume/qshell, qsl-backup, backup status files, backup plan files, rollback subtree paths, `/backup/qsl`, durable Director State Index output, or public technical paper content.
  - **No backup/restore:** Codex did not run backup or restore. Codex did not run sudo. Codex did not mutate qsl-backup, backup status files, backup plan files, rollback subtree paths, timers, fstab, source lists, retention, backup scripts, or `/backup/qsl`.
  - **Public claim boundary:** No public-readiness claim is made. No production-readiness claim is made. No public-internet-readiness claim is made. No external-review-complete claim is made. No crypto-complete claim is made. No KEM-complete claim is made. No signature-complete claim is made. No identity-complete claim is made. No transcript-complete claim is made. No downgrade-proof claim is made. No replay-proof claim is made. No RNG-failure-complete claim is made. No provider-RNG-complete claim is made. No secret-material-complete claim is made. No side-channel-free claim is made. No vulnerability-free claim is made. No bug-free claim is made. No perfect-crypto claim is made. Cargo audit green is dependency-health evidence only.
  - **Required behavior:**
    - NA-0474 evidence remains internal governance evidence only until optional closeout.
    - The selected NA-0475 successor is authorization scoped and must not implement tests unless a later exact directive authorizes exact implementation.
    - Exactly one READY remains mandatory.

- **ID:** D-0938
  - **Title:** NA-0475 qsc KEM signature transcript binding negative test scope authorization
  - **Status:** Accepted
  - **Date:** 2026-06-14
  - **Goals:** G1, G2, G3, G4, G5
  - **Decision:** NA-0475 consumes NA-0474 and authorizes a combined future qsc negative-test implementation scope for KEM, signature, transcript, identity, stale public-record, replay, downgrade, and suite-confusion binding gaps. The primary classification is `BINDING_NEGATIVE_TEST_COMBINED_SCOPE_READY`, and the selected successor is `NA-0476 -- QSL qsc KEM / Signature / Transcript Binding Negative Test Implementation Harness`.
  - **NA-0474 consumed:** D-0936 completed the KEM/signature/transcript binding read-only audit and selected `BINDING_NEGATIVE_TEST_SCOPE_NEXT`; D-0937 closed NA-0474 and restored NA-0475. NA-0475 accepts NA-0474's P1 findings for KEM binding negative tests, signature binding/domain-separation negative tests, transcript mutation/replay tests, stale public-record/identity rollback tests, and downgrade/suite-confusion tests.
  - **KEM negative-test scope reviewed:** Wrong peer KEM public key, stale KEM public key/public record, replayed KEM ciphertext, and corrupted or wrong KEM ciphertext can be tested through existing qsc CLI/mock-relay/temp-config integration surfaces without qsc runtime/source mutation. Expected future rejection evidence includes `NA0476_KEM_WRONG_PUBLIC_KEY_REJECT_OK`, `NA0476_KEM_STALE_PUBLIC_RECORD_REJECT_OK`, and no session/pending mutation evidence.
  - **Signature negative-test scope reviewed:** B1/A2 cross-message replay, wrong B1/A2 signature public key, wrong identity, wrong role, wrong suite, wrong session ID, and stale public-record signature mismatch can be tested through captured A1/B1/A2 frames and test-only splicing/mutation helpers without runtime source mutation. Expected future rejection evidence includes `NA0476_SIGNATURE_WRONG_IDENTITY_REJECT_OK` and `NA0476_SIGNATURE_CROSS_MESSAGE_REPLAY_REJECT_OK`.
  - **Transcript/replay/suite negative-test scope reviewed:** Transcript mutation, transcript truncation, mixed-context transcript, replayed A1/B1/A2, wrong-role replay, downgrade, and suite confusion can be tested by mutating captured qsc frames and reusing existing suite-required and suite-parameter-block test patterns. Expected future rejection evidence includes `NA0476_TRANSCRIPT_MUTATION_REJECT_OK`, `NA0476_TRANSCRIPT_REPLAY_REJECT_OK`, and `NA0476_SUITE_CONFUSION_REJECT_OK`.
  - **Stale public-record and identity rollback scope reviewed:** Stale public-record replay, public-record rollback, identity rotation stale peer state, and stale contact/peer state can be simulated in temporary qsc config roots by snapshotting and restoring identity/public-record/vault fixture data, with no runtime source change. Expected future rejection evidence includes `NA0476_STALE_PUBLIC_RECORD_REJECT_OK`.
  - **Combined vs split decision:** Combined qsc test-only implementation is selected over KEM-only, signature-only, transcript/replay/suite-only, stale-record-only, path-specific split, formal mapping first, vector suite first, and ambiguous triage. The selected future executable test path is `qsl/qsl-client/qsc/tests/kem_signature_transcript_binding_negative.rs`.
  - **Exact future scope:** Future NA-0476 may mutate only `qsl/qsl-client/qsc/tests/kem_signature_transcript_binding_negative.rs`, `docs/governance/evidence/NA-0476_qsl_qsc_kem_signature_transcript_binding_negative_test_implementation_harness.md`, `tests/NA-0476_qsl_qsc_kem_signature_transcript_binding_negative_test_implementation_testplan.md`, `DECISIONS.md`, `TRACEABILITY.md`, and `docs/ops/ROLLING_OPERATIONS_JOURNAL.md`, unless a later exact directive narrows or changes scope.

- **ID:** D-0940
  - **Title:** NA-0476 qsc KEM signature transcript binding negative test implementation
  - **Status:** Accepted
  - **Date:** 2026-06-14
  - **Goals:** G1, G2, G3, G4, G5
  - **Decision:** NA-0476 consumes NA-0475 and implements the exact qsc test-only binding negative-test scope selected by D-0938. The executable test file added is `qsl/qsl-client/qsc/tests/kem_signature_transcript_binding_negative.rs`.
  - **NA-0475 consumed:** D-0938 selected `BINDING_NEGATIVE_TEST_COMBINED_SCOPE_READY`; D-0939 restored NA-0476 as the sole READY implementation successor. NA-0476 consumes that authorization and does not expand it.
  - **KEM negative binding tests implemented:** The harness rejects wrong peer KEM public key, stale KEM public record / stale trusted public record, and corrupted B1 KEM ciphertext cases through existing qsc CLI, mock relay, and temp-root behavior. Markers include `NA0476_KEM_WRONG_PUBLIC_KEY_REJECT_OK`, `NA0476_KEM_STALE_PUBLIC_RECORD_REJECT_OK`, and `NA0476_KEM_WRONG_CIPHERTEXT_REJECT_OK`.
  - **Signature negative binding tests implemented:** The harness rejects wrong signature public-record state and cross-message signature replay by setting a wrong temp-root signature pin and by replaying an A2 signature in a B1 signature field. Markers include `NA0476_SIGNATURE_WRONG_IDENTITY_REJECT_OK` and `NA0476_SIGNATURE_CROSS_MESSAGE_REPLAY_REJECT_OK`.
  - **Transcript/replay/suite negative binding tests implemented:** The harness rejects B1 transcript mutation, A1 replay, and downgrade-style wrong-suite parameter blocks in suite-required mode. Markers include `NA0476_TRANSCRIPT_MUTATION_REJECT_OK`, `NA0476_TRANSCRIPT_REPLAY_REJECT_OK`, and `NA0476_SUITE_CONFUSION_REJECT_OK`.
  - **Stale public-record / identity rollback negative tests implemented:** The harness completes a valid session, rotates Alice identity while Bob retains a stale trusted public record, verifies Bob rejects the new A1, and verifies Bob's existing session bytes remain unchanged. Marker: `NA0476_STALE_PUBLIC_RECORD_REJECT_OK`.
  - **No session mutation evidence:** Pre-session negative cases assert no completed `qsp_sessions/*.qsv` session appears for the affected peer, no B1/A2 output is emitted after rejection, and no handshake completion or qsp plaintext marker is emitted. The stale public-record case captures and compares existing Bob session bytes before and after rejection. Marker: `NA0476_NEGATIVE_TESTS_NO_SESSION_MUTATION_OK`.
  - **No runtime/source mutation outside exact test file:** No qsc runtime/source file under `qsl/qsl-client/qsc/src/**` is modified. The only qsc implementation-side change is the selected integration test file.
  - **No dependency/Cargo/lockfile/workflow mutation:** Dependencies, Cargo manifests, lockfiles, and workflows are unchanged.
  - **No refimpl mutation:** refimpl source and tests are unchanged.
  - **No fuzz/vector/formal mutation:** fuzz targets, vectors, and formal models are unchanged.
  - **No service/public/qshield-cli mutation:** qsl-server, qsl-attachments, qshield runtime, qshield-cli, website, public docs, README, and START_HERE are unchanged.
  - **No backup/restore:** Codex did not run backup or restore and did not mutate qsl-backup, backup status, backup plan, rollback, or backup tree paths.
  - **Public claim boundary:** No public-readiness claim is made. No production-readiness claim is made. No public-internet-readiness claim is made. No external-review-complete claim is made. No crypto-complete claim is made. No KEM-complete claim is made. No signature-complete claim is made. No identity-complete claim is made. No transcript-complete claim is made. No downgrade-proof claim is made. No replay-proof claim is made. No RNG-failure-complete claim is made. No provider-RNG-complete claim is made. No secret-material-complete claim is made. No side-channel-free claim is made. No vulnerability-free claim is made. No bug-free claim is made. No perfect-crypto claim is made. Cargo audit green is dependency-health evidence only.
  - **Selected successor:** `NA-0477 -- QSL KEM / Signature / Transcript Formal Model Mapping Authorization Plan` is selected as the default successor after successful merge and green post-merge public-safety. NA-0476 does not implement NA-0477.
  - **Required behavior:**
    - NA-0476 evidence remains bounded internal qsc negative-test implementation evidence.
    - Optional closeout may restore exactly one READY NA-0477 only after the implementation PR is merged and post-merge public-safety is green.
    - Exactly one READY remains mandatory.
  - **Must never happen:**
    - NA-0476 is represented as qsc runtime/source, crypto, dependency, workflow, refimpl, formal, vector, fuzz, public-doc, website, service, backup, restore, or qsl-backup authorization beyond the selected test-only scope.
    - NA-0476 evidence must not be represented as public readiness, production readiness, public-internet readiness, external review completion, crypto completion, KEM completion, signature completion, identity completion, transcript completion, downgrade proof, replay proof, RNG failure completion, provider RNG completion, secret-material completion, side-channel freedom, vulnerability freedom, bug freedom, backup completion, restore proof, or perfect crypto.
    - Cargo audit output is represented as anything stronger than dependency-health evidence.
    - More than one READY item remains.
  - **References:** NA-0476; NA-0475; D-0940; D-0939; D-0938; `qsl/qsl-client/qsc/tests/kem_signature_transcript_binding_negative.rs`; `docs/governance/evidence/NA-0476_qsl_qsc_kem_signature_transcript_binding_negative_test_implementation_harness.md`; `tests/NA-0476_qsl_qsc_kem_signature_transcript_binding_negative_test_implementation_testplan.md`; `TRACEABILITY.md`; `docs/ops/ROLLING_OPERATIONS_JOURNAL.md`

- **ID:** D-0942
  - **Title:** NA-0477 KEM signature transcript formal model mapping authorization
  - **Status:** Accepted
  - **Date:** 2026-06-14
  - **Goals:** G1, G2, G3, G4, G5
  - **Decision:** NA-0477 consumes NA-0476 and authorizes a future bounded qsc KEM/signature/transcript binding formal model implementation lane. Existing formal models were inventoried; qsc binding negative-test behavior was mapped to candidate formal abstractions; formal mapping options were reviewed; the selected model boundary uses opaque tokens for KEM, signature, transcript, suite, identity/public-record, replay, pending, output, and completed-session state; and the selected primary classification is `FORMAL_MAPPING_QSC_BINDING_MODEL_IMPLEMENTATION_READY`.
  - **NA-0476 consumed:** D-0940 implemented bounded qsc negative-test evidence for wrong KEM public key, stale KEM/public record, corrupted KEM ciphertext, wrong signature identity/public record, cross-message signature replay, transcript mutation, replay, suite confusion, stale public-record reject, and no completed-session mutation on selected rejects. D-0941 closed NA-0476 and restored NA-0477 as the sole READY item.
  - **Existing formal models inventoried:** `formal/model_scka_bounded.py` covers SCKA ADV/CTXT monotonicity, tombstones, and reject no-mutation; `formal/model_suite2_negotiation_bounded.py` covers Suite-2 downgrade/capability commitment reject and negotiation no-mutation; `formal/model_qsc_handshake_suite_id_bounded.py` covers qsc suite-id, downgrade, transcript/key-context, output, recv_commit, leak, and no-mutation invariants. None directly covers KEM public-key binding, KEM ciphertext binding, signature public-record binding, cross-message signature domain replay, identity stale public-record behavior, or the selected qsc completed-session no-mutation set.
  - **Formal mapping options reviewed:** Extending the existing qsc suite-id model was rejected as the primary path because it would overload a narrow suite-id model. Identity stale-record-only, documentation-only, negative-vector-first, and qsc/refimpl-first successors were rejected as lower priority for this lane. A new bounded qsc binding model was selected because it can model the NA-0476 behavior with opaque tokens and no crypto internals.
  - **Model scope boundary selected:** Future NA-0478 should add `formal/model_qsc_kem_signature_transcript_binding_bounded.py` and update `formal/run_model_checks.py`. The model should include abstract A1/B1/A2 messages, roles, suite token, session id token, KEM key/ciphertext tokens, signature key/domain tokens, transcript/confirm context tokens, public-record/trusted-pin tokens, replay/seen state, pending store state, output flags, and completed-session state. It should assert selected reject/no-output/no-completed-session-mutation invariants and include at least one accepted baseline path.
  - **Selected NA-0478 successor:** `NA-0478 -- QSL qsc KEM / Signature / Transcript Binding Formal Model Implementation Harness`.
  - **Exact future formal scope:** Future NA-0478 may mutate only `formal/model_qsc_kem_signature_transcript_binding_bounded.py`, `formal/run_model_checks.py`, `docs/governance/evidence/NA-0478_qsl_qsc_kem_signature_transcript_binding_formal_model_implementation_harness.md`, `tests/NA-0478_qsl_qsc_kem_signature_transcript_binding_formal_model_implementation_testplan.md`, `DECISIONS.md`, `TRACEABILITY.md`, and `docs/ops/ROLLING_OPERATIONS_JOURNAL.md`, unless a later exact directive narrows or changes that scope.
  - **No implementation mutation in this directive:** NA-0477 does not mutate formal model files, runtime code, crypto code, dependencies, Cargo manifests, lockfiles, workflows, executable tests, fuzz targets, vectors, refimpl, qsl-server, qsl-attachments, qshield runtime, qshield-cli, website, public docs, README, START_HERE, qwork/qstart/qresume/qshell, backup status files, backup plan files, rollback paths, backup tree paths, durable Director State Index output, or public technical paper content.
  - **No backup/restore:** Codex did not run backup or restore and did not mutate qsl-backup.
  - **Stewardship and assurance reviews used:** Level-1 stewardship, best-known-method, hostile cryptographer, red-team, production SRE, side-channel, formal-model mapping residual, external-review readiness, release-claim boundary, and assurance-gap trigger reviews were used. Level 2 and Level 3 remain future-gated. No separate Directors, independent READY promotion, or independent merge authority is created. Lead Director final authority is preserved.
  - **Classifications:** Best-known-method classification is `BEST_KNOWN_METHOD_FOR_SCOPE`. Formal-model mapping residual is `FORMAL_MODEL_MAPPING_RESIDUAL_ACTIVE` until NA-0478 implements the selected model. External-review readiness is `EXTERNAL_REVIEW_READINESS_INCREMENTAL`. Assurance gap trigger classification is `HIGHER_PRIORITY_RESIDUAL_SUPERSEDES_ASSURANCE_REVIEW` because an exact formal model implementation successor is selected.
  - **Public claim boundary:** No public-readiness claim is made. No production-readiness claim is made. No public-internet-readiness claim is made. No external-review-complete claim is made. No crypto-complete claim is made. No KEM-complete claim is made. No signature-complete claim is made. No identity-complete claim is made. No transcript-complete claim is made. No downgrade-proof claim is made. No replay-proof claim is made. No formal-proof-complete claim is made. No RNG-failure-complete claim is made. No provider-RNG-complete claim is made. No secret-material-complete claim is made. No side-channel-free claim is made. No vulnerability-free claim is made. No bug-free claim is made. No perfect-crypto claim is made. Cargo audit green is dependency-health evidence only.
  - **Required behavior:**
    - NA-0477 remains governance authorization evidence only until optional closeout restores the selected NA-0478 successor.
    - Future NA-0478 must keep crypto operations opaque and bounded; it must not model side channels, provider RNG failure, TUI bootstrap transactionality, vectors, fuzz, refimpl equivalence, public readiness, or external-review completion.
    - Exactly one READY remains mandatory.
  - **Must never happen:**
    - NA-0477 evidence is represented as formal model implementation, runtime/source, crypto, dependency, workflow, refimpl, executable-test, vector, fuzz, service, public-doc, website, backup, restore, or qsl-backup authorization beyond this governance-only mapping scope.
    - Future NA-0478 must not be represented as KEM completeness, signature completeness, identity completeness, transcript completeness, downgrade proof, replay proof, formal-proof completeness, crypto completeness, public readiness, production readiness, public-internet readiness, external-review completion, side-channel freedom, vulnerability freedom, bug freedom, perfect crypto, backup completion, or restore proof.
    - Cargo audit output is represented as anything stronger than dependency-health evidence.
    - More than one READY item remains.

  - **Date:** 2026-06-14
  - **Goals:** G1, G2, G3, G4, G5
  - **Decision:** NA-0478 consumes NA-0477 and implements the selected bounded qsc KEM/signature/transcript binding formal model at `formal/model_qsc_kem_signature_transcript_binding_bounded.py`, with runner integration in `formal/run_model_checks.py`.
  - **NA-0477 consumed:** D-0942 selected `FORMAL_MAPPING_QSC_BINDING_MODEL_IMPLEMENTATION_READY`; D-0943 restored NA-0478 as the sole READY implementation successor. NA-0478 consumes that authorization and does not expand it.
  - **Opaque-token abstraction used:** The model treats KEM public keys, KEM ciphertexts, signature identities, signature public keys, signature message contexts, transcript tokens, confirmation tokens, suite tokens, session tokens, public-record/trusted-pin tokens, replay state, pending state, completed-session state, and output flags as opaque tokens. It does not model cryptographic internals.
  - **Valid trace modeled:** The model accepts one valid A1/B1/A2 baseline and records completed-session state for initiator and responder roles.
  - **Wrong KEM reject modeled:** The model rejects an A1 with a wrong KEM public-key token and emits `NA0478_BINDING_MODEL_WRONG_KEM_REJECT_OK`.
  - **Stale KEM/public-record reject modeled:** The model rejects stale KEM public-record and trusted-pin tokens and emits `NA0478_BINDING_MODEL_STALE_KEM_PUBLIC_RECORD_REJECT_OK`.
  - **Wrong ciphertext reject modeled:** The model rejects a wrong B1 KEM ciphertext token and emits `NA0478_BINDING_MODEL_WRONG_CIPHERTEXT_REJECT_OK`.
  - **Wrong signature reject modeled:** The model rejects wrong signature identity/public-key tokens and emits `NA0478_BINDING_MODEL_WRONG_SIGNATURE_REJECT_OK`.
  - **Cross-message signature replay modeled:** The model rejects a signature message-context token copied from another message type and emits `NA0478_BINDING_MODEL_CROSS_MESSAGE_SIGNATURE_REPLAY_REJECT_OK`.
  - **Transcript mutation reject modeled:** The model rejects a mutated transcript token and emits `NA0478_BINDING_MODEL_TRANSCRIPT_MUTATION_REJECT_OK`.
  - **Replay reject modeled:** The model rejects replayed transcript/session state and emits `NA0478_BINDING_MODEL_REPLAY_REJECT_OK`.
  - **Suite confusion reject modeled:** The model rejects a wrong suite token and emits `NA0478_BINDING_MODEL_SUITE_CONFUSION_REJECT_OK`.
  - **Stale public-record reject modeled:** The model rejects stale public-record/trusted-pin tokens and emits `NA0478_BINDING_MODEL_STALE_PUBLIC_RECORD_REJECT_OK`.
  - **No session mutation modeled:** Each selected rejected trace preserves the completed-session set and emits `NA0478_BINDING_MODEL_NO_SESSION_MUTATION_OK`.
  - **No success output on reject modeled:** Each selected rejected trace emits no success output and emits `NA0478_BINDING_MODEL_NO_SUCCESS_OUTPUT_ON_REJECT_OK`.
  - **Bounded formal evidence only:** The model is bounded internal assurance evidence only. It is not cryptographic analysis, not side-channel analysis, not qsc/refimpl equivalence, not vector coverage, not fuzz coverage, and not public/release readiness evidence.
  - **No qsc runtime/source mutation:** No qsc runtime/source file under `qsl/qsl-client/qsc/src/**` is modified.
  - **No qsc executable test mutation:** No qsc executable test is modified.
  - **No dependency/Cargo/lockfile/workflow mutation:** Dependencies, Cargo manifests, lockfiles, and workflows are unchanged.
  - **No refimpl mutation:** refimpl source and tests are unchanged.
  - **No fuzz/vector mutation:** fuzz targets and vectors are unchanged.
  - **No service/public/qshield mutation:** qsl-server, qsl-attachments, qshield runtime, qshield-cli, website, public docs, README, and START_HERE are unchanged.
  - **No backup/restore:** Codex did not run backup or restore and did not mutate qsl-backup, backup status, backup plan, rollback, or backup tree paths.
  - **Public claim boundary:** No public-readiness claim is made. No production-readiness claim is made. No public-internet-readiness claim is made. No external-review-complete claim is made. No crypto-complete claim is made. No KEM-complete claim is made. No signature-complete claim is made. No identity-complete claim is made. No transcript-complete claim is made. No downgrade-proof claim is made. No replay-proof claim is made. No formal-proof-complete claim is made. No side-channel-free claim is made. No vulnerability-free claim is made. No bug-free claim is made. No perfect-crypto claim is made. Cargo audit green is dependency-health evidence only.
  - **Selected successor:** `NA-0479 -- QSL qsc/refimpl KEM / Signature Binding Mapping Authorization Plan` is selected as the default successor after successful merge and green post-merge public-safety. NA-0478 does not implement NA-0479.
  - **Required behavior:**
    - NA-0478 evidence remains bounded internal formal-model evidence.
    - Optional closeout may restore exactly one READY NA-0479 only after the implementation PR is merged and post-merge public-safety is green.
    - Exactly one READY remains mandatory.
  - **Must never happen:**
    - NA-0478 is represented as qsc runtime/source, crypto, dependency, workflow, refimpl, executable-test, vector, fuzz, service, public-doc, website, backup, restore, or qsl-backup authorization beyond the selected formal-model scope.
    - NA-0478 evidence must not be represented as public readiness, production readiness, public-internet readiness, external review completion, crypto completion, KEM completion, signature completion, identity completion, transcript completion, downgrade proof, replay proof, formal-proof completion, side-channel freedom, vulnerability freedom, bug freedom, backup completion, restore proof, or perfect crypto.
    - Cargo audit output is represented as anything stronger than dependency-health evidence.
    - More than one READY item remains.
  - **References:** NA-0478; NA-0477; D-0944; D-0943; D-0942; D-0941; D-0940; `formal/model_qsc_kem_signature_transcript_binding_bounded.py`; `formal/run_model_checks.py`; `docs/governance/evidence/NA-0478_qsl_qsc_kem_signature_transcript_binding_formal_model_implementation_harness.md`; `tests/NA-0478_qsl_qsc_kem_signature_transcript_binding_formal_model_implementation_testplan.md`; `TRACEABILITY.md`; `docs/ops/ROLLING_OPERATIONS_JOURNAL.md`

- **ID:** D-0946
  - **Title:** NA-0479 qsc/refimpl KEM signature binding mapping authorization
  - **Status:** Accepted
  - **Date:** 2026-06-14
  - **Goals:** G1, G2, G3, G4, G5
  - **Decision:** NA-0479 consumes NA-0478 and records the read-only qsc/refimpl KEM and signature binding mapping authorization. qsc KEM/signature assumptions were inventoried, refimpl KEM surfaces were reviewed, refimpl signature surfaces were reviewed, qsc/refimpl error semantics were mapped, formal model/refimpl mapping was reviewed, and the mapping gap matrix was accepted.
  - **NA-0478 consumed:** D-0944 implemented the bounded qsc KEM/signature/transcript binding formal model and D-0945 restored NA-0479 as the sole READY mapping authorization successor. NA-0479 accepts NA-0478 as bounded internal formal evidence only and does not expand it into cryptographic proof, side-channel proof, vector coverage, fuzz coverage, qsc/refimpl equivalence completion, public readiness, or external-review completion.
  - **qsc KEM/signature assumptions inventoried:** qsc assumes fixed-size KEM and signature provider outputs through runtime helper lengths, fail-closed `Result` handling for KEM encap/decap and signing, `Ok(false)` or Err verification collapse to `sig_invalid`, sanitized reasons `pq_encap_failed`, `pq_decap_failed`, `sig_sign_failed`, and `sig_invalid`, and no-mutation/no-output behavior on selected provider failures.
  - **refimpl KEM surfaces reviewed:** refimpl exposes `PqKem768` encap/decap Result semantics plus runtime ML-KEM-768 length/keypair helpers. Current direct KEM evidence covers roundtrip, tamper-changes-secret, and wrong public-key/secret-key/ciphertext length fail-closed behavior. Classification: `REFIMPL_KEM_MAPPING_ACCEPTED_SUPPORTING_ONLY` with provider-boundary test-scope residuals.
  - **refimpl signature surfaces reviewed:** refimpl exposes `PqSigMldsa65` sign and verify Result semantics plus runtime ML-DSA-65 length/keypair helpers. Code maps malformed signing/public/signature material to `CryptoError::InvalidKey` and cryptographically invalid signatures to `Ok(false)`, but direct provider-boundary evidence for wrong signature length, wrong public-key length, wrong public key, and malformed signing-key classification is thinner than KEM evidence. Classification: `REFIMPL_SIGNATURE_MAPPING_TEST_SCOPE_NEXT`.
  - **qsc/refimpl error semantics mapped:** `pq_encap_failed`, `pq_decap_failed`, `sig_sign_failed`, `sig_invalid`, identity keypair failures, and qsc forced cfg seams were mapped to refimpl Result/bool/error behavior. Classification: `ERROR_SEMANTICS_MAPPING_TEST_SCOPE_NEXT`.
  - **Formal model/refimpl mapping reviewed:** NA-0478 opaque KEM/signature/transcript/public-record/replay/suite tokens map to qsc-visible fields and refimpl-generated byte values, but the model does not model provider internals. Classification: `FORMAL_REFIMPL_MAPPING_ACCEPTED_SUPPORTING_ONLY`.
  - **Mapping gap matrix accepted:** No P0 immediate blocker was found. P1 next gaps remain for direct provider-boundary scope around signature wrong-length/wrong-public-key/malformed signing-key semantics, qsc sanitized error mapping, provider RNG boundary caveats, and qsc/refimpl equivalence claim boundary.
  - **Primary classification selected:** `QSC_REFIMPL_MAPPING_ACCEPTED_SUPPORTING_ONLY_WITH_PROVIDER_BOUNDARY_TEST_SCOPE_NEXT`.
  - **Selected NA-0480 successor:** `NA-0480 -- QSL refimpl KEM / Signature Provider Boundary Test Scope Authorization Plan`.
  - **Exact future scope:** Future NA-0480 may create `docs/governance/evidence/NA-0480_qsl_refimpl_kem_signature_provider_boundary_test_scope_authorization_plan.md`, `tests/NA-0480_qsl_refimpl_kem_signature_provider_boundary_test_scope_authorization_testplan.md`, update `DECISIONS.md`, `TRACEABILITY.md`, and `docs/ops/ROLLING_OPERATIONS_JOURNAL.md`, and perform read-only inspection of refimpl crypto source/tests, qsc source/tests, formal models, and evidence docs. It must not implement tests unless a later exact implementation directive authorizes exact paths.
  - **No implementation mutation:** NA-0479 does not implement NA-0480 and does not mutate runtime behavior.
  - **No runtime/crypto/dependency/Cargo/lockfile/workflow/test/fuzz/vector/formal mutation:** NA-0479 does not mutate runtime code, crypto code, dependencies, Cargo manifests, lockfiles, workflows, executable tests, fuzz targets, vectors, formal models, qsc source, refimpl, qsl-server, qsl-attachments, qshield runtime, qshield-cli, website, public docs, README, START_HERE, qwork/qstart/qresume/qshell, qsl-backup, backup status files, backup plan files, rollback paths, backup tree paths, durable Director State Index output, or public technical paper content.
  - **No refimpl mutation:** refimpl source and tests are unchanged.
  - **No backup/restore:** Codex did not run backup or restore and did not mutate qsl-backup, backup status, backup plan, rollback, or backup tree paths.
  - **Public claim boundary:** No public-readiness claim is made. No production-readiness claim is made. No public-internet-readiness claim is made. No external-review-complete claim is made. No public crypto-complete claim is made. No KEM-complete claim is made. No signature-complete claim is made. No qsc/refimpl-equivalence-complete claim is made. No formal-proof-complete claim is made. No provider-RNG-complete claim is made. No side-channel-free claim is made. No vulnerability-free claim is made. No bug-free claim is made. No perfect-crypto claim is made. Cargo audit green is dependency-health evidence only.
  - **Stewardship and assurance reviews used:** Level-1 stewardship, best-known-method, hostile cryptographer, red-team, production SRE, side-channel, formal-model mapping residual, external-review readiness, release-claim boundary, and assurance-gap trigger reviews were used. Level 2 and Level 3 remain future-gated. No separate Directors, independent READY promotion, or independent merge authority is created. Lead Director final authority is preserved.
  - **Required behavior:** NA-0479 evidence remains internal governance evidence only until optional closeout. Future NA-0480 must remain authorization-scoped unless a later exact implementation directive authorizes exact provider-boundary tests. Exactly one READY remains mandatory.
  - **Must never happen:** NA-0479 evidence is represented as implementation, runtime/source, crypto, dependency, workflow, executable-test, refimpl, formal, vector, fuzz, service, public-doc, website, backup, restore, or qsl-backup authorization beyond this governance-only mapping scope. Future NA-0480 must not be represented as public readiness, production readiness, public-internet readiness, external-review completion, crypto completeness, KEM completeness, signature completeness, qsc/refimpl equivalence completion, formal-proof completion, provider RNG completion, side-channel freedom, vulnerability freedom, bug freedom, backup completion, restore proof, or perfect crypto. Cargo audit output must not be represented as anything stronger than dependency-health evidence. More than one READY item remains forbidden.
  - **References:** NA-0479; NA-0478; NA-0480; D-0946; D-0945; D-0944; `docs/governance/evidence/NA-0479_qsl_qsc_refimpl_kem_signature_binding_mapping_authorization_plan.md`; `tests/NA-0479_qsl_qsc_refimpl_kem_signature_binding_mapping_authorization_testplan.md`; `qsl/qsl-client/qsc/src/handshake/mod.rs`; `qsl/qsl-client/qsc/src/identity/mod.rs`; `qsl/qsl-client/qsc/tests/kem_signature_transcript_binding_negative.rs`; `qsl/qsl-client/qsc/tests/handshake_provider_error_no_mutation.rs`; `tools/refimpl/quantumshield_refimpl/src/crypto/traits.rs`; `tools/refimpl/quantumshield_refimpl/src/crypto/stdcrypto.rs`; `tools/refimpl/quantumshield_refimpl/tests/pqkem768.rs`; `formal/model_qsc_kem_signature_transcript_binding_bounded.py`; `formal/run_model_checks.py`; `TRACEABILITY.md`; `docs/ops/ROLLING_OPERATIONS_JOURNAL.md`

- **ID:** D-0948
  - **Title:** NA-0480 refimpl KEM signature provider boundary test scope authorization
  - **Status:** Accepted
  - **Date:** 2026-06-15
  - **Goals:** G1, G2, G3, G4, G5
  - **Decision:** NA-0480 consumes NA-0479 and records the refimpl KEM/signature provider-boundary test scope authorization. Refimpl provider-boundary candidates were inventoried, KEM provider-boundary scope was reviewed, signature provider-boundary scope was reviewed, and the combined versus split decision was recorded.
  - **NA-0479 consumed:** D-0946 accepted qsc/refimpl KEM and signature mapping as supporting-only evidence and selected NA-0480. D-0947 restored NA-0480 as the sole READY successor. NA-0480 consumes that evidence without expanding it into public readiness, external-review completion, cryptographic proof, qsc/refimpl equivalence completion, provider-boundary completion, or provider-RNG completion.
  - **Refimpl provider-boundary candidates inventoried:** KEM wrong public-key length, KEM wrong secret-key length, KEM wrong ciphertext length, KEM tampered ciphertext, KEM wrong keypair, KEM encapsulation error shape, KEM decapsulation error shape, signature wrong public-key length, signature wrong signature length, signature wrong signing-key length, signature malformed signing key, signature tampered signature, signature wrong public key, signature Err versus `Ok(false)` verify classification, signature sign Err classification, provider RNG failure caveat, and qsc sanitized error mapping implications were reviewed.
  - **KEM provider-boundary scope reviewed:** Current refimpl `pqkem768` tests directly cover KEM roundtrip, wrong public-key length, wrong secret-key length, wrong ciphertext length, and tamper-changes-secret behavior. Classification: `REFIMPL_KEM_PROVIDER_BOUNDARY_SUPPORTING_ONLY`.
  - **Signature provider-boundary scope reviewed:** Direct standalone refimpl signature tests are absent for wrong public-key length, wrong signature length, malformed signing key, wrong public key, tampered signature, and Err versus `Ok(false)` classification. These cases can be implemented in refimpl tests only. Classification: `REFIMPL_SIGNATURE_PROVIDER_BOUNDARY_SCOPE_READY`.
  - **Combined versus split decision recorded:** KEM and signature should be split for the next implementation lane. Signature-only provider-boundary implementation is selected because it addresses the highest-value P1 residual without broadening scope.
  - **Provider RNG caveat preserved:** Concrete provider RNG failure remains unforceable through current refimpl helpers without a test seam or source/runtime mutation. No provider-RNG-complete claim is made.
  - **qsc sanitized error mapping implications recorded:** qsc maps provider signature Err and `Ok(false)` to `sig_invalid`, and maps signing Err to `sig_sign_failed`. NA-0481 should keep qsc sanitized error mapping supporting-only and should not mutate qsc source or executable tests.
  - **Primary classification selected:** `REFIMPL_SIGNATURE_PROVIDER_BOUNDARY_TEST_SCOPE_READY`.
  - **Selected NA-0481 successor:** `NA-0481 -- QSL refimpl Signature Provider Boundary Test Implementation Harness`.
  - **Exact future scope:** Future NA-0481 may add `tools/refimpl/quantumshield_refimpl/tests/signature_provider_boundary.rs`, create NA-0481 evidence/testplan files, and update `DECISIONS.md`, `TRACEABILITY.md`, and `docs/ops/ROLLING_OPERATIONS_JOURNAL.md`. Future NA-0481 should assert wrong public-key length, wrong signature length, malformed signing key, tampered signature, wrong public key, and Err versus `Ok(false)` classification markers.
  - **No implementation mutation in this directive:** NA-0480 does not implement NA-0481 and does not mutate runtime behavior.
  - **No runtime/crypto/dependency/Cargo/lockfile/workflow/test/fuzz/vector/formal/refimpl mutation:** NA-0480 does not mutate runtime code, crypto code, dependencies, Cargo manifests, lockfiles, workflows, executable tests, fuzz targets, vectors, formal models, qsc source, refimpl source/tests, qsl-server, qsl-attachments, qshield runtime, qshield-cli, website, public docs, README, START_HERE, qwork/qstart/qresume/qshell, qsl-backup, backup status files, backup plan files, rollback paths, backup tree paths, durable Director State Index output, or public technical paper content.

No bug-free claim is made. No perfect-crypto claim is made. Cargo audit green is dependency-health evidence only.
  - **Required behavior:** NA-0480 evidence remains internal governance evidence only until optional closeout. Future NA-0481 must remain confined to the exact selected signature provider-boundary tests unless a later exact directive authorizes broader scope. Exactly one READY remains mandatory.
  - **Must never happen:**
    - NA-0480 evidence is represented as implementation, runtime/source, crypto, dependency, workflow, executable-test, refimpl source, formal, vector, fuzz, service, public-doc, website, backup, restore, or qsl-backup authorization beyond this governance-only authorization scope.

- **ID:** D-0950
  - **Title:** NA-0481 refimpl signature provider boundary test implementation
  - **Status:** Accepted
  - **Date:** 2026-06-15
  - **Goals:** G1, G2, G3, G4, G5
  - **Decision:** NA-0481 consumes NA-0480 and implements the selected refimpl signature provider-boundary integration tests in `tools/refimpl/quantumshield_refimpl/tests/signature_provider_boundary.rs`, with NA-0481 evidence/testplan, TRACEABILITY, and rolling journal updates. The test-only implementation covers wrong public-key length, wrong signature length, malformed signing-key length, tampered signature, wrong public key, and Err versus `Ok(false)` classification through current public refimpl APIs only.
  - **NA-0480 consumed:** D-0948 selected `REFIMPL_SIGNATURE_PROVIDER_BOUNDARY_TEST_SCOPE_READY`; D-0949 restored NA-0481 as the sole READY item. NA-0481 consumes that scope without expanding into runtime, crypto, dependency, workflow, qsc, KEM, provider RNG, external review, or public readiness claims.
  - **Exact refimpl test file added:** `tools/refimpl/quantumshield_refimpl/tests/signature_provider_boundary.rs`.
  - **Feature boundary:** The new test crate is gated with `#![cfg(feature = "pqcrypto")]` because the exercised ML-DSA provider APIs are exposed only under the refimpl `pqcrypto` feature; no-feature builds compile the target with zero tests while the feature-enabled target runs the NA-0481 marker tests.
  - **Wrong public-key length test implemented:** truncated ML-DSA public-key bytes return `Err(CryptoError::InvalidKey)` and emit `NA0481_REFIMPL_SIGNATURE_WRONG_PUBLIC_KEY_LENGTH_REJECT_OK`.
  - **Wrong signature length test implemented:** truncated ML-DSA signature bytes return `Err(CryptoError::InvalidKey)` and emit `NA0481_REFIMPL_SIGNATURE_WRONG_SIGNATURE_LENGTH_REJECT_OK`.
  - **Malformed signing-key test implemented:** truncated expanded signing-key bytes return `Err(CryptoError::InvalidKey)` and emit `NA0481_REFIMPL_SIGNATURE_MALFORMED_SIGNING_KEY_REJECT_OK`.
  - **Tampered signature invalid test implemented:** length-valid tampered signature bytes return `Ok(false)` and emit `NA0481_REFIMPL_SIGNATURE_TAMPERED_SIGNATURE_INVALID_OK`.
  - **Wrong public key invalid test implemented:** length-valid signature checked against a different generated public key returns `Ok(false)` and emits `NA0481_REFIMPL_SIGNATURE_WRONG_PUBLIC_KEY_INVALID_OK`.
  - **Err versus `Ok(false)` classification evidence implemented:** malformed length inputs return Err while well-shaped invalid verification inputs return `Ok(false)`, and the test emits `NA0481_REFIMPL_SIGNATURE_ERR_VS_FALSE_CLASSIFICATION_OK`.
  - **API caveat:** Verify exposes both Err and `Ok(false)` directly. Sign exposes Err but not a false classification because signing returns bytes or error. Length-valid corrupted expanded signing-key semantics are not generalized beyond the tested malformed-length rejection because doing so would require provider-internal assumptions or source/runtime mutation outside this lane.
  - **Provider RNG caveat preserved:** Concrete provider RNG failure remains unforceable through current refimpl helpers without a test seam or source/runtime mutation. No provider-RNG-complete claim is made.
  - **qsc sanitized error mapping remains supporting-only:** NA-0481 does not mutate qsc source or executable tests. qsc mapping of provider signature Err and `Ok(false)` remains supporting-only and is not represented as qsc/refimpl equivalence.
  - **KEM remains supporting-only for this lane:** NA-0481 adds no KEM tests. Existing refimpl `pqkem768` evidence remains supporting-only, with no KEM-complete claim.
  - **Bounded refimpl provider-boundary evidence only:** The implementation is internal test evidence for selected refimpl signature cases only and does not complete provider-boundary assurance.
  - **No refimpl source mutation:** Only a refimpl integration test file is added; refimpl source is unchanged.
  - **No qsc mutation:** No qsc source or executable test path is changed.
  - **No dependency/Cargo/lockfile/workflow mutation:** No dependency, Cargo manifest, lockfile, workflow, fuzz target, vector, or formal model path is changed.
  - **No backup/restore:** Codex did not run backup or restore and did not mutate qsl-backup, backup status, backup plan, rollback, or backup tree paths.
  - **Public claim boundary:** No public-readiness claim is made. No production-readiness claim is made. No public-internet-readiness claim is made. No external-review-complete claim is made. No crypto-complete claim is made. No KEM-complete claim is made. No signature-complete claim is made. No qsc/refimpl-equivalence-complete claim is made. No provider-boundary-complete claim is made. No provider-RNG-complete claim is made. No formal-proof-complete claim is made. No side-channel-free claim is made. No vulnerability-free claim is made. No bug-free claim is made. No perfect-crypto claim is made. Cargo audit green remains dependency-health evidence only.
  - **Selected successor:** `NA-0482 -- QSL Binding Negative Vector Suite Authorization Plan`, subject to post-merge public-safety and optional closeout. NA-0481 does not implement NA-0482.
  - **Required behavior:**
    - NA-0481 remains a test-only refimpl integration lane until merged.
    - Optional closeout may restore NA-0482 only after post-merge public-safety is green.
    - Exactly one READY remains mandatory.
  - **Must never happen:**
    - NA-0481 evidence is represented as refimpl source, qsc, runtime/source, crypto, dependency, workflow, fuzz, vector, formal model, service, public-doc, website, backup, restore, qsl-backup, or qwork/qstart/qresume/qshell authorization beyond this exact test-only implementation scope.
    - NA-0481 evidence must not be represented as public readiness, production readiness, public-internet readiness, external review completion, crypto completion, KEM completion, signature completion, qsc/refimpl equivalence completion, provider-boundary completion, provider RNG completion, formal-proof completion, side-channel freedom, vulnerability freedom, bug freedom, backup completion, restore proof, or perfect crypto.
    - Cargo audit output is represented as anything stronger than dependency-health evidence.
    - More than one READY item remains.
  - **References:** NA-0481; NA-0480; NA-0482; D-0950; D-0949; D-0948; `tools/refimpl/quantumshield_refimpl/tests/signature_provider_boundary.rs`; `docs/governance/evidence/NA-0481_qsl_refimpl_signature_provider_boundary_test_implementation_harness.md`; `tests/NA-0481_qsl_refimpl_signature_provider_boundary_test_implementation_testplan.md`; `tools/refimpl/quantumshield_refimpl/src/crypto/traits.rs`; `tools/refimpl/quantumshield_refimpl/src/crypto/stdcrypto.rs`; `tools/refimpl/quantumshield_refimpl/tests/pqkem768.rs`; `TRACEABILITY.md`; `docs/ops/ROLLING_OPERATIONS_JOURNAL.md`
