- **ID:** D-0952
  - **Title:** NA-0482 binding negative vector suite authorization
  - **Status:** Accepted
  - **Date:** 2026-06-15
  - **Goals:** G1, G2, G3, G4, G5
  - **Decision:** NA-0482 consumes NA-0481 and authorizes a combined internal binding negative vector suite implementation successor. The selected future strategy is a manifest-plus-test internal vector suite under `inputs/suite2/internal_negative_binding_vectors/`, with separate qsc frame, refimpl provider-boundary metadata, and formal-token sections.
  - **NA-0481 consumed:** D-0950 implemented the bounded refimpl signature provider-boundary test harness, and D-0951 restored NA-0482 as the sole READY item. NA-0482 consumes that evidence together with NA-0476 qsc binding negative tests, NA-0478 formal model evidence, and NA-0479 qsc/refimpl mapping evidence.
  - **Current input/vector surfaces inventoried:** Existing authoritative Suite-2 conformance vectors live under `inputs/suite2/vectors/` and are CI-consumed. Local-ops and metadata fixtures live under other `inputs/**` folders. No current binding negative vector manifest exists for qsc frames, refimpl provider-boundary metadata, and formal-token cases.
  - **Negative vector candidates inventoried:** KEM wrong public key, stale KEM/public record, wrong/corrupted KEM ciphertext, wrong KEM key/ciphertext pairing, signature wrong identity/public record, cross-message signature replay, wrong signature message context, tampered signature, wrong public key, transcript mutation/truncation, replayed A1/B1/A2, wrong-role replay, suite confusion, downgrade-style wrong suite block, stale public-record replay, public-record rollback, identity rotation stale peer state, stale trusted pin mismatch, refimpl signature provider-boundary classifications, and NA-0478 opaque-token trace cases were inventoried and accepted for the future manifest.
  - **Vector format/storage strategy selected:** `MANIFEST_PLUS_TEST_STRATEGY_SELECTED`. Future NA-0483 may create `inputs/suite2/internal_negative_binding_vectors/README.md` and `inputs/suite2/internal_negative_binding_vectors/qsl_binding_negative_vector_manifest_v1.json`. The manifest may store public or mutated message bytes, public keys, signatures, KEM ciphertexts, abstract tokens, metadata, expected reject outcomes, no-mutation expectations, and evidence links.
  - **Secret-material/public-claim risk classified:** `VECTOR_SECRET_MATERIAL_SAFE_SCOPE_READY`. Future vectors must not store private keys, signing keys, KEM secret keys, passphrases, runtime keys, backup keys, operator data, user data, or live service data. Cases requiring secret material must use ephemeral generation and store only public/mutated bytes or metadata.
  - **Combined vs split vector scope decided:** `BINDING_NEGATIVE_VECTOR_COMBINED_IMPLEMENTATION_READY`. KEM, signature, transcript/replay/suite, stale-record, refimpl provider-boundary, and formal-token vector work should be combined in one internal manifest with separate sections. Deterministic negative vectors should precede fuzz binding coverage.
  - **Primary classification selected:** `BINDING_NEGATIVE_VECTOR_COMBINED_IMPLEMENTATION_READY`.
  - **Selected NA-0483 successor:** `NA-0483 -- QSL Binding Negative Vector Suite Implementation Harness`.
  - **Exact future scope:** Future NA-0483 may mutate only `inputs/suite2/internal_negative_binding_vectors/README.md`, `inputs/suite2/internal_negative_binding_vectors/qsl_binding_negative_vector_manifest_v1.json`, `docs/governance/evidence/NA-0483_qsl_binding_negative_vector_suite_implementation_harness.md`, `tests/NA-0483_qsl_binding_negative_vector_suite_implementation_testplan.md`, `DECISIONS.md`, `TRACEABILITY.md`, and `docs/ops/ROLLING_OPERATIONS_JOURNAL.md`, unless a later exact directive changes scope.
  - **No implementation mutation in NA-0482:** This decision does not implement vectors and does not mutate inputs.
  - **No runtime/crypto/dependency/Cargo/lockfile/workflow/test/fuzz/vector/formal/refimpl mutation:** NA-0482 changed only the authorized governance evidence/testplan/decision/traceability/journal paths.
  - **No backup/restore:** Codex did not run backup or restore and did not mutate qsl-backup, backup status, backup plan, rollback, or backup tree paths.
  - **Public claim boundary:** No public crypto-complete claim is made. No vector-complete claim is made. No KEM-complete claim is made. No signature-complete claim is made. No qsc/refimpl-equivalence-complete claim is made. No provider-boundary-complete claim is made. No vulnerability-free claim is made. No perfect-crypto claim is made. No side-channel-free claim is made. No external-review-complete claim is made. No public-readiness, production-readiness, public-internet-readiness, downgrade-proof, replay-proof, provider-RNG-complete, formal-proof-complete, bug-free, backup-complete, restore-proof, off-host-backup-complete, disaster-recovery-complete, metadata-free, anonymity, or untraceable claim is made. Cargo audit green remains dependency-health evidence only.
  - **Exactly one READY remains mandatory:** NA-0482 remains READY until optional closeout after merge and post-merge public-safety. Optional closeout may restore exactly one NA-0483 successor and must not implement NA-0483.
  - **Must never happen:**
    - NA-0482 authorization is represented as vector implementation evidence.
    - Future NA-0483 stores private keys, signing keys, KEM secret keys, passphrases, runtime keys, backup keys, operator data, user data, or live service data in checked-in vectors.
    - Future NA-0483 places internal binding negative vectors directly under the authoritative `inputs/suite2/vectors/` directory unless a later exact directive authorizes and caveats that public/conformance surface.
    - More than one READY item remains.
  - **References:** NA-0482; NA-0481; NA-0479; NA-0478; NA-0476; D-0952; D-0951; D-0950; D-0948; D-0946; D-0944; D-0940; `docs/governance/evidence/NA-0482_qsl_binding_negative_vector_suite_authorization_plan.md`; `tests/NA-0482_qsl_binding_negative_vector_suite_authorization_testplan.md`; `qsl/qsl-client/qsc/tests/kem_signature_transcript_binding_negative.rs`; `formal/model_qsc_kem_signature_transcript_binding_bounded.py`; `tools/refimpl/quantumshield_refimpl/tests/signature_provider_boundary.rs`; `TRACEABILITY.md`; `docs/ops/ROLLING_OPERATIONS_JOURNAL.md`

- **ID:** D-0954
  - **Title:** NA-0483 binding negative vector suite implementation
  - **Status:** Accepted
  - **Date:** 2026-06-15
  - **Goals:** G1, G2, G3, G4, G5
  - **Decision:** NA-0483 consumes NA-0482 and implements the selected internal negative binding vector README and JSON manifest under `inputs/suite2/internal_negative_binding_vectors/`. The manifest contains 34 metadata-only internal vector entries across KEM binding, signature binding, transcript/replay/suite binding, stale identity/rollback, refimpl signature provider-boundary metadata, and NA-0478 formal-token mapping.
  - **NA-0482 consumed:** D-0952 selected `BINDING_NEGATIVE_VECTOR_COMBINED_IMPLEMENTATION_READY`, `VECTOR_SECRET_MATERIAL_SAFE_SCOPE_READY`, and the manifest-plus-test internal vector strategy. D-0953 restored NA-0483 as the sole READY item. NA-0483 implements only that selected internal README/manifest scope plus governance evidence/testplan/decision/traceability/journal updates.
  - **Internal vector README added:** `inputs/suite2/internal_negative_binding_vectors/README.md` defines the internal-only boundary, schema, sections, validation command, future-use caveat, and required NA0483 no-claim markers.
  - **Internal JSON manifest added:** `inputs/suite2/internal_negative_binding_vectors/qsl_binding_negative_vector_manifest_v1.json` uses `schema_version` `"1"`, suite `qsl-internal-negative-binding`, status `internal-negative-evidence-only`, public claim boundary metadata, secret-material policy metadata, traceability links, sections, and vector entries.
  - **KEM vector entries added:** `kem_wrong_peer_public_key`, `kem_stale_public_record`, `kem_wrong_ciphertext`, and `kem_wrong_key_ciphertext_pair`.
  - **Signature vector entries added:** `signature_wrong_identity_public_record`, `signature_cross_message_replay_b1_as_a2`, `signature_wrong_message_context`, `signature_tampered_signature`, and `signature_wrong_public_key`.
  - **Transcript/replay/suite vector entries added:** `transcript_mutation`, `transcript_truncation`, `replayed_a1`, `replayed_b1`, `replayed_a2`, `wrong_role_replay`, `suite_confusion_wrong_suite_token`, and `downgrade_wrong_suite_block`.
  - **Stale identity/rollback vector entries added:** `stale_public_record_replay`, `public_record_rollback`, `identity_rotation_stale_peer_state`, and `stale_trusted_pin_mismatch`.
  - **Refimpl signature provider-boundary vector entries added:** `refimpl_signature_wrong_public_key_length`, `refimpl_signature_wrong_signature_length`, `refimpl_signature_malformed_signing_key`, `refimpl_signature_tampered_signature_invalid`, `refimpl_signature_wrong_public_key_invalid`, and `refimpl_signature_err_vs_false_classification`.
  - **Formal-token mapping vector entries added:** `formal_wrong_kem_token`, `formal_wrong_signature_token`, `formal_transcript_mutation`, `formal_replay`, `formal_suite_confusion`, `formal_stale_public_record`, and `formal_no_session_mutation_on_reject`.
  - **No secret/private material in vectors:** Each vector entry records `contains_secret_material: false`, `contains_private_key: false`, `contains_passphrase: false`, and `contains_user_data: false`. The manifest stores metadata, formal tokens, generation recipes, public-message descriptions, expected reject outcomes, and no-mutation expectations only.
  - **No public/conformance vector path mutation:** `inputs/suite2/vectors/` is unchanged. The new manifest is explicitly labeled internal negative evidence only and is not a public, conformance, or interoperability vector set.
  - **No qsc/refimpl/formal/runtime/dependency/workflow mutation:** NA-0483 does not mutate qsc source or executable tests, refimpl source or executable tests, formal model files, fuzz targets, runtime code, crypto code, dependencies, Cargo manifests, lockfiles, workflows, qsl-server, qsl-attachments, qshield runtime, qshield-cli, website, public docs, README, START_HERE, qwork/qstart/qresume/qshell, qsl-backup, backup status, backup plan, rollback paths, backup tree paths, durable Director State Index output, or public technical paper content.
  - **Bounded internal vector evidence only:** The manifest is metadata-only pending future test consumption. Future tests may consume it only after exact directive authorization.
  - **No backup/restore:** Codex did not run backup or restore and did not mutate qsl-backup, backup status, backup plan, rollback, or backup tree paths.
  - **Public claim boundary:** No public crypto-complete claim is made. No vector-complete claim is made. No KEM-complete claim is made. No signature-complete claim is made. No identity-complete claim is made. No transcript-complete claim is made. No replay-proof claim is made. No downgrade-proof claim is made. No vulnerability-free or perfect-crypto claim is made. No side-channel-free claim is made. No public-readiness, production-readiness, public-internet-readiness, external-review-complete, qsc/refimpl-equivalence-complete, provider-boundary-complete, provider-RNG-complete, formal-proof-complete, bug-free, backup-complete, restore-proof, off-host-backup-complete, disaster-recovery-complete, metadata-free, anonymity, or untraceable claim is made. Cargo audit green remains dependency-health evidence only.
  - **Selected successor:** `NA-0484 -- QSL Fuzz Binding Coverage Scope Authorization Plan`, subject to implementation PR merge, post-merge public-safety success, and optional closeout. NA-0483 does not implement NA-0484.
  - **Required behavior:**
    - NA-0483 remains implementation-scoped to the internal README/manifest and supporting governance paths until merged.
    - Optional closeout may restore exactly one NA-0484 successor only after post-merge public-safety is green.
    - Exactly one READY remains mandatory.
  - **Must never happen:**
    - NA-0483 internal vector metadata is represented as public/conformance/interoperability vectors or public readiness evidence.
    - NA-0483 internal vector metadata is represented as crypto completion, vector completion, KEM completion, signature completion, identity completion, transcript completion, replay proof, downgrade proof, side-channel freedom, vulnerability freedom, bug freedom, or perfect crypto.
    - Private keys, signing keys, KEM secret keys, passphrases, runtime keys, backup keys, operator data, user data, live service data, or private production endpoint data are checked into vector files.

- **ID:** D-0956
  - **Title:** NA-0484 fuzz binding coverage scope authorization
  - **Status:** Accepted
  - **Date:** 2026-06-15
  - **Goals:** G1, G2, G3, G4, G5
  - **Decision:** NA-0484 consumes NA-0483 and records the fuzz binding coverage scope authorization review. Current qsc fuzz surfaces were inventoried, binding fuzz candidates were inventoried, fuzz strategy options were reviewed, Cargo/workflow/CI impact was classified, secret/corpus risk was classified, and a successor was selected without implementing fuzz coverage.
  - **NA-0483 consumed:** D-0954 implemented the internal negative binding vector README/manifest and D-0955 restored NA-0484 as the sole READY authorization successor. NA-0484 accepts that vector metadata as internal supporting evidence only and does not convert it into public vectors, conformance evidence, fuzz evidence, external-review completion, or public readiness.
  - **Current fuzz surface classification:** `FUZZ_BINDING_CURRENTLY_PARSER_ONLY`. The existing qsc fuzz targets are `qsc_route_http`, `qsc_payload_boundaries`, and `qsc_vault_envelope`; they exercise HTTP route parsing, payload parsing, and vault-envelope parsing. They do not directly reach A1/B1/A2 frame binding, KEM public-key binding, KEM ciphertext binding, signature identity/context binding, transcript mutation, replay, suite-confusion, stale public-record, or trusted-pin mismatch semantics.
  - **Binding fuzz candidates inventoried:** A1 frame mutation, B1 frame mutation, A2 frame mutation, suite block mutation, transcript field mutation, wrong KEM public key token, wrong KEM ciphertext token, wrong signature public record/identity token, cross-message signature replay, transcript mutation, replayed messages, suite confusion, stale public-record/trusted pin mismatch, metadata-guided vector seeding, no-corpus first lane, generated ephemeral seeds, and later refimpl provider-boundary fuzz candidates were reviewed.
  - **Fuzz strategy selected:** `FUZZ_BINDING_SPLIT_SCOPE_NEEDED`. Direct qsc semantic binding fuzz is valuable, but current public qsc fuzz APIs expose parser helpers rather than the private A1/B1/A2 handshake binding helpers. qsc-adversarial CI enumerates fuzz targets explicitly. A later exact authorization must decide whether implementation uses a qsc adversarial helper seam, a process/file harness, parser/admission-only fuzz first, qsc fuzz Cargo metadata, qsc-adversarial script inclusion, or another equivalent CI proof path.
  - **Cargo/workflow/CI impact classified:** `FUZZ_IMPLEMENTATION_SPLIT_NEEDED`. A new cargo-fuzz target would require `qsl/qsl-client/qsc/fuzz/Cargo.toml` bin metadata. No root dependency, root lockfile, or workflow mutation is expected if existing dependencies suffice and `.github/workflows/qsc-adversarial.yml` continues to invoke the existing script, but script or equivalent CI inclusion must be exactly authorized before implementation. A future lane must prove whether `qsl/qsl-client/qsc/fuzz/Cargo.lock` remains unchanged.
  - **Secret/corpus risk classified:** `FUZZ_CORPUS_GENERATED_EPHEMERAL_ONLY`. Future fuzz should avoid checked-in corpus in the first binding lane, use NA-0483 metadata only as traceable case descriptions, and generate identities, KEM material, signatures, transcripts, and mutated messages ephemerally. Checked-in private keys, signing keys, KEM secret keys, passphrases, runtime keys, backup keys, operator data, user data, live service data, or private production endpoint data remain forbidden.
  - **Primary classification selected:** `FUZZ_BINDING_SPLIT_SCOPE_NEEDED`.
  - **Selected NA-0485 successor:** `NA-0485 -- QSL Fuzz Binding Coverage Split-Scope Authorization Plan`.
  - **Exact future scope available now:** Future NA-0485 is an authorization-only split-scope lane. It may create `docs/governance/evidence/NA-0485_qsl_fuzz_binding_coverage_split_scope_authorization_plan.md`, create `tests/NA-0485_qsl_fuzz_binding_coverage_split_scope_authorization_testplan.md`, and update `DECISIONS.md`, `TRACEABILITY.md`, and `docs/ops/ROLLING_OPERATIONS_JOURNAL.md`. It may inspect qsc fuzz targets, qsc fuzz Cargo metadata/lock, `scripts/ci/qsc_adversarial.sh`, qsc-adversarial workflow, qsc adversarial and handshake source, NA-0476 tests, NA-0483 internal vectors, formal model, and refimpl provider-boundary tests read-only. It must not implement fuzz coverage unless a later exact directive authorizes implementation.
  - **Candidate implementation paths for later authorization only:** `qsl/qsl-client/qsc/fuzz/fuzz_targets/qsc_binding_frames.rs`, `qsl/qsl-client/qsc/fuzz/fuzz_targets/qsc_binding_semantics.rs`, `qsl/qsl-client/qsc/fuzz/Cargo.toml`, `scripts/ci/qsc_adversarial.sh`, and a tightly bounded qsc adversarial helper path only if a later exact directive authorizes it and proves no runtime behavior change.
  - **No implementation mutation in this directive:** NA-0484 does not implement NA-0485 and does not mutate fuzz targets, corpus, runtime behavior, qsc source/tests, refimpl source/tests, vectors, or formal models.
  - **No runtime/crypto/dependency/Cargo/lockfile/workflow/test/fuzz/vector/formal/refimpl mutation:** NA-0484 changes only the authorized governance evidence/testplan/decision/traceability/journal paths.
  - **No backup/restore:** Codex did not run backup or restore and did not mutate qsl-backup, backup status, backup plan, rollback, or backup tree paths.
  - **Public claim boundary:** No public crypto-complete claim is made. No fuzz-complete claim is made. No vector-complete claim is made. No KEM-complete claim is made. No signature-complete claim is made. No identity-complete claim is made. No transcript-complete claim is made. No qsc/refimpl-equivalence-complete claim is made. No provider-boundary-complete claim is made. No provider-RNG-complete claim is made. No formal-proof-complete claim is made. No replay-proof claim is made. No downgrade-proof claim is made. No side-channel-free claim is made. No vulnerability-free claim is made. No bug-free claim is made. No perfect-crypto claim is made. No external-review-complete claim is made. No public-readiness, production-readiness, public-internet-readiness, backup-complete, restore-proof, off-host-backup-complete, disaster-recovery-complete, metadata-free, anonymity, or untraceable claim is made. Cargo audit green remains dependency-health evidence only.
  - **Stewardship and assurance reviews used:** Level-1 stewardship, best-known-method, hostile cryptographer, red-team, production SRE, side-channel, formal-model mapping residual, external-review readiness, release-claim boundary, and assurance-gap trigger reviews were used. Classification: `BEST_KNOWN_METHOD_FOR_SCOPE`, `FORMAL_MODEL_MAPPING_SUPPORTING_ONLY`, `EXTERNAL_REVIEW_READINESS_INCREMENTAL`, and `HIGHER_PRIORITY_RESIDUAL_SUPERSEDES_ASSURANCE_REVIEW`. Level 2 and Level 3 remain future-gated. No separate Directors, independent READY promotion, or independent merge authority is created. Lead Director final authority is preserved.
  - **Required behavior:** NA-0484 evidence remains internal governance evidence only until optional closeout. Future NA-0485 must remain authorization-scoped unless a later exact implementation directive authorizes exact fuzz target/Cargo/script/helper/corpus scope. Exactly one READY remains mandatory.
  - **Must never happen:** NA-0484 is represented as fuzz implementation evidence. Future NA-0485 is represented as authorization to mutate runtime/source, crypto, dependencies, Cargo, lockfiles, workflows, scripts, executable tests, fuzz targets, corpora, vectors, formal models, refimpl, services, public docs, website, backups, qsl-backup, status/plan, or qwork/qstart/qresume/qshell outside exact successor scope. NA-0484 or future NA-0485 evidence is represented as public readiness, production readiness, public-internet readiness, external-review completion, crypto completion, fuzz completion, vector completion, KEM completion, signature completion, identity completion, transcript completion, qsc/refimpl equivalence completion, provider-boundary completion, provider RNG completion, formal-proof completion, replay proof, downgrade proof, side-channel freedom, vulnerability freedom, bug freedom, backup completion, restore proof, off-host backup completion, disaster recovery completion, metadata-free behavior, anonymity, untraceability, or perfect crypto. More than one READY item remains.
  - **References:** NA-0484; NA-0483; NA-0485; D-0956; D-0955; D-0954; `docs/governance/evidence/NA-0484_qsl_fuzz_binding_coverage_scope_authorization_plan.md`; `tests/NA-0484_qsl_fuzz_binding_coverage_scope_authorization_testplan.md`; `inputs/suite2/internal_negative_binding_vectors/README.md`; `inputs/suite2/internal_negative_binding_vectors/qsl_binding_negative_vector_manifest_v1.json`; `qsl/qsl-client/qsc/fuzz/Cargo.toml`; `qsl/qsl-client/qsc/fuzz/fuzz_targets/qsc_route_http.rs`; `qsl/qsl-client/qsc/fuzz/fuzz_targets/qsc_payload_boundaries.rs`; `qsl/qsl-client/qsc/fuzz/fuzz_targets/qsc_vault_envelope.rs`; `scripts/ci/qsc_adversarial.sh`; `.github/workflows/qsc-adversarial.yml`; `qsl/qsl-client/qsc/src/handshake/mod.rs`; `qsl/qsl-client/qsc/tests/kem_signature_transcript_binding_negative.rs`; `formal/model_qsc_kem_signature_transcript_binding_bounded.py`; `TRACEABILITY.md`; `docs/ops/ROLLING_OPERATIONS_JOURNAL.md`

- **ID:** D-0958
  - **Title:** NA-0485 fuzz binding coverage split scope authorization
  - **Status:** Accepted
  - **Date:** 2026-06-15
  - **Goals:** G1, G2, G3, G4, G5
  - **Decision:** NA-0485 consumes NA-0484 and records the qsc fuzz binding split-scope authorization review. Current qsc fuzz targets, qsc fuzz Cargo metadata, qsc-adversarial script integration, qsc-adversarial workflow wiring, internal negative vector metadata, qsc handshake helper visibility, inherited formal checks, qsc binding negative tests, and refimpl signature provider-boundary tests were inventoried without implementing fuzz coverage.
  - **NA-0484 consumed:** D-0956 classified current qsc fuzz as parser-only for binding purposes and selected NA-0485 to decide exact helper/API/process-harness, Cargo/script/CI, corpus/no-corpus, and secret-material boundaries. NA-0485 accepts that inheritance and does not convert NA-0484 evidence into fuzz implementation evidence.
  - **Current qsc fuzz target/Cargo/script/CI inventory:** Existing targets remain `qsc_route_http`, `qsc_payload_boundaries`, and `qsc_vault_envelope`. Each target imports public `qsc::adversarial::*` parser helpers and remains parser-boundary oriented for binding purposes. `qsl/qsl-client/qsc/fuzz/Cargo.toml` enumerates all targets as `[[bin]]`; adding a new target requires Cargo metadata. `scripts/ci/qsc_adversarial.sh` explicitly invokes each target and expects matching corpus directories; adding a CI-visible target requires script scope. `.github/workflows/qsc-adversarial.yml` invokes the script and does not enumerate target names; workflow mutation is not expected.
  - **Helper/API access classified:** `FUZZ_HELPER_ACCESS_REQUIRES_RUNTIME_SOURCE_DESIGN`. Current public qsc exports include `qsc::adversarial` parser helpers and `qsc::envelope`, while A1/B1/A2 encode/decode, transcript MAC/hash, signature-message construction, suite-context admission, KEM decapsulation handling, trusted-pin checks, and session no-mutation behavior remain internal to `handshake/mod.rs`. A target-only semantic binding fuzz implementation is therefore not ready.
  - **Cargo/script/CI exact scope classified:** `FUZZ_EXACT_SCOPE_SPLIT_REQUIRED`. If a later helper/API design authorizes implementation, the expected implementation shape is target plus qsc fuzz Cargo metadata plus qsc-adversarial script inclusion, with no workflow mutation expected, no dependency mutation expected, and no lockfile mutation expected if existing dependencies suffice.
  - **Corpus/secret strategy classified:** `FUZZ_CORPUS_EPHEMERAL_GENERATION_READY` and `FUZZ_CORPUS_METADATA_ONLY_READY`. Future semantic binding fuzz should avoid checked-in corpus in the first implementation lane, use NA-0483 internal manifest metadata only as seed-class guidance, and generate identities, KEM material, signatures, transcripts, and mutated messages ephemerally. Checked-in private keys, signing keys, KEM secret keys, passphrases, runtime keys, backup keys, operator data, user data, live service data, and private production endpoint data remain forbidden.
  - **Implementation readiness classification selected:** `FUZZ_BINDING_HELPER_API_DESIGN_NEEDED`. NA-0485 does not authorize fuzz implementation because current fuzz target files alone cannot safely reach qsc semantic binding checks.
  - **Selected NA-0486 successor:** `NA-0486 -- QSL qsc Binding Fuzz Helper / API Design Authorization Plan`.
  - **Exact future NA-0486 scope:** Future NA-0486 may create `docs/governance/evidence/NA-0486_qsl_qsc_binding_fuzz_helper_api_design_authorization_plan.md`, create `tests/NA-0486_qsl_qsc_binding_fuzz_helper_api_design_authorization_testplan.md`, and update `DECISIONS.md`, `TRACEABILITY.md`, and `docs/ops/ROLLING_OPERATIONS_JOURNAL.md`. - **ID:** D-0960
  - **Title:** NA-0486 qsc binding fuzz helper API design authorization
  - **Status:** Accepted
  - **Date:** 2026-06-15
  - **Goals:** G1, G2, G3, G4, G5
  - **Decision:** NA-0486 consumes NA-0485 and records the qsc binding fuzz helper/API design authorization. Helper/API candidates were inventoried, process-vs-in-process fuzz options were reviewed, Cargo/script/CI exact scope was decided, helper design safety was classified, corpus/seed strategy was classified, and a future exact implementation successor was selected without implementing helper code or fuzz coverage in NA-0486.
  - **NA-0485 consumed:** D-0958 classified current qsc fuzz targets as parser-boundary oriented for binding purposes, classified helper/API access as `FUZZ_HELPER_ACCESS_REQUIRES_RUNTIME_SOURCE_DESIGN`, and selected NA-0486 to decide whether a narrow helper/API or process harness can safely support semantic qsc binding fuzz. NA-0486 accepts that inheritance and does not represent NA-0485 evidence as fuzz implementation evidence.
  - **Helper/API candidates inventoried:** Existing `qsc::adversarial` helpers are public parser helpers for route, payload, and vault-envelope surfaces. A1/B1/A2 encode/decode, transcript MAC/hash, signature message construction, suite-context admission, KEM decapsulation handling, trusted-pin checks, and session no-mutation behavior remain internal to `handshake/mod.rs` and identity internals. qsc integration tests provide useful CLI/temp-root/mock-relay patterns but are not reusable from fuzz targets without source/test mutation. Formal/vector metadata remains traceability-only seed-class guidance.
  - **Process-vs-in-process decision recorded:** In-process helper/API fuzz is selected for the first implementation because it can run in the existing qsc fuzz/cargo-fuzz shape and can reach real semantic qsc reject paths if narrowly cfg-gated. Process-harness fuzz is rejected as the first implementation because it is slower, filesystem/network-heavy, and less suitable for libFuzzer. A target-local simplified oracle is rejected as primary evidence because it would not exercise real qsc binding code. Extending parser fuzz only is rejected because it repeats the parser-only limitation.
  - **Cargo/script/CI exact scope decided:** Selected future scope is helper plus fuzz target plus qsc fuzz Cargo metadata plus qsc-adversarial script inclusion. Future NA-0487 must add a new qsc fuzz `[[bin]]` entry and include the target in `scripts/ci/qsc_adversarial.sh`. Workflow mutation is not selected because `.github/workflows/qsc-adversarial.yml` invokes the script generically. Dependency mutation and qsc fuzz lockfile mutation are not selected if existing dependencies suffice.
  - **Helper design safety classified:** `HELPER_DESIGN_SAFE_TEST_FUZZ_ONLY`. The selected helper must live at `qsl/qsl-client/qsc/src/adversarial/binding_fuzz.rs` and be exported from `qsl/qsl-client/qsc/src/adversarial/mod.rs` only under an explicit custom fuzz/test cfg such as `qsc_binding_fuzz_helper`, following existing qsc custom-cfg seam precedent. Normal no-cfg builds must not expose the helper or change qsc public API/runtime behavior. The helper must call real qsc reject paths, avoid validation bypass, emit no secret material, use no unsafe code, and require no dependency changes.
  - **Corpus/seed strategy classified:** `FUZZ_SEED_NO_CORPUS_FIRST`, `FUZZ_SEED_EPHEMERAL_GENERATION_ONLY`, `FUZZ_SEED_MANIFEST_TRACEABILITY_ONLY`, and `FUZZ_SEED_CORPUS_SEPARATE_LANE`. Future fuzz should generate identities, KEM material, signatures, transcripts, and mutations ephemerally from fuzzer input. The NA-0483 manifest may guide seed classes as metadata/comments only and should not be consumed at runtime. Checked-in corpus is deferred to a later lane.
  - **Implementation readiness classification selected:** `QSC_BINDING_FUZZ_HELPER_PLUS_TARGET_PLUS_CARGO_PLUS_SCRIPT_IMPLEMENTATION_READY`.
  - **Selected NA-0487 successor:** `NA-0487 -- QSL qsc Binding Fuzz Helper and Target Implementation Harness`.
  - **Exact future scope if authorized by NA-0487:** `qsl/qsl-client/qsc/src/adversarial/binding_fuzz.rs`; `qsl/qsl-client/qsc/src/adversarial/mod.rs`; `qsl/qsl-client/qsc/fuzz/fuzz_targets/qsc_binding_semantics.rs`; `qsl/qsl-client/qsc/fuzz/Cargo.toml`; `scripts/ci/qsc_adversarial.sh`; `docs/governance/evidence/NA-0487_qsl_qsc_binding_fuzz_helper_and_target_implementation_harness.md`; `tests/NA-0487_qsl_qsc_binding_fuzz_helper_and_target_implementation_testplan.md`; `DECISIONS.md`; `TRACEABILITY.md`; `docs/ops/ROLLING_OPERATIONS_JOURNAL.md`.
  - **No implementation mutation in this directive:** NA-0486 does not implement helper/API code, fuzz targets, Cargo metadata, script changes, workflows, corpus, vectors, formal models, refimpl tests, or runtime behavior.
  - **No runtime/crypto/dependency/Cargo/lockfile/workflow/test/fuzz/vector/formal/refimpl mutation:** NA-0486 changes only the authorized governance evidence/testplan/decision/traceability/journal paths.
  - **No backup/restore:** Codex did not run backup or restore and did not mutate qsl-backup, backup status, backup plan, rollback, or backup tree paths.
  - **Public claim boundary:** no public crypto-complete claim is made. no fuzz-complete claim is made. no vector-complete claim is made. no KEM-complete claim is made. no signature-complete claim is made. no replay-proof claim is made. no downgrade-proof claim is made. no vulnerability-free claim is made. no perfect-crypto claim is made. no side-channel-free claim is made. no external-review-complete claim is made. no public-readiness claim is made. no production-readiness claim is made. no public-internet-readiness claim is made. no identity-complete claim is made. no transcript-complete claim is made. no qsc/refimpl-equivalence-complete claim is made. no provider-boundary-complete claim is made. no provider-RNG-complete claim is made. no formal-proof-complete claim is made. Cargo audit green remains dependency-health evidence only.
  - **Stewardship and assurance reviews used:** Level-1 stewardship, best-known-method, hostile cryptographer, red-team, production SRE, side-channel caveat, formal-model mapping residual, external-review readiness, release-claim boundary, and assurance-gap trigger reviews were used. Classifications: `BEST_KNOWN_METHOD_FOR_SCOPE`, `FORMAL_MODEL_MAPPING_SUPPORTING_ONLY`, `EXTERNAL_REVIEW_READINESS_INCREMENTAL`, and `HIGHER_PRIORITY_RESIDUAL_SUPERSEDES_ASSURANCE_REVIEW`. Level 2 and Level 3 remain future-gated. No separate Directors, independent READY promotion, or independent merge authority is created. Lead Director final authority is preserved.
  - **Required behavior:** NA-0486 evidence remains internal governance evidence only until optional closeout. Future NA-0487 must implement only the exact helper plus target plus Cargo plus script scope selected here, prove no-cfg production behavior is unchanged, prove no secret output, call real qsc reject paths, and stop if broader source, dependency, lockfile, workflow, corpus, vector, formal, refimpl, service, public-doc, backup, or public-claim scope is required. Exactly one READY remains mandatory.
  - **Must never happen:** NA-0486 is represented as helper/fuzz implementation evidence. Future NA-0487 is represented as authorization to mutate qsc source outside the exact adversarial helper paths, fuzz files outside the exact target and Cargo metadata, qsc-adversarial script behavior outside adding/running the selected target, dependency files, lockfiles, workflows, checked-in corpus, vectors/inputs, formal models, refimpl source/tests, services, public docs, website, qshield, qsl-server, qsl-attachments, qwork/qstart/qresume/qshell, backups, qsl-backup, status/plan, rollback, durable Director State Index output, or public technical paper content outside exact successor scope. NA-0486 or future NA-0487 evidence is represented as public readiness, production readiness, public-internet readiness, external-review completion, crypto completion, fuzz completion, vector completion, KEM completion, signature completion, identity completion, transcript completion, qsc/refimpl equivalence completion, provider-boundary completion, provider RNG completion, formal-proof completion, replay proof, downgrade proof, side-channel freedom, vulnerability freedom, bug freedom, backup completion, restore proof, off-host backup completion, disaster recovery completion, metadata-free behavior, anonymity, untraceability, or perfect crypto. More than one READY item remains.
  - **References:** NA-0486; NA-0485; NA-0484; NA-0483; NA-0487; D-0960; D-0959; D-0958; `docs/governance/evidence/NA-0486_qsl_qsc_binding_fuzz_helper_api_design_authorization_plan.md`; `tests/NA-0486_qsl_qsc_binding_fuzz_helper_api_design_authorization_testplan.md`; `docs/governance/evidence/NA-0485_qsl_fuzz_binding_coverage_split_scope_authorization_plan.md`; `qsl/qsl-client/qsc/src/adversarial/mod.rs`; `qsl/qsl-client/qsc/src/handshake/mod.rs`; `qsl/qsl-client/qsc/src/identity/mod.rs`; `qsl/qsl-client/qsc/fuzz/Cargo.toml`; `qsl/qsl-client/qsc/fuzz/fuzz_targets/qsc_route_http.rs`; `qsl/qsl-client/qsc/fuzz/fuzz_targets/qsc_payload_boundaries.rs`; `qsl/qsl-client/qsc/fuzz/fuzz_targets/qsc_vault_envelope.rs`; `scripts/ci/qsc_adversarial.sh`; `.github/workflows/qsc-adversarial.yml`; `inputs/suite2/internal_negative_binding_vectors/qsl_binding_negative_vector_manifest_v1.json`; `qsl/qsl-client/qsc/tests/kem_signature_transcript_binding_negative.rs`; `formal/model_qsc_kem_signature_transcript_binding_bounded.py`; `TRACEABILITY.md`; `docs/ops/ROLLING_OPERATIONS_JOURNAL.md`

- **ID:** D-0962
  - **Title:** NA-0487 qsc binding fuzz source-boundary recovery authorization
  - **Status:** Accepted
  - **Date:** 2026-06-16
  - **Goals:** G1, G2, G3, G4, G5
  - **Decision:** NA-0487 consumes the D347 stop response and accepts the qsc source-boundary problem: the current qsc library exports only `adversarial` and `envelope`, while real A1/B1/A2, suite-confusion, replay, transcript, signature, KEM, identity, and stale-public-record reject paths live behind qsc binary/internal module boundaries. The current qsc library boundary was inventoried, recovery options were reviewed, and the selected recovery classification is `SOURCE_BOUNDARY_RECOVERY_MINIMAL_READY`.
  - **D347 stop consumed:** D347 stopped before mutation because the then-selected helper-only path could not reach real qsc semantic handshake reject paths inside the allowed scope. Implementing anyway would have required a helper-local fake oracle, unauthorized `lib.rs` / `handshake/mod.rs` / `identity/mod.rs` source-boundary mutation, dependency/Cargo changes, or a process harness that D-0960 rejected as the first implementation shape.
  - **Recovery option selected:** Option 1 is selected for the next NA-0487 implementation: a minimal qsc library/source-boundary expansion behind exact cfg `qsc_binding_fuzz_helper`, plus the previously selected helper, fuzz target, qsc fuzz Cargo metadata, and qsc-adversarial script inclusion. Option 2 process harness is rejected for the next implementation but retained as fallback if the minimal boundary proves too broad. Option 3 parser/metadata-only fuzz is rejected as a semantic-evidence downgrade. Option 4 vector-consumer tests are deferred. Option 5 aborting the fuzz chain is rejected.
  - **Revised NA-0487 implementation scope:** Future NA-0487 may mutate `qsl/qsl-client/qsc/src/lib.rs`, `qsl/qsl-client/qsc/src/adversarial/mod.rs`, `qsl/qsl-client/qsc/src/adversarial/binding_fuzz.rs`, `qsl/qsl-client/qsc/src/handshake/mod.rs`, `qsl/qsl-client/qsc/src/identity/mod.rs` only if required for stale-public-record/trusted-pin reachability, `qsl/qsl-client/qsc/fuzz/fuzz_targets/qsc_binding_semantics.rs`, `qsl/qsl-client/qsc/fuzz/Cargo.toml`, `scripts/ci/qsc_adversarial.sh`, the NA-0487 implementation evidence/testplan paths, `DECISIONS.md`, `TRACEABILITY.md`, and `docs/ops/ROLLING_OPERATIONS_JOURNAL.md`.
  - **Safety criteria:** Future helper code must compile/export only under exact cfg `qsc_binding_fuzz_helper`; normal no-cfg builds must not compile or export the helper; no production behavior drift or normal public API expansion may occur; no secret/private material output, unsafe code, dependency change, lockfile change, workflow change, or checked-in corpus is allowed; the helper must route through real qsc parsing/reject/binding paths and must not duplicate protocol semantics as a fake oracle; helper outcomes must be bounded classifications, markers, or enum-style values; A1, B1, A2, suite-confusion, replay, and stale-public-record categories must be supported; the fuzz target must not read the manifest JSON at runtime.
  - **NA-0487 remains READY:** This recovery does not mark NA-0487 DONE and does not restore NA-0488. Exactly one READY remains mandatory.
  - **No implementation mutation in this directive:** This directive does not implement the helper, fuzz target, Cargo target, script wiring, corpus, tests, runtime behavior, crypto behavior, dependencies, workflows, vectors, formal models, refimpl behavior, qsc source changes, service changes, public docs, backup, restore, or qsl-backup.
  - **No qsc source/fuzz/Cargo/script/workflow mutation in this directive:** qsc source files, qsc fuzz targets, qsc fuzz Cargo files, qsc-adversarial scripts, workflows, dependencies, and lockfiles are unchanged by this recovery directive.
  - **No backup/restore:** Codex did not run backup or restore and did not mutate qsl-backup, backup status, backup plan, rollback, or backup tree paths.
  - **Public claim boundary:** no public-readiness claim is made. no production-readiness claim is made. no external-review-complete claim is made. no crypto-complete claim is made. no fuzz-complete claim is made. no vector-complete claim is made. no KEM-complete claim is made. no signature-complete claim is made. no qsc-refimpl-equivalence-complete claim is made. no replay-proof claim is made. no downgrade-proof claim is made. no side-channel-free claim is made. no vulnerability-free claim is made. no bug-free claim is made. no perfect-crypto claim is made. Cargo audit green remains dependency-health evidence only.
  - **Required behavior:** Future NA-0487 must prove no-cfg production behavior is unchanged, cfg-on helper reachability is limited to `qsc_binding_fuzz_helper`, helper outputs contain no secret/private material, helper calls real qsc reject paths for the selected categories, qsc fuzz Cargo/script changes are limited to the selected target, and no dependency/lockfile/workflow/vector/corpus/formal/refimpl/service/public/backup mutation occurs. If implementation requires additional qsc source-boundary mutation outside the revised scope, it must STOP or split rather than widening scope silently.
  - **Must never happen:** D347 is ignored; a helper-local fake oracle is represented as semantic qsc binding evidence; NA-0487 is marked DONE by this recovery; NA-0488 is restored; more than one READY item remains; this recovery is represented as implementation evidence; future NA-0487 is represented as authorization for dependencies, lockfiles, workflows, vectors, corpora, formal models, refimpl, services, public docs, qsl-backup, backup/restore, or public-claim expansion.

- **ID:** D-0963
  - **Title:** NA-0487 qsc binding fuzz helper and target implementation
  - **Status:** Accepted
  - **Date:** 2026-06-16
  - **Goals:** G1, G2, G3, G4, G5
  - **Decision:** NA-0487 consumes the D347/D348 recovery chain and implements the minimal cfg-gated qsc source-boundary selected by D-0962. The implementation adds the helper/API file `qsl/qsl-client/qsc/src/adversarial/binding_fuzz.rs`, gates the adversarial module export under exact cfg `qsc_binding_fuzz_helper`, updates the qsc library/source boundary only as authorized, limits handshake/identity source changes to helper reachability for real qsc reject-path parsing, replay, and trusted-pin categories, adds the semantic fuzz target `qsc_binding_semantics`, adds one qsc fuzz Cargo bin target, and includes the target in the qsc-adversarial script.
  - **Recovery consumed:** D347 stopped before mutation because helper-only scope would not reach real qsc semantic reject paths. D348 selected `SOURCE_BOUNDARY_RECOVERY_MINIMAL_READY`. D-0962 authorized the exact future implementation paths consumed by this decision.
  - **cfg-gated helper boundary:** The helper is compiled and exported only under exact cfg `qsc_binding_fuzz_helper`. Normal no-cfg builds do not expose the helper and do not need it. No production public API expansion occurs in normal builds.
  - **Real reject-path routing:** cfg-on qsc handshake code delegates suite-context parsing, frame-header parsing, A1 replay candidate matching, and stale-public-record/trusted-pin comparison to helper-facing routines used by the fuzz target. This avoids representing a target-local fake oracle as qsc semantic binding evidence.
  - **Fuzz target scope:** The target covers A1 mutation, B1 mutation, A2 mutation, suite confusion, replay, stale public-record, and vector traceability categories by deterministic input-byte category selection. The target does not read the vector manifest JSON at runtime and does not perform filesystem-heavy process harness behavior.
  - **Secret and corpus boundary:** The helper emits only bounded classifications and static reason markers. It emits no secret/private material. No checked-in corpus or seed file is added.
  - **Cargo/script boundary:** qsc fuzz Cargo metadata adds exactly one `[[bin]]` entry for `qsc_binding_semantics` and no dependency entry. `scripts/ci/qsc_adversarial.sh` includes the target with `RUSTFLAGS='--cfg qsc_binding_fuzz_helper'`, preserves existing fuzz targets, and preserves the provider-error no-mutation step.
  - **No forbidden mutation:** No dependency mutation, root `Cargo.lock` mutation, qsc fuzz `Cargo.lock` mutation, workflow mutation, vector/input mutation, formal mutation, refimpl mutation, qsl-server mutation, qsl-attachments mutation, qshield runtime mutation, qshield-cli mutation, service mutation, public-doc mutation, backup mutation, restore mutation, qsl-backup mutation, backup status mutation, backup plan mutation, rollback mutation, qwork mutation, qstart mutation, qresume mutation, or qshell mutation is introduced.
  - **Validation boundary:** Local validation covers no-cfg qsc binding negative tests, cfg-on qsc binding negative tests, cfg-on qsc cargo check, cfg-on qsc fuzz target cargo check, qsc-adversarial shell syntax, vector manifest JSON validation, formal checks, inherited qsc/refimpl tests, root cargo audit, nested qsc fuzz lock audit, dependency probes, scope guard, link check, leak scan, overclaim scan, PR body preflight, goal-lint, and `cargo fmt --check`. Local cargo-fuzz is unavailable and PR CI qsc-adversarial-smoke is required for cargo-fuzz-backed smoke evidence.
  - **Successor selected:** After NA-0487 merges and post-merge public-safety and qsc-adversarial-smoke are green, the selected successor is `NA-0488 -- QSL Binding Fuzz Corpus / Seed Strategy Authorization Plan`. NA-0488 must not be implemented by this decision.
  - **Public claim boundary:** no public-readiness claim is made. no production-readiness claim is made. no external-review-complete claim is made. no crypto-complete claim is made. no fuzz-complete claim is made. no vector-complete claim is made. no KEM-complete claim is made. no signature-complete claim is made. no identity-complete claim is made. no transcript-complete claim is made. no qsc/refimpl-equivalence-complete claim is made. no provider-boundary-complete claim is made. no provider-RNG-complete claim is made. no replay-proof claim is made. no downgrade-proof claim is made. no side-channel-free claim is made. no vulnerability-free claim is made. no bug-free claim is made. no perfect-crypto claim is made. Cargo audit green remains dependency-health evidence only.
  - **References:** NA-0487; NA-0488; D-0963; D-0962; D-0961; D-0960; D347 response `/home/victor/work/qsl/codex/responses/NA0487_20260616T020314Z_D347.md`; D348 response `/home/victor/work/qsl/codex/responses/NA0487_source_boundary_recovery_20260616T022143Z_D348.md`; `docs/governance/evidence/NA-0487_qsl_qsc_binding_fuzz_helper_and_target_implementation_harness.md`; `tests/NA-0487_qsl_qsc_binding_fuzz_helper_and_target_implementation_testplan.md`; `qsl/qsl-client/qsc/src/lib.rs`; `qsl/qsl-client/qsc/src/adversarial/mod.rs`; `qsl/qsl-client/qsc/src/adversarial/binding_fuzz.rs`; `qsl/qsl-client/qsc/src/handshake/mod.rs`; `qsl/qsl-client/qsc/src/identity/mod.rs`; `qsl/qsl-client/qsc/fuzz/fuzz_targets/qsc_binding_semantics.rs`; `qsl/qsl-client/qsc/fuzz/Cargo.toml`; `scripts/ci/qsc_adversarial.sh`; `TRACEABILITY.md`; `docs/ops/ROLLING_OPERATIONS_JOURNAL.md`

- **ID:** D-0965
  - **Title:** NA-0488 binding fuzz corpus seed strategy authorization
  - **Status:** Accepted
  - **Date:** 2026-06-16
  - **Goals:** G1, G2, G3, G4, G5
  - **Decision:** NA-0488 consumes NA-0487 and D350, inventories the current qsc fuzz corpus and seed surface, reviews corpus/seed options, selects internal vector manifest traceability-only usage with static category mapping allowed, completes secret/private-material review, completes CI/Cargo/workflow impact review, and selects `BINDING_FUZZ_EPHEMERAL_SEED_STRATEGY_READY` as the primary classification. No checked-in corpus is authorized now. The selected successor is `NA-0489 -- QSL Binding Fuzz Ephemeral Seed Strategy Implementation Harness`.
  - **NA-0487/D350 consumed:** PR #1245 merged at `ea1af60e84f0` with the cfg-gated qsc binding fuzz helper and `qsc_binding_semantics` target. PR #1246 merged at `d78d163535ff` and restored NA-0488 as the sole READY item. D350 post-merge evidence shows public-safety and qsc-adversarial-smoke success and recorded fully productive wait accounting.
  - **Current corpus/seed surface inventoried:** existing checked-in qsc fuzz corpus directories are limited to `qsc_route_http`, `qsc_payload_boundaries`, and `qsc_vault_envelope`; `qsc_binding_semantics` is present and invoked by qsc-adversarial but has no checked-in corpus directory. Classifications selected: `FUZZ_CORPUS_EXISTING_PARSER_TARGETS_ONLY` and `FUZZ_CORPUS_BINDING_TARGET_NONE`.
  - **Corpus/seed options reviewed:** no checked-in corpus is selected for now; generated deterministic ephemeral seed recipes are selected for the next lane; metadata-only/static category guidance is selected; checked-in public/mutated corpus, category-split corpus, immediate vector-consumer tests, fuzz stabilization, and side-channel-first follow-up are not selected as NA-0489.
  - **Internal vector manifest usage selected:** the NA-0483 manifest remains traceability-only. Future seed code must not read the JSON manifest at runtime. Future seed code may hardcode stable category names with traceability comments. Classifications selected: `VECTOR_MANIFEST_TRACEABILITY_ONLY_SELECTED` and `VECTOR_MANIFEST_STATIC_CATEGORY_MAPPING_READY`.
  - **Secret/private material review completed:** checked-in binding corpus is not authorized without a future validator. Future NA-0489 seed material must be public constants, mutated public bytes, short synthetic byte arrays, or generated ephemerally. Classification selected: `CORPUS_SECRET_MATERIAL_EPHEMERAL_ONLY`; future checked-in corpus requires `CORPUS_SECRET_MATERIAL_VALIDATOR_REQUIRED_FIRST`.
  - **CI/Cargo/workflow impact classified:** selected no-corpus ephemeral seed work requires only target-level fuzz harness mutation and no qsc fuzz Cargo, qsc-adversarial script, workflow, dependency, or lockfile mutation. Classifications selected: `CORPUS_NO_CARGO_SCRIPT_WORKFLOW_IMPACT` and `CORPUS_FUZZ_TARGET_CHANGE_NEEDED`.
  - **Exact future scope selected:** Future NA-0489 may mutate `qsl/qsl-client/qsc/fuzz/fuzz_targets/qsc_binding_semantics.rs`, `docs/governance/evidence/NA-0489_qsl_binding_fuzz_ephemeral_seed_strategy_implementation_harness.md`, `tests/NA-0489_qsl_binding_fuzz_ephemeral_seed_strategy_implementation_testplan.md`, `DECISIONS.md`, `TRACEABILITY.md`, and `docs/ops/ROLLING_OPERATIONS_JOURNAL.md`. If deterministic recipes require qsc source/helper mutation, checked-in corpus, Cargo changes, script changes, workflow changes, dependency or lockfile changes, runtime JSON consumption, vector/input changes, formal/refimpl/service/public/backup mutation, or public-claim expansion, the future lane must STOP or split.
  - **No implementation mutation in this directive:** NA-0488 changes only governance evidence/testplan/decision/traceability/journal paths.
  - **No corpus/vector/input mutation:** NA-0488 does not add, delete, or modify corpus files, vector files, input fixtures, or manifest files.
  - **No runtime/crypto/dependency/Cargo/lockfile/workflow/test/fuzz/formal/refimpl mutation:** NA-0488 does not mutate qsc source, qsc fuzz target code, qsc fuzz Cargo metadata, qsc-adversarial scripts, workflows, dependencies, lockfiles, executable tests, formal models, or refimpl files.
  - **No backup/restore:** Codex did not run backup or restore and did not mutate qsl-backup, backup status, backup plan, rollback, or backup tree paths.

  - **Public claim boundary:** no public crypto-complete claim is made. no fuzz-complete claim is made. no corpus-complete claim is made. no vector-complete claim is made. no KEM-complete claim is made. no signature-complete claim is made. no replay-proof claim is made. no downgrade-proof claim is made. no vulnerability-free claim is made. no perfect-crypto claim is made. no side-channel-free claim is made. no external-review-complete claim is made. no public-readiness claim is made. no production-readiness claim is made. no public-internet-readiness claim is made. no identity-complete claim is made. no transcript-complete claim is made. no qsc/refimpl-equivalence-complete claim is made. no provider-boundary-complete claim is made. no provider-RNG-complete claim is made. no formal-proof-complete claim is made. - **ID:** D-0967
  - **Title:** NA-0489 binding fuzz ephemeral seed strategy implementation
  - **Status:** Accepted
  - **Date:** 2026-06-16
  - **Goals:** G1, G2, G3, G4, G5
  - **Decision:** NA-0489 consumes NA-0488/D351 and implements deterministic ephemeral seed generation inside the existing `qsc_binding_semantics` fuzz target. The implementation keeps the inherited arbitrary-byte fuzz path and adds target-local deterministic public/synthetic recipes for A1 mutation, B1 mutation, A2 mutation, suite confusion, replay, stale public-record / trusted-pin, and vector-manifest traceability categories.
  - **NA-0488/D351 consumed:** PR #1247 authorized no checked-in binding fuzz corpus, manifest traceability-only usage, no runtime JSON manifest reading, and target-only deterministic ephemeral seed recipes. PR #1248 closed NA-0488 and restored NA-0489 as the sole READY item.
  - **No checked-in corpus:** No `qsc_binding_semantics` corpus directory or corpus file is added. No qsc fuzz corpus path is mutated.
  - **Manifest traceability only:** The NA-0483 manifest remains traceability-only. Static category names are present in the fuzz target comments/constants, but the target does not read the manifest JSON at runtime and performs no file IO.
  - **No secret/private material:** Seed bytes are generated from public constants, static public labels, public byte-length constants, and fuzzer input bytes. No private key, KEM secret key, signing key, passphrase, runtime key, backup key, operator data, user data, live service data, private endpoint, PEM private header, or production-like secret string is added.
  - **No qsc source/helper mutation:** qsc source and helper files are unchanged. The implementation uses the existing cfg-gated helper API from the fuzz target only.
  - **No qsc fuzz Cargo mutation:** qsc fuzz Cargo metadata and qsc fuzz `Cargo.lock` are unchanged.
  - **No script/workflow/dependency/lockfile mutation:** qsc-adversarial scripts, workflows, dependencies, root `Cargo.lock`, and qsc fuzz `Cargo.lock` are unchanged.
  - **No vector/input mutation:** `inputs/` and `inputs/suite2/internal_negative_binding_vectors/` are unchanged.
  - **No refimpl/formal/service/public/backup mutation:** refimpl, formal, service, public-doc, qsl-server, qsl-attachments, qshield, qshield-cli, backup, qsl-backup, backup status, backup plan, rollback, qwork, qstart, qresume, and qshell paths are unchanged.
  - **Validation boundary:** Target validation includes `cargo fmt --check` and cfg-on fuzz target cargo check. Full local validation, scope guard, leak scan, overclaim scan, root audit, nested qsc fuzz lock audit, inherited qsc/refimpl/formal tests, local cargo-fuzz availability proof, qsc-adversarial syntax/script evidence, PR CI public-safety, and PR CI qsc-adversarial-smoke are required before merge/closeout. Cargo audit green remains dependency-health evidence only.
  - **Selected successor:** `NA-0490 -- QSL Binding Fuzz Corpus Secret-Material Validator Authorization Plan` is selected as the next safest residual because future checked-in binding corpus remains blocked until validator strategy is authorized. NA-0490 is not implemented by this decision.

- **ID:** D-0969
  - **Title:** NA-0490 binding fuzz corpus secret-material validator authorization
  - **Status:** Accepted
  - **Date:** 2026-06-17
  - **Goals:** G1, G2, G3, G4, G5
  - **Decision:** NA-0490 consumes NA-0489/D355 and in-tree D-0968 evidence, inventories the current qsc fuzz corpus and validator surface, defines the secret-material pattern matrix, reviews validator strategy options, classifies CI/workflow/dependency impact, and selects `BINDING_FUZZ_CORPUS_VALIDATOR_SCRIPT_READY` as the primary classification. The selected successor is `NA-0491 -- QSL Binding Fuzz Corpus Secret-Material Validator Implementation Harness`.
  - **NA-0489/D0968 consumed:** D-0967 implemented deterministic target-local ephemeral seed generation in `qsc_binding_semantics`; D-0968 closed NA-0489 after PR #1249 and restored NA-0490 as the sole READY item; D355 verified closeout PR #1250, public-safety, qsc-adversarial-smoke, disk recovery, and exactly-one-READY state. NA-0490 accepts this evidence as internal governance evidence only and does not represent it as fuzz-complete, corpus-complete, vector-complete, crypto-complete, or public-readiness evidence.
  - **Current corpus/validator surface inventoried:** Existing checked-in qsc fuzz corpus directories are `qsc_payload_boundaries` with 5 files, `qsc_route_http` with 3 files, and `qsc_vault_envelope` with 2 files. `qsc_binding_semantics` exists as a fuzz target but has no checked-in corpus directory. Existing `qsl_evidence_helper.py leak-scan` can scan added lines or full selected paths and detects common private-key/token patterns, but it does not provide corpus-specific secret-label rules, passphrase rules, qsc secret-key naming rules, high-entropy allowlist policy, or deterministic corpus validator JSON reporting.
  - **Secret-material pattern matrix defined:** Classification `SECRET_PATTERN_MATRIX_READY` is selected. Future validator reject/flag classes include PEM private key headers, OpenSSH private key headers, age/minisign/PGP/private markers if present, obvious API tokens, passphrase labels, KEM/signature/identity secret-key names, backup or recovery key names, runtime/service secret labels, private endpoints or production-like identifiers if policy disallows them, high-entropy unallowlisted blobs, actual qsc vault secret filenames or contents if detected, and user/operator data markers. Future allowed/safe classes include short synthetic byte arrays, mutated public message bytes, clearly synthetic/test/public public keys, vector IDs, manifest category names, non-secret labels, expected reject reason strings, small structured JSON metadata, and comments documenting internal-only status.
  - **Validator options reviewed:** Extending `qsl_evidence_helper.py` first is rejected because it broadens a shared helper surface and still needs corpus-specific rules. A dedicated repo-local validator script is selected. Ad hoc proof-root-only validation is rejected as non-durable. Requiring no checked-in corpus indefinitely is retained only as fallback. Vector-consumer tests and side-channel/external-review lanes remain residuals but do not solve the checked-in corpus secret-material gate.
  - **CI/workflow/dependency impact classified:** `VALIDATOR_NO_DEPENDENCY_NO_WORKFLOW_READY` and `VALIDATOR_WORKFLOW_INTEGRATION_LATER` are selected. Future implementation can use standard-library Python only, should initially run in local validation and future corpus implementation lanes, and does not require Cargo, qsc fuzz Cargo, lockfile, dependency, workflow, qsc source, qsc fuzz target, qsc-adversarial script, formal, refimpl, service, public-doc, backup, or qsl-backup mutation. Later CI/public-safety/qsc-adversarial/helper integration requires a separate exact directive.
  - **Exact future scope selected:** Future NA-0491 may mutate `scripts/audit/validate_binding_fuzz_corpus_no_secrets.py`, `tests/NA-0491_qsl_binding_fuzz_corpus_secret_material_validator_implementation_testplan.md`, `docs/governance/evidence/NA-0491_qsl_binding_fuzz_corpus_secret_material_validator_implementation_harness.md`, `DECISIONS.md`, `TRACEABILITY.md`, and `docs/ops/ROLLING_OPERATIONS_JOURNAL.md`. It should inspect all qsc fuzz corpus directories by default, treat future `qsc_binding_semantics` corpus as the immediate blocker, emit deterministic JSON when requested, redact matched secret values from output, and fail closed on secret findings.
  - **No implementation mutation in this directive:** NA-0490 does not implement the validator and does not mutate scripts, helpers, fuzz targets, qsc source, qsc fuzz Cargo, qsc fuzz lockfile, qsc-adversarial scripts, workflows, dependencies, lockfiles, vectors, inputs, corpus files, formal models, refimpl, services, public docs, README, START_HERE, website, backup, qsl-backup, backup status, backup plan, rollback, qwork, qstart, qresume, or qshell.
  - **No corpus/vector/input mutation:** NA-0490 does not add, delete, move, archive, or modify corpus files, vector files, input fixtures, or manifest files. Future checked-in binding corpus remains blocked until validator implementation exists and passes.
  - **No runtime/crypto/dependency/Cargo/lockfile/workflow/test/fuzz/formal/refimpl mutation:** NA-0490 changes only the authorized governance evidence/testplan/decision/traceability/journal paths.
  - **No backup/restore:** Codex did not run backup or restore and did not mutate qsl-backup, backup status, backup plan, rollback, archived paths, nightly/local-ops scripts, or `/backup/qsl`.
  - **Stewardship and assurance reviews used:** Level-1 stewardship, best-known-method, hostile cryptographer, red-team, production SRE, side-channel caveat, formal-model mapping residual, external-review readiness, release-claim boundary, and assurance-gap trigger reviews were used. Classifications: `BEST_KNOWN_METHOD_FOR_SCOPE`, `FORMAL_MODEL_MAPPING_SUPPORTING_ONLY`, `EXTERNAL_REVIEW_READINESS_INCREMENTAL`, and `HIGHER_PRIORITY_RESIDUAL_SUPERSEDES_ASSURANCE_REVIEW`. Level 2 and Level 3 remain future-gated. No separate Directors, independent READY promotion, or independent merge authority is created. Lead Director final authority is preserved.
  - **Public claim boundary:** no public-readiness claim is made. no production-readiness claim is made. no public-internet-readiness claim is made. no external-review-complete claim is made. no crypto-complete claim is made. no fuzz-complete claim is made. no corpus-complete claim is made. no vector-complete claim is made. no replay-proof claim is made. no downgrade-proof claim is made. no side-channel-free claim is made. no vulnerability-free claim is made. no bug-free claim is made. no perfect-crypto claim is made. Cargo audit green remains dependency-health evidence only.
  - **Required behavior:** NA-0490 remains authorization-only until optional closeout. Future NA-0491 must implement only the exact validator script/governance scope selected here unless a later directive authorizes narrower additional scope. Exactly one READY remains mandatory.
  - **Must never happen:** NA-0490 is represented as validator implementation, checked-in corpus evidence, public readiness, production readiness, public-internet readiness, external-review completion, crypto completion, fuzz completion, corpus completion, vector completion, replay proof, downgrade proof, side-channel freedom, vulnerability freedom, bug freedom, backup completion, restore proof, off-host backup completion, disaster recovery completion, or perfect crypto. Future NA-0491 is represented as authorization to mutate corpus, vectors, inputs, qsc source, qsc fuzz targets, qsc fuzz Cargo, qsc fuzz lockfiles, qsc-adversarial scripts, workflows, dependencies, lockfiles, formal models, refimpl, services, public docs, website, backups, qsl-backup, status/plan, rollback, or qwork/qstart/qresume/qshell outside exact successor scope.
  - **References:** NA-0490; NA-0491; D-0969; D-0968; D-0967; PR #1249; PR #1250; D352 response `/home/victor/work/qsl/codex/responses/NA0489_20260616T212856Z_D352.md`; D353 response `/home/victor/work/qsl/codex/responses/NA0489_closeout_restore_na0490_20260616T223437Z_D353.md`; D354 response `/home/victor/work/qsl/codex/responses/NA0489_disk_pressure_recovery_20260616T225916Z_D354.md`; D355 response `/home/victor/work/qsl/codex/responses/NA0489_closeout_restore_na0490_20260617T004737Z_D355.md`; `docs/governance/evidence/NA-0490_qsl_binding_fuzz_corpus_secret_material_validator_authorization_plan.md`; `tests/NA-0490_qsl_binding_fuzz_corpus_secret_material_validator_authorization_testplan.md`; `qsl/qsl-client/qsc/fuzz/corpus/`; `qsl/qsl-client/qsc/fuzz/fuzz_targets/qsc_binding_semantics.rs`; `inputs/suite2/internal_negative_binding_vectors/qsl_binding_negative_vector_manifest_v1.json`; `scripts/ci/qsl_evidence_helper.py`; `scripts/audit/`; `TRACEABILITY.md`; `docs/ops/ROLLING_OPERATIONS_JOURNAL.md`

- **ID:** D-0971
  - **Title:** NA-0491 binding fuzz corpus secret-material validator implementation
  - **Status:** Accepted
  - **Date:** 2026-06-17
  - **Goals:** G1, G2, G3, G4, G5
  - **Decision:** NA-0491 consumes NA-0490/D356 and D-0969/D-0970, implements `scripts/audit/validate_binding_fuzz_corpus_no_secrets.py` as a dependency-free Python validator, and records deterministic redacted JSON proof that the validator rejects disallowed secret-material classes while accepting current safe qsc fuzz corpus inputs.
  - **NA-0490/D356 consumed:** `BINDING_FUZZ_CORPUS_VALIDATOR_SCRIPT_READY`, `SECRET_PATTERN_MATRIX_READY`, `VALIDATOR_NO_DEPENDENCY_NO_WORKFLOW_READY`, and `VALIDATOR_WORKFLOW_INTEGRATION_LATER` are consumed and preserved. Future checked-in binding corpus remains blocked until a later exact corpus-scope directive uses the validator gate.
  - **Validator implemented:** The validator scans supplied paths recursively, defaults to `qsl/qsl-client/qsc/fuzz/corpus`, supports repeated `--path`, supports `--paths`, supports `--format json` and `--format text`, supports explicit `--allow-missing`, does not follow symlinks, and exits nonzero on findings. It emits deterministic JSON without timestamps and includes path, kind, severity, byte offset or line number, short context digest, and `[redacted]` for each finding.
  - **Secret-material reject proof:** Proof-root fixtures show private-key marker rejection, secret-label rejection, and high-entropy encoded-looking text rejection with redacted output. The secret-label fixture covers passphrase, KEM secret-key, signature secret-key, identity secret-key, backup/recovery key, runtime/service secret, private endpoint, user/operator data, and qsc vault secret filename/path label classes. No matched payload bytes are printed.
  - **Safe material proof:** Proof-root fixtures show short synthetic byte arrays, mutated public-message-like bytes, vector IDs, manifest category names, non-secret labels, expected reject strings, and small structured metadata are accepted. Existing checked-in qsc fuzz corpus files pass with zero findings.
  - **Missing corpus policy:** `qsl/qsl-client/qsc/fuzz/corpus/qsc_binding_semantics` remains absent. The validator accepts that missing path only with explicit `--allow-missing`; the same path without allow-missing exits nonzero.
  - **No corpus/vector/input mutation:** No checked-in corpus file, vector file, input file, or internal vector manifest is added, deleted, moved, archived, or modified. Proof fixtures are temp-only under the NA-0491 proof root.
  - **No forbidden implementation mutation:** No qsc source, qsc fuzz target, qsc fuzz Cargo, qsc fuzz lockfile, qsc-adversarial script, workflow, dependency, lockfile, formal model, refimpl, qsl-server, qsl-attachments, qshield runtime, qshield-cli, service, public-doc, README, START_HERE, website, backup, qsl-backup, backup status, backup plan, rollback, qwork, qstart, qresume, or qshell mutation is introduced.
  - **Validation boundary:** Local validation covers validator CLI/fixture/current-corpus/missing-corpus/deterministic JSON proof, py_compile with pycache redirected outside the repo, internal negative vector manifest JSON validation, formal model checks, qsc binding negative tests with and without `qsc_binding_fuzz_helper`, refimpl signature provider-boundary, refimpl `pqkem768`, qsc key lifecycle zeroization, qsc provider-error no-mutation, root cargo audit, nested qsc fuzz lock audit, cargo fmt, qsc-adversarial shell syntax, and local qsc-adversarial prerequisite Rust tests before the expected local cargo-fuzz unavailable boundary.
  - **Successor selected:** After merge and post-merge public-safety success, the selected successor is `NA-0492 -- QSL Binding Fuzz Checked-In Corpus Scope Authorization Plan`. NA-0492 must authorize exact checked-in corpus scope before any corpus implementation and must require the validator gate.
  - **Public claim boundary:** no public-readiness claim is made. no production-readiness claim is made. no public-internet-readiness claim is made. no external-review-complete claim is made. no crypto-complete claim is made. no fuzz-complete claim is made. no corpus-complete claim is made. no vector-complete claim is made. no replay-proof claim is made. no downgrade-proof claim is made. no side-channel-free claim is made. no vulnerability-free claim is made. no bug-free claim is made. no perfect-crypto claim is made. Cargo audit green remains dependency-health evidence only.
  - **Required behavior:** NA-0491 remains READY until a separate closeout marks it DONE and restores the selected NA-0492 successor. Exactly one READY remains mandatory.
  - **Must never happen:** NA-0491 is represented as checked-in corpus implementation, vector/input implementation, qsc runtime or crypto behavior mutation, dependency/workflow integration, public readiness, production readiness, public-internet readiness, external-review completion, crypto completion, fuzz completion, corpus completion, vector completion, replay proof, downgrade proof, side-channel freedom, vulnerability freedom, bug freedom, backup completion, restore proof, off-host backup completion, disaster recovery completion, or perfect crypto.
  - **References:** NA-0491; NA-0492; D-0971; D-0970; D-0969; D356 response `/home/victor/work/qsl/codex/responses/NA0490_20260617T031514Z_D356.md`; `scripts/audit/validate_binding_fuzz_corpus_no_secrets.py`; `docs/governance/evidence/NA-0491_qsl_binding_fuzz_corpus_secret_material_validator_implementation_harness.md`; `tests/NA-0491_qsl_binding_fuzz_corpus_secret_material_validator_implementation_testplan.md`; `qsl/qsl-client/qsc/fuzz/corpus/`; `inputs/suite2/internal_negative_binding_vectors/qsl_binding_negative_vector_manifest_v1.json`; `TRACEABILITY.md`; `docs/ops/ROLLING_OPERATIONS_JOURNAL.md`

  - **D359 stop recovered:** The D359 stop reason was a local cargo-fuzz availability probe writing to `/tmp/na0492_cargo_fuzz_version.out` outside the approved proof root. D360 records, hashes, copies, summarizes without trusting, deletes exactly that stray file if present, verifies it absent, and sets proof-output guard under the D360 proof root. Codex did not run qwork, qstart, or qresume.

The validator provides deterministic JSON/text behavior, rejects private-marker, secret-label, and high-entropy unallowlisted material, accepts safe synthetic public bytes, passes current qsc fuzz corpus, and accepts a missing binding corpus only with explicit `--allow-missing`.
  - **Current binding corpus scope inventoried:** Existing qsc fuzz corpus directories are `qsc_payload_boundaries` with 5 files, `qsc_route_http` with 3 files, and `qsc_vault_envelope` with 2 files. `qsl/qsl-client/qsc/fuzz/corpus/qsc_binding_semantics/` is absent. The `qsc_binding_semantics` fuzz target exists and already covers A1 mutation, B1 mutation, A2 mutation, suite-confusion, replay, stale public-record, and vector-manifest traceability categories. `scripts/ci/qsc_adversarial.sh` includes `qsc_binding_semantics`. Local cargo-fuzz was unavailable in the D360 environment and was recorded as local availability evidence only.

Ephemeral-only continuation, validator CI integration first, vector-consumer tests first, category-split authorization, and fuzz stabilization first are rejected as primary options but remain fallback or later hardening residuals.
  - **Corpus format/file strategy selected:** Future corpus format is raw binary libFuzzer input files. Future path is `qsl/qsl-client/qsc/fuzz/corpus/qsc_binding_semantics/`. Exact future files are `seed_00_a1_mutation.bin`, `seed_01_b1_mutation.bin`, `seed_02_a2_mutation.bin`, `seed_03_suite_confusion.bin`, `seed_04_replay.bin`, `seed_05_stale_public_record.bin`, and `seed_ff_vector_traceability.bin`. No corpus README or metadata file is authorized; traceability remains in governance evidence.

It requires no Cargo changes, qsc-adversarial script changes, workflow changes, dependency changes, or lockfile changes. Classification selected: `CORPUS_DATA_ONLY_NO_CARGO_SCRIPT_WORKFLOW_READY`.

- **ID:** D-0979
  - **Title:** NA-0495 binding fuzz corpus validator qsc-adversarial integration implementation
  - **Status:** Accepted
  - **Date:** 2026-06-18
  - **Goals:** G1, G2, G3, G4, G5
  - **Decision:** NA-0495 consumes NA-0494/D365 and integrates the existing binding fuzz corpus secret-material validator into `scripts/ci/qsc_adversarial.sh`. The qsc-adversarial script now scans the checked-in binding corpus and the full qsc fuzz corpus before cargo-fuzz target execution, and validator findings fail the script under existing fail-closed shell behavior.
  - **NA-0494/D365 consumed:** D-0977 selected `BINDING_FUZZ_VALIDATOR_QSC_ADVERSARIAL_INTEGRATION_READY`; D-0978 restored NA-0495 as the sole READY successor; D365 recorded that local/testplan-only gating, public-safety/helper/workflow integration, standalone CI job, and JSON artifact upload were not selected for first integration.
  - **Binding corpus scanned:** `qsl/qsl-client/qsc/fuzz/corpus/qsc_binding_semantics` is passed to the validator with text output before cargo-fuzz targets run.
  - **All qsc fuzz corpus scanned:** `qsl/qsl-client/qsc/fuzz/corpus` is also passed to the validator with text output before cargo-fuzz targets run.
  - **Validator findings fail the script:** The script runs with `set -eu`, invokes the validator directly, and performs no softening or warning-only conversion. Direct proof-root fixture validation showed validator findings exit nonzero.
  - **Binding corpus is not allow-missing:** The qsc-adversarial script contains no `--allow-missing` use, and direct missing-path proof without allow-missing exited nonzero.
  - **Existing qsc-adversarial targets preserved:** Existing stable Rust adversarial tests, the provider-error no-mutation step, target order, `qsc_binding_semantics` cargo-fuzz target, and target-specific `RUSTFLAGS` behavior remain in place.
  - **qsc-adversarial-smoke required:** Local execution reached the expected local `cargo fuzz` unavailable boundary after passing the new validator step; PR CI qsc-adversarial-smoke is required before merge as cargo-fuzz-backed evidence.
  - **No workflow mutation:** Workflow files are unchanged, and JSON artifact upload remains deferred to a later workflow-authorized lane.
  - **No dependency mutation:** Dependencies, Cargo metadata, and lockfiles are unchanged. Cargo audit green remains dependency-health evidence only.
  - **No validator script mutation:** `scripts/audit/validate_binding_fuzz_corpus_no_secrets.py` is unchanged.
  - **No corpus/vector/input mutation:** Corpus files, vector files, input fixtures, and the internal negative vector manifest are unchanged.
  - **No qsc source/fuzz target/Cargo mutation:** qsc source, qsc fuzz target code, qsc fuzz Cargo metadata, qsc fuzz lockfile, root Cargo files, and root lockfile are unchanged.
  - **No formal/refimpl/service/public/backup mutation:** formal models, refimpl, qsl-server, qsl-attachments, qshield runtime, qshield-cli, services, public docs, README, START_HERE, website, backup, qsl-backup, backup status, backup plan, rollback, qwork, qstart, qresume, and qshell paths are unchanged.
  - **No public claim expansion:** no public-readiness claim is made. no production-readiness claim is made. no public-internet-readiness claim is made. no external-review-complete claim is made. no crypto-complete claim is made. no fuzz-complete claim is made. no corpus-complete claim is made. no validator-complete claim beyond bounded internal evidence is made. no vector-complete claim is made. no replay-proof claim is made. no downgrade-proof claim is made. no side-channel-free claim is made. no vulnerability-free claim is made. no bug-free claim is made. no perfect-crypto claim is made.
  - **Selected successor:** `NA-0496 -- QSL Binding Negative Vector Consumer Test Scope Authorization Plan` is selected for closeout restoration if NA-0495 merges and post-merge public-safety is green. NA-0495 does not implement NA-0496.
  - **Required behavior:** NA-0495 remains READY until a separate closeout marks it DONE and restores the selected NA-0496 successor. Exactly one READY remains mandatory.
  - **Must never happen:** NA-0495 is represented as workflow mutation, validator mutation, corpus/vector/input mutation, qsc source/fuzz target/Cargo mutation, dependency/lockfile mutation, formal/refimpl/service/public/qshield/qsl-server/qsl-attachments mutation, backup/restore/qsl-backup mutation, public readiness, production readiness, public-internet readiness, external-review completion, crypto completion, fuzz completion, corpus completion, vector completion, replay proof, downgrade proof, side-channel freedom, vulnerability freedom, bug freedom, backup completion, restore proof, off-host backup completion, disaster recovery completion, or perfect crypto.

- **ID:** D-0981
  - **Title:** NA-0496 binding negative vector consumer test scope authorization
  - **Status:** Accepted
  - **Date:** 2026-06-18
  - **Goals:** G1, G2, G3, G4, G5
  - **Decision:** NA-0496 consumes NA-0495/D367, inventories the internal negative binding vector manifest and current qsc/test/fuzz/formal/refimpl/validator surfaces, reviews vector-consumer options, reviews claim boundaries, reviews CI/dependency/scope impact, and selects `BINDING_NEGATIVE_VECTOR_SCHEMA_MAPPING_TEST_READY` as the primary classification.
  - **NA-0495/D367 consumed:** D-0979 integrated the existing corpus secret-material validator into qsc-adversarial, and D-0980 closed NA-0495 and restored NA-0496 as the sole READY successor. The checked-in binding corpus exists with seven 8-byte public/synthetic seeds, the validator scans the binding corpus and all qsc fuzz corpus, qsc-adversarial-smoke and public-safety were green on startup main, and the internal negative binding vector manifest remains internal-only.
  - **Vector/test/manifest inventory completed:** the manifest schema keys, sections, all 34 vector IDs, qsc-frame/refimpl/formal layer counts, groups, reject/no-success/no-mutation expectations, material policy, public claim caveats, direct vector ID occurrences, marker mapping surfaces, existing qsc integration tests, qsc `serde_json` availability, and no-secret/metadata-only status were recorded.
  - **Consumer options reviewed:** a new qsc integration test is selected; extending `kem_signature_transcript_binding_negative.rs`, standalone audit script as primary, formal model consumer as primary, documentation-only mapping, full section split before first consumer, no-action/defer, and manifest-generated dynamic qsc assertions are rejected for first implementation scope.
  - **Claim boundary reviewed:** future consumption means reading/parsing the manifest and mapping vector IDs to evidence classes, not generating direct qsc handshake assertions from metadata. All 34 vectors should be schema/mapping checked; qsc-frame vectors are the qsc-facing subset; refimpl and formal-token vectors are supporting-only in first consumer scope.
  - **CI/dependency/scope impact classified:** selected classifications include `VECTOR_CONSUMER_NO_DEPENDENCY_READY`, `VECTOR_CONSUMER_QSC_TEST_SCOPE_READY`, and `VECTOR_CONSUMER_SCOPE_SPLIT_NEEDED` for later dynamic refimpl/formal/qsc execution. Future implementation requires no dependency, Cargo, lockfile, qsc source, workflow, script, helper, corpus, vector, input, formal, refimpl, service, public, backup, qwork, qstart, or qresume mutation.
  - **Selected NA-0497 successor:** `NA-0497 -- QSL Binding Negative Vector Consumer Test Implementation Harness`.
