- **ID:** D-0983
  - **Title:** NA-0497 binding negative vector consumer test implementation
  - **Status:** Accepted
  - **Date:** 2026-06-18
  - **Goals:** G1, G2, G3, G4, G5
  - **Decision:** NA-0497 consumes NA-0496/D372 and implements the selected qsc integration test `qsl/qsl-client/qsc/tests/binding_negative_vector_consumer.rs`. The test parses the internal negative binding vector manifest, validates schema/counts, validates layer/group coverage, validates mapping/traceability metadata, validates expected reject/no-success/no-mutation/provider-return-shape metadata, validates no-secret material policy, validates public-claim caveats, maps qsc-frame vectors to qsc-facing evidence classes, and keeps refimpl/formal sections supporting-only in this first consumer.
  - **NA-0496/D372 consumed:** NA-0496 is DONE, D-0981 records the future consumer scope authorization, D-0982 restores NA-0497 as the sole READY successor, D372 selected the future test path, and D372 bounded semantics to manifest schema/category/layer/mapping coverage without dynamic crypto execution claims.
  - **qsc integration test implemented:** `binding_negative_vector_consumer.rs` reads the manifest from `env!("CARGO_MANIFEST_DIR")` plus `../../../inputs/suite2/internal_negative_binding_vectors/qsl_binding_negative_vector_manifest_v1.json`, uses the existing qsc `serde_json` dependency, and does not add dependencies or mutate Cargo files.
  - **Manifest parsed and schema/counts validated:** the test asserts schema version `1`, status `internal-negative-evidence-only`, exact top-level keys, 3 sections, 34 total vectors, 21 qsc-frame vectors, 6 refimpl signature-provider-boundary vectors, 7 formal token-mapping vectors, exact vector ID sets, exact group counts, unique IDs, and required metadata fields.
  - **qsc-frame vectors mapped:** qsc-frame vectors are mapped to qsc-facing evidence classes through their `layer` and `group` metadata and required source-evidence/marker traceability.
  - **Refimpl vectors supporting-only:** refimpl signature-provider-boundary vectors are asserted as provider-boundary metadata only and are not treated as dynamic qsc runtime proof.
  - **Formal token vectors supporting-only:** formal token-mapping vectors are asserted as formal-token metadata only and are not treated as dynamic qsc runtime proof.
  - **No public/conformance vector claim:** manifest claim-boundary booleans require internal-only, not-public, not-conformance, not-interoperability, and not-completion status. Vector caveats must reject public/conformance/completion overclaim.
  - **No vector-complete claim:** validating all 34 metadata records is not represented as vector completeness or dynamic execution completeness.
  - **No replay-proof claim:** replay-related metadata is checked only as internal negative mapping evidence, with no replay-proof claim.
  - **No downgrade-proof claim:** downgrade-related metadata is checked only as internal negative mapping evidence, with no downgrade-proof claim.
  - **No qsc source mutation:** qsc source paths are unchanged.
  - **No qsc fuzz target/Cargo mutation:** qsc fuzz target code, qsc fuzz Cargo metadata, qsc fuzz lockfile, root Cargo metadata, and root lockfile are unchanged.
  - **No corpus/vector/input mutation:** corpus files, vector files, input fixtures, and the internal negative vector manifest are unchanged.
  - **No workflow/script/helper mutation:** workflows, qsc-adversarial scripts, validator scripts, CI helpers, evidence helpers, qwork, qstart, qresume, qshell, and local-ops scripts are unchanged.
  - **No dependency/lockfile mutation:** dependencies and lockfiles are unchanged. Cargo audit green remains dependency-health evidence only.
  - **No formal/refimpl/service/public/backup mutation:** formal models, refimpl, qsl-server, qsl-attachments, qshield runtime, qshield-cli, services, public docs, README, START_HERE, website, backup, qsl-backup, backup status, backup plan, rollback, archived paths, and `/backup/qsl` are unchanged.
  - **Selected successor:** `NA-0498 -- QSL Core Assurance Checkpoint and Next Highest-Risk Security Lane Authorization Plan` is selected for closeout restoration if NA-0497 merges and post-merge public-safety is green. NA-0497 does not implement NA-0498.
  - **Public claim boundary:** no public-readiness claim is made. no production-readiness claim is made. no public-internet-readiness claim is made. no public/conformance vector claim is made. no external-review-complete claim is made. no crypto-complete claim is made. no fuzz-complete claim is made. no corpus-complete claim is made. no vector-complete claim is made. no replay-proof claim is made. no downgrade-proof claim is made. no side-channel-free claim is made. no vulnerability-free claim is made. no bug-free claim is made. no perfect-crypto claim is made.
  - **Required behavior:** NA-0497 remains READY until a separate closeout marks it DONE and restores the selected NA-0498 successor. Exactly one READY remains mandatory.
  - **Must never happen:** NA-0497 drops any of these boundaries: no public-readiness claim, no production-readiness claim, no public-internet-readiness claim, no public/conformance vector publication claim, no external-review-complete claim, no crypto-complete claim, no fuzz-complete claim, no corpus-complete claim, no vector-complete claim, no replay-proof claim, no downgrade-proof claim, no side-channel-free claim, no vulnerability-free claim, no bug-free claim, no perfect-crypto claim, no qsc source mutation, no qsc fuzz mutation, no Cargo/dependency/lockfile mutation, no workflow/script/helper mutation, no corpus/vector/input mutation, no manifest mutation, no formal/refimpl/service/public/qshield/qsl-server/qsl-attachments mutation, no backup/restore/qsl-backup mutation, and no NA-0498 implementation.
  - **References:** NA-0497; NA-0498; D-0983; D-0982; D-0981; D372 response `/home/victor/work/qsl/codex/responses/NA0496_closeout_restore_na0497_20260618T191907Z_D372.md`; `qsl/qsl-client/qsc/tests/binding_negative_vector_consumer.rs`; `inputs/suite2/internal_negative_binding_vectors/qsl_binding_negative_vector_manifest_v1.json`; `docs/governance/evidence/NA-0497_qsl_binding_negative_vector_consumer_test_implementation_harness.md`; `tests/NA-0497_qsl_binding_negative_vector_consumer_test_implementation_testplan.md`; `TRACEABILITY.md`; `docs/ops/ROLLING_OPERATIONS_JOURNAL.md`

- **ID:** D-0985

  - **Status:** Accepted
  - **Date:** 2026-06-19
  - **Goals:** G1, G2, G3, G4, G5
  - **Decision:** NA-0498 consumes NA-0497/D375 inheritance, inventories current direct, bounded, supporting-only, governance-only, absent, and residual-open security evidence, reviews highest-risk residuals, applies hostile cryptographer, red-team, production SRE, side-channel caveat, formal-model mapping residual, external-review readiness, and release-claim boundary reviews, completes a prioritization matrix, and selects `CORE_ASSURANCE_SIDE_CHANNEL_SECRET_MATERIAL_NEXT` as the primary classification.
  - **NA-0497/D375 consumed:** NA-0497 is DONE, NA-0498 is READY, D-0983 records the vector consumer implementation, D-0984 records closeout/restoration, D375 response exists, the qsc binding negative vector consumer test consumes the internal manifest as executable schema/category/layer/mapping evidence, and the internal-only/no-public-conformance-vector boundary is preserved.
  - **Evidence inventory completed:** identity/provider RNG, KEM/signature/transcript binding, replay/downgrade/stale/rollback behavior, secret-material lifecycle, qsc/refimpl/formal mapping, fuzz/corpus/vector evidence, external-review readiness, public-claim boundary, local CI/tooling, and backup/off-host/restore/key-custody residuals were inventoried.
  - **Highest-risk residual review completed:** side-channel / secret-material lifecycle scope was classified as the highest current core security and external-review readiness reducer. X25519/ephemeral provider RNG, qsc/refimpl/formal mapping, stale identity / replay / rollback expansion, external-review package, local cargo-fuzz parity, CI watcher tooling, disk/archive routine, backup/off-host/restore/key-custody, public technical paper timing, and D132 cleanup remain residuals or deferred alternatives.
  - **Hostile cryptographer/red-team/SRE/release-claim reviews completed:** the reviews identify bounded formal/model overinterpretation, vector-consumer overinterpretation, qsc/refimpl non-equivalence, provider RNG residuals, secret-material leakage, diagnostic/logging leakage, side-channel caveats, and operational confidence drift as the main risks. Process/tooling lanes are rejected because they do not currently block core assurance.
  - **Prioritization matrix completed:** the matrix compares side-channel / secret-material lifecycle, X25519 / ephemeral provider RNG, qsc/refimpl mapping, stale identity / replay / rollback deeper tests, qsc/refimpl/formal mapping checkpoint, external-review readiness package, local cargo-fuzz parity, CI watcher/wait tooling, disk/archive routine, backup/off-host/restore/key-custody, and public technical paper/external claim boundary.
  - **Selected NA-0499 successor:** `NA-0499 -- QSL Side-Channel / Secret-Material Lifecycle Assurance Scope Authorization Plan`.
  - **Exact future scope:** future NA-0499 is authorization-only for secret-material lifecycle assurance, including in-memory lifetime caveats, zeroization evidence boundaries, secret-output discipline, diagnostic/logging boundaries, and side-channel non-claim preservation. Future NA-0499 may mutate only its governance evidence/testplan, `DECISIONS.md`, `TRACEABILITY.md`, and rolling journal unless a later exact directive authorizes otherwise. Future read-only inspection may cover qsc identity/key lifecycle tests, TUI bootstrap pre-generation evidence, provider-error evidence, qsc/refimpl crypto helpers, formal evidence, and public-claim boundaries.
  - **No implementation mutation in this directive:** NA-0498 changes only the authorization evidence doc, authorization testplan, `DECISIONS.md`, `TRACEABILITY.md`, and `docs/ops/ROLLING_OPERATIONS_JOURNAL.md`.

  - **No public claim expansion:** no public-readiness claim is made. no production-readiness claim is made. no public-internet-readiness claim is made. no public/conformance vector claim is made. no external-review-complete claim is made. no crypto-complete claim is made. no KEM-complete claim is made. no signature-complete claim is made. no identity-complete claim is made. no provider-RNG-complete claim is made. no fuzz-complete claim is made. no corpus-complete claim is made. no vector-complete claim is made. no replay-proof claim is made. no downgrade-proof claim is made. no side-channel-free claim is made. no vulnerability-free claim is made. no bug-free claim is made. no perfect-crypto claim is made.

  - **NA-0498/D377 consumed:** NA-0498 is DONE, NA-0499 is READY, D-0985 records the core assurance checkpoint, D-0986 records closeout/restoration, and D377 response exists. The inherited checkpoint selected side-channel / secret-material lifecycle work as the top current core assurance risk.

- **ID:** D-0989
  - **Title:** NA-0500 qsc secret-material diagnostic no-output boundary test implementation
  - **Status:** Accepted
  - **Date:** 2026-06-19
  - **Goals:** G1, G2, G3, G4, G5
  - **Decision:** NA-0500 consumes NA-0499/D379 inheritance and implements the selected qsc integration test `qsl/qsl-client/qsc/tests/secret_material_diagnostic_boundary.rs`. The test exercises selected deterministic qsc reject/error/diagnostic surfaces, captures stdout, stderr, process error output, and test-visible diagnostic strings, and asserts those captured diagnostics do not contain synthetic secret-shaped marker labels or long high-entropy-looking material.
  - **NA-0499/D379 consumed:** NA-0499 is DONE, NA-0500 is READY, D-0987 records the side-channel / secret-material lifecycle assurance scope authorization, D-0988 records NA-0499 closeout and NA-0500 restoration, and D379 response exists. The inherited selected classification is `SECRET_MATERIAL_DIAGNOSTIC_NO_OUTPUT_TEST_READY`.
  - **qsc integration test implemented:** `secret_material_diagnostic_boundary.rs` uses existing qsc integration-test helpers, `common::TestIsolation`, the real qsc binary, plain qsc markers, synthetic temp roots, and no new dependencies.
  - **Selected reject/error/diagnostic paths tested:** the test covers invalid policy-profile CLI error output, util-sanitize usage error output including stderr usage text, and a real handshake `identity_unknown` reject path that emits `identity_unknown`, `handshake_reject`, and error markers.
  - **Output scrub checks pass:** captured real qsc diagnostics pass the scanner for private-key, passphrase, KEM secret, signature secret, shared-secret, backup/recovery key, runtime/service secret, private endpoint, operator/user data, route-token marker, qsp session-store key, pending-handshake secret, identity-signing secret, and high-entropy-looking material.
  - **Synthetic secret marker fail proof included:** the test feeds synthetic marker labels into the scanner and asserts findings are produced before printing NA-0500 marker-absence OK markers. The fixtures use synthetic labels only and no real secret values.
  - **No qsc source mutation:** qsc source paths are unchanged.
  - **No qsc fuzz/Cargo mutation:** qsc fuzz target code, qsc fuzz Cargo metadata, qsc fuzz lockfile, root Cargo metadata, root lockfile, dependencies, and lockfiles are unchanged.
  - **No corpus/vector/input mutation:** corpus files, vector files, input fixtures, and internal manifests are unchanged.
  - **No workflow/script/helper mutation:** workflows, qsc-adversarial scripts, validator scripts, CI helpers, evidence helpers, local-ops scripts, qwork, qstart, qresume, and qshell are unchanged.
  - **No refimpl/formal/service/public/backup mutation:** formal models, refimpl, qsl-server, qsl-attachments, qshield runtime, qshield-cli, services, public docs, README, START_HERE, website, backup, qsl-backup, backup status, backup plan, rollback, archived paths, nightly/local-ops scripts, `/backup/qsl`, and public-surface paths are unchanged.
  - **Validation evidence:** targeted qsc diagnostic boundary test passed; inherited qsc key lifecycle zeroization test passed; inherited qsc provider-error no-mutation test passed; binding corpus validator and all qsc fuzz corpus validator scans passed with zero findings. Full pre-PR validation remains required by the NA-0500 testplan.
  - **Selected successor:** `NA-0501 -- QSL qsc Key Lifecycle / Zeroization Expansion Scope Authorization Plan` is selected as the default closeout successor if NA-0500 merges and post-merge public-safety is green. NA-0500 does not implement NA-0501.
  - **Public claim boundary:** no public-readiness claim is made. no production-readiness claim is made. no public-internet-readiness claim is made. no external-review-complete claim is made. no crypto-complete claim is made. no KEM-complete claim is made. no signature-complete claim is made. no identity-complete claim is made. no provider-RNG-complete claim is made. no secret-material-complete claim is made. no zeroization-complete claim is made. no memory-erasure-complete claim is made. no side-channel-free claim is made. no vulnerability-free claim is made. no bug-free claim is made. no perfect-crypto claim is made. Cargo audit green remains dependency-health evidence only.
  - **Exactly one READY remains mandatory:** NA-0500 remains READY until a separate closeout marks it DONE and restores the selected NA-0501 successor. D-0990 remains absent at implementation authoring.
  - **Required behavior:** NA-0500 remains implementation scoped only to the selected qsc diagnostic / no-output boundary test and governance surfaces until merge and closeout. Exactly one READY remains mandatory.
  - **Must never happen:** NA-0500 is represented as public readiness, production readiness, public-internet readiness, external-review completion, crypto completion, KEM completion, signature completion, identity completion, provider RNG completion, secret-material completion, zeroization completion, memory-erasure completion, side-channel freedom, vulnerability freedom, bug freedom, perfect crypto, qsc source mutation, qsc fuzz/Cargo mutation, workflow/script/helper mutation, dependency/lockfile mutation, corpus/vector/input/internal-manifest mutation, formal/refimpl/service/public/qshield/qsl-server/qsl-attachments mutation, backup/restore/qsl-backup mutation, or NA-0501 implementation.

- **ID:** D-0991
  - **Title:** NA-0501 qsc key lifecycle zeroization expansion scope authorization
  - **Status:** Accepted
  - **Date:** 2026-06-19
  - **Goals:** G1, G2, G3, G4, G5
  - **Decision:** NA-0501 consumes NA-0500/D384 inheritance and PR #1273 queue-heading repair evidence, inventories current qsc key lifecycle / zeroization evidence, compares client-to-client E2E and other residual assurance candidates, and selects `NA-0502 -- QSL qsc Key Lifecycle Zeroization Expansion Test Implementation Harness` as the exact successor. Primary classification: `KEY_LIFECYCLE_ZEROIZATION_EXPANSION_TEST_READY`.
  - **NA-0500/D384 consumed:** NA-0500 is DONE, NA-0501 is READY, D-0989 records the qsc secret-material diagnostic no-output boundary test implementation, D-0990 records NA-0500 closeout and NA-0501 restoration, and D384 response exists at `/home/victor/work/qsl/codex/responses/NA0500_closeout_restore_na0501_20260619T173827Z_D384.md`.
  - **PR #1273 repair consumed:** PR #1273 merged as a formatting-only queue-heading repair that changed only `NEXT_ACTIONS.md`; qwork/helper queue parsing is clean again with READY_COUNT 1 and READY NA-0501.
  - **Inventory completed:** qsc identity KEM/signing secret lifecycle, KEM secret-key lifecycle, KEM shared-secret lifecycle, transcript/confirm/session material, X25519/ephemeral residual, pending-handshake success and reject cleanup, session-store and temp-root artifact behavior, key lifecycle zeroization tests, provider-error no-mutation evidence, NA-0500 diagnostic/no-output evidence, corpus/vector/validator evidence, refimpl supporting evidence, formal bounded evidence, side-channel caveats, and memory-erasure caveats were reviewed.
  - **Client-to-client E2E comparison completed:** same-host client-to-client E2E remains a near-term core assurance candidate, but current lifecycle evidence gaps are narrower, directly aligned to NA-0501, and feasible as a qsc integration test without source/dependency/workflow mutation. Existing qsc tests already provide partial two-root and seeded send/receive/reply evidence, so E2E is deferred, not rejected.
  - **Option review completed:** the new qsc key lifecycle zeroization expansion test is selected; extending the existing key lifecycle test, a standalone temp-root artifact scan, pending-handshake reject cleanup scope, TUI/bootstrap memory lifetime scope, X25519/ephemeral lifecycle scope, same-host client-to-client E2E scope, and process/tooling work are rejected or deferred with recorded rationale.

- **ID:** D-0993
  - **Title:** NA-0502 qsc key lifecycle zeroization expansion test implementation
  - **Status:** Accepted
  - **Date:** 2026-06-19
  - **Goals:** G1, G2, G3, G4, G5
  - **Decision:** NA-0502 consumes NA-0501/D386 inheritance and implements the bounded qsc integration test at `qsl/qsl-client/qsc/tests/key_lifecycle_zeroization_expansion.rs`. The test checks selected lifecycle/cleanup surfaces: identity KEM/signing secret rotation and public-record artifact boundaries, plus responder pending-confirm reject and session-artifact boundaries. The selected successor after successful merge and closeout is `NA-0503 -- QSL qsc Same-Host Client-to-Client End-to-End Test Scope Authorization Plan` unless a concrete defect changes the successor.
  - **NA-0501/D386 consumed:** NA-0501 completed, NA-0502 was restored READY, selected classification `KEY_LIFECYCLE_ZEROIZATION_EXPANSION_TEST_READY` was consumed, selected future path `qsl/qsl-client/qsc/tests/key_lifecycle_zeroization_expansion.rs` was used, and the new-file preference over modifying `key_lifecycle_zeroization.rs` was honored.
  - **qsc integration test implemented:** `key_lifecycle_zeroization_expansion.rs` uses existing qsc integration-test helpers, synthetic test roots, qsc mock vault inspection, and the existing inbox test server. It introduces no new dependencies.
  - **Selected lifecycle/cleanup surfaces tested:** identity rotation replacement/absence boundaries are checked for generated KEM/signing secret material in current selected artifacts, and responder pending-confirm reject/session artifact boundaries are checked after A1/B1 pending state.
  - **Reject/artifact boundary checked:** malformed synthetic confirm input is rejected by qsc, no handshake completion marker appears, no completed session artifact is created or changed, no session-store key is populated before confirm completion, no legacy pending plaintext artifact appears, and the synthetic marker is not retained in selected artifacts or diagnostics.
  - **Recovered failure evidence:** the first isolated test run failed before reaching the selected surface because the new test used a synthetic route-token shape rejected by existing qsc validation. Classification: recoverable in-scope local validation failure with understood cause. Corrective action: changed only the new test constants to the valid route-token shape used by existing qsc integration tests. Final result: rerun passed with all required NA-0502 markers emitted.
  - **No qsc source mutation:** qsc source paths are unchanged.
  - **No qsc fuzz/Cargo mutation:** qsc fuzz target code, qsc fuzz Cargo metadata, qsc fuzz lockfile, root Cargo metadata, root lockfile, dependencies, and lockfiles are unchanged.
  - **No corpus/vector/input mutation:** corpus files, vector files, input fixtures, and internal manifests are unchanged.
  - **No workflow/script/helper mutation:** workflows, qsc-adversarial scripts, validator scripts, CI helpers, evidence helpers, local-ops scripts, qwork, qstart, qresume, and qshell are unchanged.
  - **No dependency/lockfile mutation:** dependencies and lockfiles are unchanged. Cargo audit green remains dependency-health evidence only.

Local validation also passed for git diff check, inherited qsc key lifecycle, diagnostic, provider-error, binding vector consumer tests, binding corpus validator, all qsc fuzz corpus validator, formal model runner, root cargo audit, nested qsc fuzz lock audit, cargo fmt, and qsc-adversarial shell syntax under both sh and bash.
  - **Selected successor:** `NA-0503 -- QSL qsc Same-Host Client-to-Client End-to-End Test Scope Authorization Plan` is selected as the default closeout successor if NA-0502 merges and post-merge public-safety is green. NA-0502 does not implement NA-0503.

- **ID:** D-0995

  - **Status:** Accepted

  - **Goals:** G1, G2, G3, G4, G5
  - **Decision:** NA-0503 consumes NA-0502/D388 inheritance, completes the current qsc client-to-client / E2E surface inventory, completes option review, completes hostile cryptographer/red-team/production SRE/release-claim reviews, completes the prioritization matrix, and selects `SAME_HOST_CLIENT_TO_CLIENT_E2E_IMPLEMENTATION_READY` as the primary classification.
  - **NA-0502/D388 consumed:** NA-0502 is DONE, NA-0503 is READY, D-0993 records the qsc key lifecycle zeroization expansion implementation, D-0994 records NA-0502 closeout and NA-0503 restoration, and D388 response exists at `/home/victor/work/qsl/codex/responses/NA0502_closeout_restore_na0503_20260619T215318Z_D388.md`.

Existing qsc surfaces can support deterministic same-host Alice/Bob E2E using two independent client roots and existing CLI/test-visible patterns without qsc source/helper/dependency/workflow mutation.
  - **Option review completed:** same-host client-to-client E2E implementation is selected; same-host CLI smoke, two-root identity-only, mock-relay split authorization, negative-only E2E authorization, continued internal lifecycle/mapping tests, and process/tooling lanes are rejected or deferred with recorded rationale. Remote/LAN client-to-client scope is deferred because it is out of scope and requires separate operator/host/network authorization.

- **ID:** D-0996
  - **Title:** NA-0503 closeout and NA-0504 restoration
  - **Status:** Accepted

  - **Goals:** G1, G2, G3, G4, G5
  - **Decision:** NA-0503 is closed after authorization PR #1278 merged at `5c39ddba4991` and post-merge public-safety completed success on `5c39ddba4991` inside the short attach/early-failure window. D-0995 exists exactly once and records the same-host client-to-client E2E implementation authorization. This closeout accepts NA-0503, marks NA-0503 DONE, and restores `NA-0504 -- QSL qsc Same-Host Client-to-Client End-to-End Test Implementation Harness` as the sole READY successor.
  - **Evidence consumed:** PR #1278 merged state, implementation authorization commit prefix `eaf0680537d2`, merge commit `5c39ddba4991`, D-0995, NA-0503 evidence doc, NA-0503 authorization testplan, PR #1278 post-merge public-safety success, short post-merge no-red check poll, exact closeout scope, and rolling journal wait accounting are accepted as closeout evidence.

  - **Selected successor:** NA-0504 is restored for a bounded same-host qsc integration test using two independent temp client roots and realistic Alice/Bob behavior, including identity setup, public-record/trusted-pin exchange, message exchange, reply, and at least one negative reject/no-mutation boundary.

- **ID:** D-0997
  - **Title:** NA-0504 qsc same-host client-to-client E2E test implementation
  - **Status:** Accepted
  - **Date:** 2026-06-20
  - **Goals:** G1, G2, G3, G4, G5
  - **Decision:** NA-0504 consumes NA-0503/D389 inheritance and implements `qsl/qsl-client/qsc/tests/same_host_client_to_client_e2e.rs` as the selected same-host qsc Alice/Bob client-to-client integration test. The test uses two independent same-host temp client roots, performs identity setup, exchanges public identity fingerprints through trusted contact/device records, completes local relay-backed handshake setup, tests Alice-to-Bob send/receive, tests Bob-to-Alice reply send/receive, tests wrong-mailbox reject/no-mutation behavior, and tests captured stdout/stderr/diagnostics for secret-shaped output. Selected successor after successful merge and green post-merge protection checks is `NA-0505 -- QSL Remote Client-to-Client Test Account / SSH Boundary Scope Authorization Plan`.
  - **NA-0503/D389 consumed:** NA-0503 is DONE, NA-0504 is READY, D-0995 records same-host client-to-client E2E implementation authorization, D-0996 records NA-0503 closeout and NA-0504 restoration, and D389 response exists at `/home/victor/work/qsl/codex/responses/NA0503_20260620T013404Z_D389.md`.
  - **qsc same-host E2E integration test implemented:** the new test file exercises real qsc CLI behavior through existing qsc integration-test patterns and the existing local inbox server. It is not a static fixture scan and not a fake oracle.
  - **Two independent client roots used:** Alice and Bob each use a separate `common::TestIsolation` root and distinct `QSC_CONFIG_DIR`; the test asserts both roots differ.
  - **Identity setup and public-record/trust exchange tested:** Alice and Bob run `identity rotate --confirm`, expose qsc identity fingerprints through `identity show`, add each other as contacts with route tokens, list contact devices, and trust those devices.
  - **Send/receive flow tested:** after local relay-backed handshake setup, Alice sends a synthetic payload to Bob and Bob receives and verifies the received bytes.
  - **Reply flow tested:** Bob sends a synthetic reply to Alice and Alice receives and verifies the received bytes.
  - **Negative reject/no-mutation boundary tested:** a wrong-mailbox receive attempt fails closed, creates no receive-output artifact, preserves the selected Bob session artifact bytes, and leaves the valid queued message receivable afterward.
  - **stdout/stderr no-secret-output boundary tested:** captured real qsc stdout/stderr/diagnostics are scanned for synthetic forbidden secret-shaped markers and high-entropy-looking material; the scanner is also tested against synthetic marker strings.
  - **No remote SSH or two-machine setup:** NA-0504 uses only same-host loopback/local mock relay behavior and does not run SSH, mutate remote hosts, create remote accounts, generate or install SSH keys, or perform LAN/two-machine testing.
  - **No qsc source mutation:** qsc source files are unchanged.
  - **No qsc fuzz/Cargo mutation:** qsc fuzz target code, qsc fuzz Cargo metadata, qsc fuzz lockfile, root Cargo metadata, and root lockfile are unchanged.
  - **No corpus/vector/input mutation:** corpus files, vector files, input fixtures, and internal manifests are unchanged.
  - **No workflow/script/helper mutation:** workflows, qsc-adversarial scripts, validator scripts, CI helpers, evidence helpers, local-ops scripts, qwork, qstart, qresume, and qshell are unchanged.
  - **No dependency/lockfile mutation:** dependencies and lockfiles are unchanged. Cargo audit green remains dependency-health evidence only.
  - **No refimpl/formal/service/public/backup mutation:** formal models, refimpl, qsl-server, qsl-attachments, qshield runtime, qshield-cli, services, public docs, README, START_HERE, website, backup, qsl-backup, backup status, backup plan, rollback, archived paths, nightly/local-ops scripts, `/backup/qsl`, and public-surface paths are unchanged.
  - **Public claim boundary:** no public-readiness claim is made. no production-readiness claim is made. no public-internet-readiness claim is made. no external-review-complete claim is made. no crypto-complete claim is made. no KEM-complete claim is made. no signature-complete claim is made. no identity-complete claim is made. no provider-RNG-complete claim is made. no secret-material-complete claim is made. no zeroization-complete claim is made. no memory-erasure-complete claim is made. no replay-proof claim is made. no downgrade-proof claim is made. no side-channel-free claim is made. no vulnerability-free claim is made. no bug-free claim is made. no perfect-crypto claim is made.
  - **Selected successor:** `NA-0505 -- QSL Remote Client-to-Client Test Account / SSH Boundary Scope Authorization Plan`, authorization-only and no remote action, is selected as the default closeout successor unless post-merge evidence reveals a concrete defect that requires a recovery/hardening authorization lane.
  - **Required behavior:** NA-0504 remains READY until a separate closeout marks it DONE and restores the selected NA-0505 successor. Exactly one READY remains mandatory.
  - **Must never happen:** NA-0504 is represented as remote/LAN readiness, production/public readiness, crypto completion, replay proof, downgrade proof, side-channel freedom, secret-material completion, zeroization completion, memory-erasure completion, vulnerability freedom, bug freedom, perfect crypto, qsc source mutation, qsc fuzz/Cargo mutation, workflow/script/helper mutation, dependency/lockfile mutation, corpus/vector/input/internal-manifest mutation, formal/refimpl/service/public/qshield/qsl-server/qsl-attachments mutation, remote SSH setup, two-machine setup, backup/restore/qsl-backup mutation, or NA-0505 implementation.
  - **References:** NA-0504; selected NA-0505; D-0997; D-0996; D-0995; D389 response `/home/victor/work/qsl/codex/responses/NA0503_20260620T013404Z_D389.md`; `qsl/qsl-client/qsc/tests/same_host_client_to_client_e2e.rs`; `docs/governance/evidence/NA-0504_qsl_qsc_same_host_client_to_client_e2e_test_implementation_harness.md`; `tests/NA-0504_qsl_qsc_same_host_client_to_client_e2e_test_implementation_testplan.md`; `qsl/qsl-client/qsc/tests/receive_e2e.rs`; `qsl/qsl-client/qsc/tests/key_lifecycle_zeroization_expansion.rs`; `qsl/qsl-client/qsc/tests/secret_material_diagnostic_boundary.rs`; `qsl/qsl-client/qsc/tests/handshake_provider_error_no_mutation.rs`; `qsl/qsl-client/qsc/tests/binding_negative_vector_consumer.rs`; `scripts/audit/validate_binding_fuzz_corpus_no_secrets.py`; `TRACEABILITY.md`; `docs/ops/ROLLING_OPERATIONS_JOURNAL.md`

Optional `remote` convenience alias is permitted only if a future setup lane proves no local collision and records explicit operator approval. Codex must not generate keys, read private key material, install keys, run SSH, run ssh-keyscan, mutate local SSH config, mutate system SSH config, mutate known_hosts, mutate authorized_keys, or mutate remote hosts in NA-0505 or NA-0506.

- **ID:** D-1001
  - **Title:** NA-0506 remote test account SSH operator setup runbook implementation
  - **Status:** Accepted
  - **Date:** 2026-06-20
  - **Goals:** G1, G2, G3, G4, G5
  - **Decision:** NA-0506 consumes NA-0505/D393, D-0999, and D-1000 inheritance and implements the operator setup runbook and proof checklist at `docs/governance/evidence/NA-0506_qsl_remote_test_account_ssh_operator_setup_runbook.md` plus the testplan at `tests/NA-0506_qsl_remote_test_account_ssh_operator_setup_testplan.md`.
  - **Runbook implemented:** the runbook documents the remote account model, SSH key model, host alias model, local operator responsibilities, Codex restrictions, remote host restrictions, dedicated remote directory model, future setup checklist, future proof checklist, cleanup/revocation plan, failure handling, paste-back boundaries, claim boundaries, stewardship reviews, and markers.
  - **Remote account model documented:** future setup uses a dedicated non-root project test user placeholder, no sudo/admin capability, no primary operator account, no production data access, no backup access, and a dedicated test work root.
  - **SSH key model documented:** future setup uses a dedicated per-project operator-owned key, generated only by the operator outside Codex, with no private key in repo, no private key pasted to Codex, no passphrase pasted to Codex, no personal key reuse, and key-only login if feasible.
  - **Host alias model documented:** default alias is `qsl-remote-test`; optional `remote` is allowed only after collision check and explicit operator approval because it is convenient but riskier and more collision-prone.
  - **Stewardship and assurance:** Best-Known-Method Review, Hostile Cryptographer Review, Red-Team Review, Production SRE Review, Side-Channel Caveat, Formal-Model Mapping Residual, External-Review Readiness, Release-Claim Boundary, and Assurance Gap Review Trigger are recorded as advisory evidence; the Lead Director remains final authority.
  - **No remote action:** NA-0506 performs no remote action, no SSH execution, no scp/sftp/rsync execution, no remote account creation, no remote test execution, and no remote host mutation.
  - **No SSH/key/config mutation:** NA-0506 performs no SSH key generation or installation, no local SSH config mutation, no system SSH config mutation, no known_hosts mutation, no authorized_keys mutation, and no ssh-keyscan or ssh-keygen action.
  - **No privileged/setup mutation:** NA-0506 performs no sudo/admin action, no package installation, no service installation, no persistent daemon setup, no qwork/qstart/qresume mutation or remote execution, and no qsl-backup execution or mutation.
  - **No implementation-code mutation:** NA-0506 changes no qsc source, no qsc test source, no qsc fuzz target, no qsc Cargo metadata, no root Cargo metadata, no dependency/lockfile, no workflow, no script/helper, no validator, no corpus/vector/input/internal-manifest, no formal model, no refimpl, no qsl-server, no qsl-attachments, no qshield runtime, no qshield-cli, no public docs, no backup plan/status, no qsl-backup, no rollback/archive, and no `/backup/qsl` path.

  - **NA-0507/D396 consumed:** NA-0507 is DONE, NA-0508 is READY, D-1003 records operator manual setup authorization, D-1004 records NA-0507 closeout and NA-0508 restoration, and D396 exists at `/home/victor/work/qsl/codex/responses/NA0507_closeout_restore_na0508_20260620T161558Z_D396.md`.

  - **Backup/qwork/qsl-backup boundary reviewed:** `/backup/qsl` is documented absent or not readable by `qslcodex`; qwork and qsl-backup are documented absent on the remote host and absent for the account.

  - **No remote action in this directive:** NA-0508 performs no remote action, no SSH execution, no scp/sftp/rsync execution to remote, no remote account creation, no remote test execution, and no remote host mutation.

- **ID:** D-1007
  - **Title:** NA-0509 remote host capability probe scope authorization
  - **Status:** Accepted
  - **Date:** 2026-06-20
  - **Goals:** G1, G2, G3, G4, G5
  - **Decision:** NA-0509 consumes NA-0508/D397 inheritance, accepts the operator setup proof as bounded setup/account proof only, designs the exact future remote host capability probe scope, selects the exact future command family, expected outputs, redaction rules, and stop conditions, and selects primary classification `REMOTE_READ_ONLY_CAPABILITY_PROBE_IMPLEMENTATION_READY`. The selected successor is `NA-0510 -- QSL Remote Host Read-Only Capability Probe Implementation Harness`.
  - **NA-0508/D397 consumed:** NA-0508 is DONE, NA-0509 is READY, D-1005 records the operator setup proof review, D-1006 records NA-0508 closeout and NA-0509 restoration, and D397 exists at `/home/victor/work/qsl/codex/responses/NA0508_20260620T211615Z_D397.md`.
  - **Operator setup proof accepted:** prior proof accepted remote alias `inspiron`, remote account `qslcodex`, optional alias `remote` not approved/configured, non-root/no-sudo/no-privileged-groups account boundary, writable `qsl-remote-test` workdir, no remote qwork, no remote qsl-backup, no readable backup exposure, host key fingerprint match, public key fingerprint match, and no private key/passphrase/token/password/production endpoint/backup material.
  - **Remote capability probe scope designed:** future NA-0510 may first run redacted local `ssh -G inspiron` and optional `ssh -G remote` effective-config proofs, then run exactly one bounded non-interactive read-only SSH invocation: `ssh -o BatchMode=yes -o PasswordAuthentication=no -o ConnectTimeout=10 inspiron 'bash -s'`.
  - **Future remote command family selected:** future remote script is limited to `hostname`, `id -un`, `id -u`, `id -Gn`, `pwd`, fixed `printf`, `test -d "$HOME/qsl-remote-test"`, `test -w "$HOME/qsl-remote-test"`, `sudo -n true` as a negative check only, `test -e /backup/qsl`, `test -r /backup/qsl`, `command -v qwork`, and `command -v qsl-backup`.
  - **Expected outputs selected:** future success markers include account `qslcodex`, non-root, non-privileged groups, workdir exists, workdir writable, no sudo, no backup exposure, qwork absent, qsl-backup absent, no remote file write, no remote E2E, and read-only probe executed.
  - **Redaction rules selected:** future paste-back must never include private keys, passphrases, tokens, passwords, credentials, production endpoints, backup material, private host material, full `ssh -G` output, full identityfile paths, unapproved hostnames/IPs, or long secret-like dumps. Future docs may contain only redacted or summarized hostnames, IPs, `$HOME`, `pwd`, group details, and identity paths while preserving fixed markers.
  - **Stop conditions selected:** future NA-0510 must stop on stale qwork proof, alias drift, host-key mismatch or ambiguity, wrong remote user, UID 0, privileged group membership, sudo success, missing or non-writable workdir, readable `/backup/qsl`, remote qwork present, remote qsl-backup present, any remote file write, any remote E2E attempt, private material in output, redaction ambiguity, or any public/security/completion overclaim.
  - **Remote write probe deferred:** NA-0509 authorizes read-only proof first. The marker-file create/read/delete probe is deferred to a later lane unless future evidence shows it is necessary.
  - **No remote action in this directive:** NA-0509 performs no remote action, no SSH execution, no scp/sftp/rsync execution to remote, no remote account creation, no remote test execution, and no remote host mutation.
  - **No SSH/key/config mutation:** NA-0509 performs no SSH key generation or installation, no local SSH config mutation, no system SSH config mutation, no known_hosts mutation, no authorized_keys mutation, and no ssh-keyscan or ssh-keygen action.
  - **No privileged/setup mutation:** NA-0509 performs no sudo/admin action, no package installation, no service installation, no persistent daemon setup, no qwork/qstart/qresume mutation or remote execution, and no qsl-backup execution or mutation.
  - **No implementation-code mutation:** NA-0509 changes no qsc source, no qsc test source, no qsc fuzz target, no qsc Cargo metadata, no root Cargo metadata, no dependency/lockfile, no workflow, no script/helper, no validator, no corpus/vector/input/internal-manifest, no formal model, no refimpl, no qsl-server, no qsl-attachments, no qshield runtime, no qshield-cli, no public docs, no backup plan/status, no qsl-backup, no rollback/archive, and no `/backup/qsl` path.
  - **No remote E2E:** NA-0509 does not authorize remote qsc send/receive, remote source checkout/build, remote daemon/service setup, remote write-marker proof, or remote client-to-client E2E.
  - **Public claim boundary:** no public-readiness claim is made. no production-readiness claim is made. no public-internet-readiness claim is made. no external-review-complete claim is made. no crypto-complete claim is made. no replay-proof claim is made. no downgrade-proof claim is made. no secret-material-complete claim is made. no side-channel-free claim is made. no vulnerability-free claim is made. no bug-free claim is made. no perfect-crypto claim is made.
  - **Exactly one READY remains mandatory:** NA-0509 remains READY until a separate closeout marks it DONE and restores the selected NA-0510 successor.
  - **Required markers:** `NA0509_QWORK_PROOF_FILES_VERIFIED_OK`; `NA0509_D397_INHERITANCE_CONSUMED_OK`; `NA0509_OPERATOR_PROOF_BOUNDARY_RESTATED_OK`; `NA0509_REMOTE_PROBE_SCOPE_DESIGNED_OK`; `NA0509_EXACT_FUTURE_COMMANDS_SELECTED_OK`; `NA0509_READ_ONLY_PROBE_SELECTED_OK`; `NA0509_REMOTE_WRITE_PROBE_DEFERRED_OK`; `NA0509_NO_REMOTE_E2E_OK`; `NA0509_REDACTION_RULES_SELECTED_OK`; `NA0509_STOP_CONDITIONS_SELECTED_OK`; `NA0509_NO_REMOTE_ACTION_BY_CODEX_OK`; `NA0509_NO_SSH_EXECUTION_BY_CODEX_OK`; `NA0509_NO_SSH_KEY_GENERATION_OK`; `NA0509_NO_SSH_CONFIG_MUTATION_OK`; `NA0509_NO_QSC_IMPLEMENTATION_MUTATION_OK`; `NA0509_NO_WORKFLOW_DEPENDENCY_MUTATION_OK`; `NA0509_NO_CORPUS_VECTOR_INPUT_MUTATION_OK`; `NA0509_NO_PUBLIC_READINESS_CLAIM_OK`; `NA0509_NO_PRODUCTION_READINESS_CLAIM_OK`; `NA0509_ONE_READY_INVARIANT_OK`.
  - **Required behavior:** future closeout must preserve authorization-only evidence, one-READY invariant, no remote action by Codex in NA-0509, exact NA-0510 read-only successor scope, no key/config/host mutation by Codex, no qwork/qsl-backup exposure, and no public/security/completion claim expansion.
  - **Must never happen:** NA-0509 is represented as remote E2E, remote account creation, SSH key generation or installation, SSH execution, scp/sftp/rsync execution, local or system SSH config mutation, known_hosts mutation, authorized_keys mutation, remote host mutation, sudo/admin action, package installation, remote test execution, qwork/qstart/qresume mutation, qsl-backup execution or mutation, qsc source/test/fuzz/Cargo mutation, workflow/script/helper mutation, dependency/lockfile mutation, corpus/vector/input/internal-manifest mutation, formal/refimpl/service/public/qshield/qsl-server/qsl-attachments mutation, backup/restore/qsl-backup mutation, or any forbidden public/security/completion claim.

- **ID:** D-1009
  - **Title:** NA-0510 remote host read-only capability probe implementation
  - **Status:** Accepted
  - **Date:** 2026-06-20
  - **Goals:** G1, G2, G3, G4, G5
  - **Decision:** NA-0510 consumes NA-0509/D398 inheritance, verifies fresh qwork proof without rerunning qwork, reviews local `ssh -G inspiron` effective-config proof, executes exactly one bounded read-only SSH probe to `inspiron` as `qslcodex`, redacts the output, and classifies the result as `REMOTE_READ_ONLY_PROBE_PASS`. The selected successor is `NA-0511 -- QSL Remote Host Read/Write Marker and Toolchain/Disk Capability Probe Scope Authorization Plan`.
  - **NA-0509/D398 consumed:** NA-0509 is DONE, NA-0510 is READY, D-1007 records the read-only probe authorization, D-1008 records NA-0509 closeout and NA-0510 restoration, and D398 exists at `/home/victor/work/qsl/codex/responses/NA0509_20260620T223434Z_D398.md`.
  - **Local SSH config proof reviewed:** parsed `ssh -G inspiron` safe fields showed `hostname=inspiron`, `user=qslcodex`, identity basename `qslcodex_ed25519`, `identitiesonly=yes`, `passwordauthentication=no`, `batchmode=yes`, strict host-key checking enabled, no agent forwarding, no X11 forwarding, and clear forwarding enabled. Codex did not run `ssh -G remote`.
  - **Remote probe executed or failed with classification:** exactly one authorized remote SSH invocation was executed: `ssh -o BatchMode=yes -o PasswordAuthentication=no -o ConnectTimeout=10 inspiron 'bash -s'`. The probe exited 0, emitted no STOP markers, and selected `REMOTE_READ_ONLY_PROBE_PASS`.
  - **Remote account checked:** the remote probe emitted `NA0510_REMOTE_ACCOUNT_QSLCODEX_OK` and remote user `qslcodex`.
  - **Non-root checked:** the remote probe emitted `NA0510_REMOTE_NOT_ROOT_OK` and remote UID `1003`.
  - **No sudo checked:** the only sudo action was the authorized negative `sudo -n true` check, which did not succeed and emitted `NA0510_REMOTE_NO_SUDO_OK`.
  - **Workdir existence/writability checked:** read-only `test -d "$HOME/qsl-remote-test"` and `test -w "$HOME/qsl-remote-test"` emitted `NA0510_REMOTE_WORKDIR_EXISTS_OK` and `NA0510_REMOTE_WORKDIR_WRITABLE_OK`.
  - **No backup exposure checked:** read-only `/backup/qsl` existence/readability checks emitted `NA0510_REMOTE_NO_BACKUP_EXPOSURE_OK absent`.
  - **qwork absence checked:** `command -v qwork` emitted `NA0510_REMOTE_QWORK_ABSENT_OK`; qwork was not run locally or remotely.
  - **qsl-backup absence checked:** `command -v qsl-backup` emitted `NA0510_REMOTE_QSL_BACKUP_ABSENT_OK`; backup, restore, and qsl-backup were not run locally or remotely.
  - **No remote E2E:** the probe emitted `NA0510_NO_REMOTE_E2E_OK`; NA-0510 did not run qsc remotely, qsc send/receive, remote source checkout/build, service actions, or protocol execution.
  - **No remote file write:** the probe emitted `NA0510_NO_REMOTE_FILE_WRITE_OK`; NA-0510 did not create, write, read/delete a marker, delete, upload, or synchronize remote files.
  - **No SSH/key/config mutation:** NA-0510 performed no ssh-keygen, no ssh-keyscan, no SSH key generation or installation, no local/system SSH config mutation, no known_hosts mutation, no authorized_keys mutation, and no remote host mutation.
  - **No implementation-code mutation:** NA-0510 changes no qsc source, no qsc test source, no qsc fuzz target, no qsc Cargo metadata, no root Cargo metadata, no dependency/lockfile, no workflow, no script/helper, no validator, no corpus/vector/input/internal-manifest, no formal model, no refimpl, no qsl-server, no qsl-attachments, no qshield runtime, no qshield-cli, no public docs, no backup plan/status, no qsl-backup, no rollback/archive, and no `/backup/qsl` path.
  - **Public claim boundary:** no public-readiness claim is made. no production-readiness claim is made. no public-internet-readiness claim is made. no external-review-complete claim is made. no crypto-complete claim is made. no replay-proof claim is made. no downgrade-proof claim is made. no secret-material-complete claim is made. no side-channel-free claim is made. no vulnerability-free claim is made. no bug-free claim is made. no perfect-crypto claim is made.

- **ID:** D-1011
  - **Title:** NA-0511 remote read/write marker and toolchain disk capability probe scope authorization
  - **Status:** Accepted
  - **Date:** 2026-06-21
  - **Goals:** G1, G2, G3, G4, G5
  - **Decision:** NA-0511 consumes NA-0510 / D401 inheritance, accepts the NA-0510 read-only remote probe classification `REMOTE_READ_ONLY_PROBE_PASS`, completes the time-sensitive remote assurance review, selects future marker write/read/delete scope plus future toolchain/disk scope, selects the exact future command family, redaction rules, expected outputs, and stop conditions, and classifies the selected successor as `REMOTE_MARKER_TOOLCHAIN_DISK_PROBE_IMPLEMENTATION_READY`. The selected successor is `NA-0512 -- QSL Remote Host Read/Write Marker and Toolchain/Disk Capability Probe Implementation Harness`.
  - **NA-0510/D401 consumed:** PR #1292 merged at `bff96ccc1fe7`; D401 closeout PR #1293 merged at `451d596e8f94`; NA-0510 is DONE; NA-0511 is READY; D-1009 and D-1010 exist once; D400 records exactly one bounded SSH invocation in NA-0510 implementation and no SSH invocation in D401 closeout.
  - **Read-only remote probe accepted:** the account proof for `qslcodex` passed, non-root proof passed, no-sudo proof passed, workdir existence/writability proof passed using `test -w`, backup exposure proof passed, qwork absence proof passed, qsl-backup absence proof passed, no remote E2E ran, no remote file write occurred, no marker write/read/delete occurred, and no remote qsc/git/cargo/rustc/qsc/toolchain probe occurred.
  - **Marker scope selected:** future NA-0512 may create exactly one synthetic marker file under existing `$HOME/qsl-remote-test`, using marker string `QSL_REMOTE_MARKER_SYNTHETIC_NA0512` and path pattern `$HOME/qsl-remote-test/na0512_marker_${PROOF_ID}.txt`; it must stop if the path exists, read back exact content, delete the marker, and verify absence after deletion.
  - **Toolchain/disk scope selected:** future NA-0512 may run only read-only presence/version/capacity commands for git, cargo, rustc, qsc presence, uname, and `df -h` on `$HOME` and `$HOME/qsl-remote-test`; qsc help/version may run only if a future pre-check proves the chosen command is non-mutating, otherwise qsc presence only is recorded.
  - **Exact future command family selected:** future NA-0512 may parse safe `ssh -G inspiron` fields locally, prove optional alias `remote` remains absent unless later approved, and run one bounded SSH invocation `ssh -o BatchMode=yes -o PasswordAuthentication=no -o ConnectTimeout=10 inspiron 'bash -s'` with the remote script limited to identity, privilege, workdir, backup, qwork/qsl-backup, toolchain/disk, one marker lifecycle, and fixed marker prints.
  - **Redaction rules selected:** future evidence must include no private key, passphrase, token, password, production endpoint, or backup material; must avoid full `ssh -G`, known_hosts, authorized_keys, unrelated environment, and unrelated directory listings; must keep raw proof under the proof root and final summaries sanitized.
  - **Expected outputs selected:** future NA-0512 markers include account, non-root, no-sudo, workdir exists/writable, marker write/read/delete/absent-after-delete, no backup exposure, qwork absent, qsl-backup absent, toolchain status captured, disk status captured, no remote E2E, no remote source build, no package install, and probe done markers; toolchain absence is non-fatal.
  - **Stop conditions selected:** future NA-0512 must stop on alias/host-key/connectivity drift, wrong user, UID 0, privileged group, sudo success, readable `/backup/qsl`, qwork or qsl-backup presence, missing/unwritable workdir, existing marker path, marker write/read/delete failure, marker residue, sensitive output, need for sudo/admin, package installation, source checkout/build, remote E2E, mutation outside the marker, production/user-data sensitivity, or public/production readiness claim pressure.
  - **No remote action in this directive:** NA-0511 performs no remote action, no SSH execution, no marker write/read/delete, no remote toolchain command, no remote E2E, no scp/sftp/rsync, no ssh-keygen, no ssh-keyscan, no account creation, no SSH key generation or installation, no SSH config mutation, no known_hosts mutation, no authorized_keys mutation, no remote host mutation, no sudo/admin action, no package installation, no qwork/qstart/qresume execution or mutation, and no qsl-backup execution or mutation.
  - **No implementation-code mutation:** NA-0511 changes no qsc source/test/fuzz/Cargo, workflow/script/helper, dependency/lockfile, corpus/vector/input, formal model, refimpl, qsl-server, qsl-attachments, qshield runtime, qshield-cli, service, public docs, README, START_HERE, website, backup, qsl-backup, backup status, backup plan, rollback, archive, move, delete, or `/backup/qsl` path.
  - **No remote E2E:** future NA-0512 remains marker/toolchain/disk capability only. Remote E2E remains deferred until marker write/read/delete passes, toolchain/disk evidence is available, staging/build/smoke strategy is authorized or toolchain absence is addressed, and a separate remote E2E authorization lane selects exact protocol flow.
  - **Public claim boundary:** no public-readiness claim is made. no production-readiness claim is made. no public-internet-readiness claim is made. no external-review-complete claim is made. no crypto-complete claim is made. no replay-proof claim is made. no downgrade-proof claim is made. no secret-material-complete claim is made. no side-channel-free claim is made. no vulnerability-free claim is made. no bug-free claim is made. no perfect-crypto claim is made.
  - **Exactly one READY remains mandatory:** NA-0511 remains READY until a separate closeout marks it DONE and restores the selected NA-0512 successor.
  - **Required markers:** `NA0511_QWORK_PROOF_FILES_VERIFIED_OK`; `NA0511_D401_INHERITANCE_CONSUMED_OK`; `NA0511_TIME_SENSITIVE_REMOTE_ASSURANCE_REVIEW_OK`; `NA0511_MARKER_SCOPE_SELECTED_OK`; `NA0511_TOOLCHAIN_DISK_SCOPE_SELECTED_OK`; `NA0511_EXACT_FUTURE_COMMANDS_SELECTED_OK`; `NA0511_EXPECTED_OUTPUTS_SELECTED_OK`; `NA0511_REDACTION_RULES_SELECTED_OK`; `NA0511_STOP_CONDITIONS_SELECTED_OK`; `NA0511_NO_REMOTE_E2E_BOUNDARY_OK`; `NA0511_STEWARDSHIP_REVIEWS_COMPLETED_OK`; `NA0511_PRIORITY_MATRIX_COMPLETED_OK`; `NA0511_REMOTE_MARKER_TOOLCHAIN_DISK_PROBE_IMPLEMENTATION_READY`; `NA0511_SELECTED_NA0512_SUCCESSOR_OK`; `NA0511_NO_REMOTE_ACTION_BY_CODEX_OK`; `NA0511_NO_SSH_EXECUTION_BY_CODEX_OK`; `NA0511_NO_MARKER_WRITE_READ_DELETE_BY_CODEX_OK`; `NA0511_NO_QSC_IMPLEMENTATION_MUTATION_OK`; `NA0511_NO_PUBLIC_READINESS_CLAIM_OK`; `NA0511_NO_PRODUCTION_READINESS_CLAIM_OK`; `NA0511_ONE_READY_INVARIANT_OK`.
  - **Required behavior:** future closeout must preserve authorization-only evidence, one-READY invariant, no remote action by Codex in NA-0511, exact NA-0512 combined marker/toolchain/disk successor scope, and no public/security/completion claim expansion.

- **ID:** D-1013
  - **Title:** NA-0512 remote read/write marker and toolchain disk capability probe implementation
  - **Status:** Accepted
  - **Date:** 2026-06-21
  - **Goals:** G1, G2, G3, G4, G5
  - **Decision:** NA-0512 consumes NA-0511 / D403 inheritance, verifies fresh qwork proof without rerunning qwork, reviews local `ssh -G inspiron` effective-config proof, executes exactly one bounded SSH marker/toolchain/disk probe to the authorized remote alias as `qslcodex`, redacts the output, and classifies the result as `REMOTE_MARKER_PROBE_PASS_TOOLCHAIN_ABSENT`. The selected successor is `NA-0513 -- QSL Remote qsc Staging Strategy Authorization Plan`.
  - **NA-0511/D403 consumed:** NA-0511 is DONE, NA-0512 is READY, D-1011 and D-1012 exist once, D402 selected `REMOTE_MARKER_TOOLCHAIN_DISK_PROBE_IMPLEMENTATION_READY`, D403 recovered PR #1294 missing required checks via one close/reopen and no empty commit, PR #1294 merged at `5f27d289e088`, D403 closeout PR #1295 merged at `6b18574cbf16`, and no remote action occurred in NA-0511 or D403.
  - **Probe result classification:** `REMOTE_MARKER_PROBE_PASS_TOOLCHAIN_ABSENT`.
  - **Bounded SSH command:** exactly one bounded SSH command was executed. No second SSH invocation, scp, sftp, rsync, ssh-keygen, or ssh-keyscan was executed.
  - **Marker write/read/delete result:** one synthetic marker under `$HOME/qsl-remote-test` was written with `QSL_REMOTE_MARKER_SYNTHETIC_NA0512`, read back exactly, and deleted.
  - **Marker deletion result:** marker absence after deletion was verified with `NA0512_REMOTE_MARKER_ABSENT_AFTER_DELETE_OK`; no marker residue was accepted.
  - **Toolchain/disk capture result:** remote toolchain capture completed and found `git`, `cargo`, `rustc`, and `qsc` absent; remote disk capture completed for `$HOME` and `$HOME/qsl-remote-test`.
  - **Account and privilege checks:** account `qslcodex` was checked, non-root was checked, no privileged group was checked, and no sudo was checked using only the authorized negative `sudo -n true` probe.
  - **Workdir checks:** existing `$HOME/qsl-remote-test` existence and writability were checked before the marker lifecycle.
  - **Boundary checks:** no backup exposure was checked, qwork absence was checked, and qsl-backup absence was checked. qwork, qstart, qresume, backup, restore, and qsl-backup were not run locally or remotely.
  - **No remote E2E/source build/package install:** no remote E2E, no qsc protocol send/receive, no qsc key generation, no remote source checkout/build/update, no remote cargo build/test/update, no rustup install/update, and no package installation occurred.
  - **No SSH/key/config mutation:** no ssh-keygen, no ssh-keyscan, no SSH key generation, no SSH key installation, no local/system SSH config mutation, no known_hosts mutation, and no authorized_keys mutation occurred.
  - **No remote host mutation outside marker lifecycle:** no remote account creation, no service/daemon action, no directory creation, no setup mutation, and no remote host mutation occurred outside the one marker file lifecycle.
  - **No implementation-code mutation:** NA-0512 changes no qsc source/test/fuzz/Cargo, workflow/script/helper, dependency/lockfile, corpus/vector/input, formal model, refimpl, qsl-server, qsl-attachments, qshield runtime, qshield-cli, service, public docs, README, START_HERE, website, backup, qsl-backup, backup status, backup plan, rollback, archive, move, delete, or `/backup/qsl` path.

  - **Exactly one READY remains mandatory:** NA-0512 remains READY until a separate closeout marks it DONE and restores the selected NA-0513 successor.
  - **Required markers:** `NA0512_REMOTE_SCOPE_CONSUMED_OK`; `NA0512_REMOTE_MARKER_TOOLCHAIN_DISK_PROBE_EXECUTED_OK`; `NA0512_REMOTE_ACCOUNT_QSLCODEX_OK`; `NA0512_REMOTE_NOT_ROOT_OK`; `NA0512_REMOTE_NO_SUDO_OK`; `NA0512_REMOTE_WORKDIR_EXISTS_OK`; `NA0512_REMOTE_WORKDIR_WRITABLE_OK`; `NA0512_REMOTE_MARKER_WRITE_OK`; `NA0512_REMOTE_MARKER_READ_OK`; `NA0512_REMOTE_MARKER_DELETE_OK`; `NA0512_REMOTE_MARKER_ABSENT_AFTER_DELETE_OK`; `NA0512_REMOTE_NO_BACKUP_EXPOSURE_OK`; `NA0512_REMOTE_QWORK_ABSENT_OK`; `NA0512_REMOTE_QSL_BACKUP_ABSENT_OK`; `NA0512_REMOTE_TOOLCHAIN_STATUS_CAPTURED_OK`; `NA0512_REMOTE_DISK_STATUS_CAPTURED_OK`; `NA0512_NO_REMOTE_E2E_OK`; `NA0512_NO_REMOTE_SOURCE_BUILD_OK`; `NA0512_NO_PACKAGE_INSTALL_OK`; `NA0512_NO_PUBLIC_READINESS_CLAIM_OK`; `NA0512_NO_PRODUCTION_READINESS_CLAIM_OK`; `NA0512_ONE_READY_INVARIANT_OK`.

- **ID:** D-1015
  - **Title:** NA-0513 remote qsc staging strategy authorization
  - **Status:** Accepted
  - **Date:** 2026-06-21
  - **Goals:** G1, G2, G3, G4, G5
  - **Decision:** NA-0513 triages the inherited D404 red remote checks, consumes NA-0512/D404 evidence, and authorizes `REMOTE_PREBUILT_QSC_BINARY_STAGING_SMOKE_READY` as the next safe strategy. Future NA-0514 should stage a locally built qsc binary under `$HOME/qsl-remote-test/bin/qsc`, verify local/remote sha256, run a bounded non-protocol qsc `--help` or `--version` smoke, and retain the binary only if path/owner/hash/cleanup/no-backup proof passes. Remote E2E remains deferred.
  - **Packet A red check triage completed:** `remote-handshake`, `remote-relay`, and `relay-ui-integration` on closeout merge `72994b2882e7` completed failure, but were classified as `REMOTE_CHECK_NONREQUIRED_REMOTE_RESIDUAL`. They are scheduled non-required workflows, not required branch-protection contexts, not public-safety aggregate inputs, not NA-0512 closeout acceptance requirements, and not treated as passed or public-readiness evidence.
  - **D404 closeout public-safety consumed:** `public-safety` for `72994b2882e7` completed success. Required branch-protection contexts exclude the three red scheduled remote checks.
  - **NA-0512/D404 consumed:** NA-0512 completed, NA-0513 was restored READY, D-1013 and D-1014 exist once, D-1015 was absent before this decision, and duplicate decision count was zero.
  - **Probe evidence accepted:** marker probe passed; marker content was synthetic; marker write/read/delete/absent-after-delete passed; remote `git`, `cargo`, `rustc`, and `qsc` were absent; toolchain/qsc absence is staging-strategy evidence.
  - **Staging options reviewed:** prebuilt binary transfer plus remote smoke with retention was selected; cleanup-after-smoke remains fallback; operator-managed toolchain setup, remote source checkout/build, pre-existing remote qsc smoke, remote E2E now, same-host E2E expansion, remote setup remediation, and CI/tooling lane were rejected or deferred as recorded in the NA-0513 evidence.
  - **Exact future command family selected:** future NA-0514 may run local `cargo build -p qsc --locked`, local hash/size/help proof, one bounded transfer to `$HOME/qsl-remote-test/bin/qsc`, optional bounded directory creation under `$HOME/qsl-remote-test/bin`, and one bounded remote `qsc --help` or `--version` smoke with remote hash verification. Future NA-0514 must not run qsc send/receive, remote E2E, remote source checkout/build, package installation, qwork/qstart/qresume, or qsl-backup.
  - **Retention/cleanup policy selected:** prefer retaining the staged binary only if smoke passes and proof records qsl-remote-test path, owner `qslcodex`, sha256, size, cleanup command, no production data, and no backup exposure. Otherwise select cleanup-after-smoke.
  - **Redaction/stop rules selected:** future proof must include no private key, passphrase, token, password, production endpoint, or backup material; raw logs remain proof-root-local; checked-in evidence redacts host/IP/disk topology. Stop on local build failure, ambiguous binary, local smoke failure, transfer failure, remote hash mismatch, remote qsc smoke failure, secret-looking output, backup exposure, qwork/qsl-backup presence, sudo success, package-install need, remote source-build need, remote E2E need, or public/production readiness claim pressure.
  - **Selected successor:** `NA-0514 -- QSL Remote qsc Prebuilt Binary Staging and Smoke Implementation Harness`.
  - **No remote action in this directive:** no SSH execution, no scp/sftp/rsync, no binary transfer, no remote command, no remote E2E, no remote qsc execution, no package install, no remote source checkout/build, no sudo/admin action, no key generation or installation, no SSH config mutation, no known_hosts mutation, no remote host mutation, no qwork/qstart/qresume mutation or execution, and no qsl-backup execution occurred in NA-0513.
  - **No implementation mutation:** NA-0513 changes no qsc source/test/fuzz/Cargo, no workflow/script/helper, no corpus/vector/input, no dependency/lockfile, no formal/refimpl/service/public/backup path, no qsl-server/qsl-attachments/qshield/qshield-cli path, no website/README/START_HERE path, and no backup path.
  - **Public claim boundary:** no public-readiness claim is made. no production-readiness claim is made. no public-internet-readiness claim is made. no external-review-complete claim is made. no crypto-complete claim is made. no replay-proof claim is made. no downgrade-proof claim is made. no secret-material-complete claim is made. no side-channel-free claim is made. no vulnerability-free claim is made. no bug-free claim is made. no perfect-crypto claim is made.
  - **Exactly one READY remains mandatory:** NA-0513 remains READY until a separate closeout marks it DONE and restores the selected NA-0514 successor.
  - **References:** NA-0513; selected NA-0514; D-1015; D-1014; D-1013; PR #1297; closeout merge `72994b2882e7`; D404 response `/home/victor/work/qsl/codex/responses/NA0512_20260621T051441Z_D404.md`; D405 response `/home/victor/work/qsl/codex/responses/NA0513_20260621T133821Z_D405.md`; `docs/governance/evidence/NA-0513_qsl_remote_qsc_staging_strategy_authorization_plan.md`; `tests/NA-0513_qsl_remote_qsc_staging_strategy_authorization_testplan.md`; `docs/governance/evidence/NA-0512_qsl_remote_host_read_write_marker_toolchain_disk_capability_probe_implementation_harness.md`; `tests/NA-0512_qsl_remote_host_read_write_marker_toolchain_disk_capability_probe_implementation_testplan.md`; `NEXT_ACTIONS.md`; `TRACEABILITY.md`; `docs/ops/ROLLING_OPERATIONS_JOURNAL.md`

- **ID:** D-1017
  - **Title:** NA-0514 remote qsc prebuilt binary staging and smoke implementation
  - **Status:** Accepted
  - **Date:** 2026-06-21
  - **Goals:** G1, G2, G3, G4, G5
  - **Decision:** NA-0514 consumes NA-0513 / D406 inheritance, builds the local `qsc` binary from clean qsl-protocol source commit `6e0796de79c9`, records local qsc binary sha256 `6f12ab5eec2468a4146012dfc247cef15be2337cb8e8e99cefa96a8c258d91ea`, stages the binary to the approved Inspiron `qslcodex` qsl-remote-test binary path, verifies the remote stage and final hashes match the local hash, runs one bounded non-protocol `--help` smoke, retains the final binary with cleanup instructions, and classifies the result as `REMOTE_PREBUILT_QSC_STAGING_SMOKE_PASS_RETAINED`.
  - **NA-0513/D406 consumed:** NA-0513 completed, NA-0514 was restored READY, D406 triaged `remote-handshake`, `remote-relay`, and `relay-ui-integration` as non-required scheduled remote residuals, NA-0512 classification `REMOTE_MARKER_PROBE_PASS_TOOLCHAIN_ABSENT` was consumed, and remote `git`, `cargo`, `rustc`, and `qsc` absence was accepted as the reason for prebuilt staging instead of remote build.
  - **Local qsc binary proof:** local source commit `6e0796de79c9`; local build command `cargo build -p qsc --locked --bin qsc`; binary size `102103920` bytes; binary sha256 `6f12ab5eec2468a4146012dfc247cef15be2337cb8e8e99cefa96a8c258d91ea`; local `--help` smoke exit code 0.
  - **Remote staging proof:** exactly one bounded SSH prep invocation checked account, privilege, backup, qwork, qsl-backup, workdir, final-path, and stage-path boundaries; exactly one scp transfer staged the binary under qsl-remote-test; exactly one bounded SSH verify/smoke invocation checked the stage file, matched remote stage hash to local hash, chmodded only the stage path, ran `--help`, moved stage to final, verified stage absence, verified final hash, and verified owner `qslcodex`.
  - **Remote smoke result:** remote qsc `--help` exit code 0. No remote qsc send/receive, no remote E2E, no qsc key generation, no protocol flow, no source checkout/build, and no package installation occurred.
  - **Retention/cleanup decision:** final qsc binary is retained under qsl-remote-test for near-term E2EE authorization because hash, owner, path, smoke, no-backup, qwork absence, qsl-backup absence, and cleanup proof passed. Cleanup command: `ssh -o BatchMode=yes -o PasswordAuthentication=no -o ConnectTimeout=10 inspiron 'rm -f -- "$HOME/qsl-remote-test/bin/qsc"'`.
  - **No remote E2E:** NA-0514 is staging/smoke evidence only and does not execute remote client-to-client E2EE, qsc send, qsc receive, handshake, relay, or protocol state commands.
  - **No remote source checkout/build:** no remote git clone/fetch/pull, cargo build/test/update, rustup install/update, or source build occurred.
  - **No package install or sudo/admin:** no package installation occurred and no sudo/admin action occurred beyond the negative `sudo -n true` boundary check.
  - **No SSH/key/config mutation:** no ssh-keygen, no ssh-keyscan, no SSH key generation, no SSH key installation, no local/system SSH config mutation, no known_hosts mutation, and no authorized_keys mutation occurred.
  - **No remote host mutation outside staged artifact:** remote mutation was limited to qsl-remote-test bin directory readiness, the staged qsc file, chmod on the staged qsc file, and final retained qsc artifact. No remote account, service, system path, backup path, package, toolchain, or source checkout/build mutation occurred.
  - **No qwork/qsl-backup mutation:** qwork, qstart, qresume, backup, restore, and qsl-backup were not run locally or remotely.
  - **No implementation-code mutation:** NA-0514 changes no qsc source/test/fuzz/Cargo, no workflow/script/helper, no corpus/vector/input, no dependency/lockfile, no formal/refimpl/service/public/backup path, no qsl-server/qsl-attachments/qshield/qshield-cli path, no website/README/START_HERE path, and no backup path.
  - **Public claim boundary:** no public-readiness claim is made. no production-readiness claim is made. no public-internet-readiness claim is made. no external-review-complete claim is made. no crypto-complete claim is made. no replay-proof claim is made. no downgrade-proof claim is made. no secret-material-complete claim is made. no side-channel-free claim is made. no vulnerability-free claim is made. no bug-free claim is made. no perfect-crypto claim is made.
  - **Selected successor:** `NA-0515 -- QSL Build-to-Inspiron Remote qsc Client-to-Client E2EE Scope Authorization Plan`.
  - **Exactly one READY remains mandatory:** NA-0514 remains READY until a separate closeout marks it DONE and restores the selected NA-0515 successor.
  - **Required markers:** `NA0514_REMOTE_STAGING_SCOPE_CONSUMED_OK`; `NA0514_LOCAL_QSC_BINARY_BUILT_OR_SELECTED_OK`; `NA0514_LOCAL_QSC_BINARY_HASH_RECORDED_OK`; `NA0514_LOCAL_QSC_SMOKE_OK`; `NA0514_REMOTE_PREP_BOUNDARY_OK`; `NA0514_REMOTE_QSC_BINARY_STAGED_OK`; `NA0514_REMOTE_QSC_BINARY_HASH_MATCH_OK`; `NA0514_REMOTE_QSC_SMOKE_OK`; `NA0514_REMOTE_QSC_RETENTION_DECISION_OK`; `NA0514_NO_REMOTE_E2E_OK`; `NA0514_NO_REMOTE_SOURCE_BUILD_OK`; `NA0514_NO_PACKAGE_INSTALL_OK`; `NA0514_NO_SUDO_ADMIN_OK`; `NA0514_NO_BACKUP_EXPOSURE_OK`; `NA0514_NO_QWORK_QSLBACKUP_OK`; `NA0514_NO_PUBLIC_READINESS_CLAIM_OK`; `NA0514_NO_PRODUCTION_READINESS_CLAIM_OK`; `NA0514_ONE_READY_INVARIANT_OK`.
  - **Required behavior:** future closeout must preserve the staging/smoke result classification, one-READY invariant, retained-binary cleanup command, exact NA-0515 authorization-only successor scope, and no public/security/completion claim expansion.
  - **Must never happen:** NA-0514 is represented as remote E2E, remote qsc send/receive, remote protocol correctness proof, public readiness, production readiness, public-internet readiness, crypto completeness, replay proof, downgrade proof, vulnerability-free proof, bug-free proof, perfect-crypto proof, remote source checkout/build, remote package installation, remote account creation, SSH key generation or installation, ssh-keygen, ssh-keyscan, local or system SSH config mutation, known_hosts mutation, authorized_keys mutation, remote host mutation outside the staged artifact, qwork/qstart/qresume mutation or execution, qsl-backup execution or mutation, qsc source/test/fuzz/Cargo mutation, workflow/script/helper mutation, dependency/lockfile mutation, corpus/vector/input/internal-manifest mutation, formal/refimpl/service/public/qshield/qsl-server/qsl-attachments mutation, or backup/restore/qsl-backup mutation.
  - **References:** NA-0514; selected NA-0515; D-1017; D-1016; D-1015; D406 response `/home/victor/work/qsl/codex/responses/NA0513_20260621T142803Z_D406.md`; D404 response `/home/victor/work/qsl/codex/responses/NA0512_20260621T051441Z_D404.md`; `docs/governance/evidence/NA-0514_qsl_remote_qsc_prebuilt_binary_staging_smoke_implementation_harness.md`; `tests/NA-0514_qsl_remote_qsc_prebuilt_binary_staging_smoke_implementation_testplan.md`; `docs/governance/evidence/NA-0513_qsl_remote_qsc_staging_strategy_authorization_plan.md`; `tests/NA-0513_qsl_remote_qsc_staging_strategy_authorization_testplan.md`; `docs/governance/evidence/NA-0512_qsl_remote_host_read_write_marker_toolchain_disk_capability_probe_implementation_harness.md`; `tests/NA-0512_qsl_remote_host_read_write_marker_toolchain_disk_capability_probe_implementation_testplan.md`; `NEXT_ACTIONS.md`; `TRACEABILITY.md`; `docs/ops/ROLLING_OPERATIONS_JOURNAL.md`

- **ID:** D-1019
  - **Title:** NA-0515 Build-to-Inspiron remote qsc client-to-client E2EE scope authorization
  - **Status:** Accepted
  - **Date:** 2026-06-21
  - **Goals:** G1, G2, G3, G4, G5
  - **Decision:** NA-0515 consumes NA-0514 / D407 inheritance, reviews the retained Inspiron qsc binary hash/path/owner/provenance evidence, reviews local/remote E2EE options, selects the exact future Build-to-Inspiron qsc client-to-client E2EE implementation scope at command-family level, selects synthetic-message-only proof policy, selects redaction and stop rules, selects cleanup/retention policy, and classifies the next lane as `REMOTE_BUILD_TO_INSPIRON_E2EE_IMPLEMENTATION_READY`.
  - **NA-0514/D407 consumed:** NA-0514 completed, NA-0515 was restored READY, classification `REMOTE_PREBUILT_QSC_STAGING_SMOKE_PASS_RETAINED` was accepted for authorization, implementation PR #1300 merged at `9567f845c1c5`, closeout PR #1301 merged at `6e12e1849737`, and D407 response `/home/victor/work/qsl/codex/responses/NA0514_20260621T152257Z_D407.md` was read.
  - **Retained binary reviewed:** local source commit `6e0796de79c9abb4d3c5e18b46b004b5bd585167`, retained remote qsc sha256 `6f12ab5eec2468a4146012dfc247cef15be2337cb8e8e99cefa96a8c258d91ea`, qsl-remote-test-relative path `qsl-remote-test/bin/qsc`, owner `qslcodex`, non-protocol `--help` smoke exit code 0, and cleanup command were reviewed. The retained binary is accepted only as input to NA-0516 boundary rechecks, not as permission to skip hash/path/owner/provenance verification.
  - **Options reviewed:** full Build-to-Inspiron E2EE implementation next is selected; identity/trust-only smoke, retained-binary re-smoke, relay/transport diagnostic, same-host negative expansion, remote cleanup/remediation, and CI/tooling lanes are deferred or conditional; operator toolchain setup and NA-0515 remote E2EE implementation are rejected.
  - **Exact future command family selected:** future NA-0516 may use local qsc commands, bounded SSH commands to `inspiron` as `qslcodex`, bounded transfer of synthetic public/trust/message artifacts, retained remote qsc at `$HOME/qsl-remote-test/bin/qsc`, isolated local and remote roots, observed qsc CLI surfaces from same-host tests, and an explicitly bounded loopback-only relay tunnel if needed. Future NA-0516 must recheck qwork freshness, remote account/privilege/backup/qwork/qsl-backup boundaries, retained qsc path/owner/hash, local qsc provenance, and retained qsc smoke before E2EE.
  - **Synthetic message policy selected:** future NA-0516 uses synthetic plaintext labels only, including Build-to-Inspiron and Inspiron-to-Build labels derived from the proof ID. No production data, personal data, private keys, passphrases, tokens, passwords, credentials, backup material, or private material may be transferred or stored in checked-in evidence.
  - **Redaction/stop rules selected:** raw output remains proof-root-local; checked-in evidence summarizes commands, markers, synthetic labels, hash/path checks, send/receive/reply results, negative boundary result, and cleanup/retention status. Stop on stale qwork proof, retained binary mismatch, remote boundary failure, local qsc ambiguity, need for sudo/admin, package installation, remote source checkout/build, secret-looking output, production or personal data involvement, send/receive/reply failure, unexpected negative-boundary mutation, cleanup ambiguity, or public/security/completion claim pressure.
  - **Cleanup/retention policy selected:** default cleanup removes the remote E2EE synthetic root under `$HOME/qsl-remote-test/e2ee/<PROOF_ID>` when safe. If retained for debugging, NA-0516 must record reason, retained path, owner expectation, and cleanup command. The retained qsc binary may remain unless a stop condition or cleanup decision says otherwise.
  - **Selected successor:** `NA-0516 -- QSL Build-to-Inspiron Remote qsc Client-to-Client E2EE Implementation Harness`, READY, Goals G1, G2, G3, G4, G5. Future NA-0516 deliverables are remote E2EE implementation evidence, testplan, its implementation decision as applicable, TRACEABILITY update, rolling journal update, and selected follow-up successor.
  - **No remote action in this directive:** no SSH execution, no scp/sftp/rsync, no remote command, no qsc send/receive, no remote E2EE, no binary transfer, no package install, no remote source checkout/build, no sudo/admin action, no SSH key generation or installation, no ssh-keygen, no ssh-keyscan, no local SSH config mutation, no known_hosts mutation, no remote host mutation, no qwork/qstart/qresume mutation or execution, and no qsl-backup execution occurred in NA-0515.
  - **No implementation mutation:** NA-0515 changes no qsc source/test/fuzz/Cargo path, no workflow/script/helper path, no corpus/vector/input path, no dependency/lockfile, no formal/refimpl/service/public/backup path, no qsl-server/qsl-attachments/qshield/qshield-cli path, no website/README/START_HERE path, and no backup path.
  - **Public claim boundary:** no public-readiness claim is made. no production-readiness claim is made. no public-internet-readiness claim is made. no external-review-complete claim is made. no crypto-complete claim is made. no replay-proof claim is made. no downgrade-proof claim is made. no secret-material-complete claim is made. no side-channel-free claim is made. no vulnerability-free claim is made. no bug-free claim is made. no perfect-crypto claim is made.
  - **Exactly one READY remains mandatory:** NA-0515 remains READY until a separate closeout marks it DONE and restores selected NA-0516 as the sole READY successor.
  - **Required markers:** `NA0515_QWORK_PROOF_FILES_VERIFIED_OK`; `NA0515_NA0514_D407_INHERITANCE_CONSUMED_OK`; `NA0515_RETAINED_REMOTE_QSC_REVIEWED_OK`; `NA0515_E2EE_OPTIONS_REVIEWED_OK`; `NA0515_EXACT_FUTURE_COMMAND_FAMILY_SELECTED_OK`; `NA0515_REDACTION_STOP_RULES_SELECTED_OK`; `NA0515_STEWARDSHIP_REVIEWS_COMPLETED_OK`; `NA0515_PRIORITY_MATRIX_COMPLETED_OK`; `NA0515_REMOTE_BUILD_TO_INSPIRON_E2EE_IMPLEMENTATION_READY`; `NA0515_SELECTED_NA0516_SUCCESSOR_OK`; `NA0515_NO_REMOTE_ACTION_BY_CODEX_OK`; `NA0515_NO_SSH_EXECUTION_BY_CODEX_OK`; `NA0515_NO_QSC_SEND_RECEIVE_OK`; `NA0515_NO_REMOTE_E2EE_OK`; `NA0515_NO_IMPLEMENTATION_MUTATION_OK`; `NA0515_NO_QSC_SOURCE_TEST_FUZZ_CARGO_MUTATION_OK`; `NA0515_NO_WORKFLOW_DEPENDENCY_MUTATION_OK`; `NA0515_NO_CORPUS_VECTOR_INPUT_MUTATION_OK`; `NA0515_NO_PUBLIC_READINESS_CLAIM_OK`; `NA0515_NO_PRODUCTION_READINESS_CLAIM_OK`; `NA0515_ONE_READY_INVARIANT_OK`.

D-1019 exists once, NA-0515 authorization evidence is in-tree, NA-0515 is marked DONE, and `NA-0516 -- QSL Build-to-Inspiron Remote qsc Client-to-Client E2EE Implementation Harness` is restored as the sole READY successor.

It may run only after fresh qwork proof, remote boundary rechecks, retained qsc hash/path/owner/provenance recheck, local qsc provenance recording, synthetic data setup, redacted proof capture, and cleanup/retention planning.
