- **ID:** D-1070
  - **Title:** NA-0540 daily public Progress update cadence and site-wide public accuracy sweep authorization
  - **Date:** 2026-06-25
  - **Status:** Accepted
  - **Goal IDs:** G1, G2, G3, G4, G5
  - **Decision:** NA-0540 consumes D452/D451 inheritance, confirms public evidence sync is complete, performs a read-only inventory and classification of existing public-facing repository surfaces, and authorizes the manual end-of-day public Progress cadence with a site-wide public accuracy sweep. Selected classification is `DAILY_PUBLIC_PROGRESS_SITE_ACCURACY_IMPLEMENTATION_READY`. The selected successor is `NA-0541 -- QSL Daily Public Progress Section, First End-of-Day Update, and Site-Wide Accuracy Sweep Implementation Harness`.
  - **Cadence selected:** One entry per active QSL workday, target publication approximately 21:30 America/Chicago, publish only after stable handoff, defer if work remains unstable, retain the America/Chicago workday date, skip inactive days unless the operator wants a concise no-public-evidence-change entry, and report stopped lanes only as truthful blockers without implying success.
  - **Stable handoff rule:** A stable handoff is a merged PR with accepted decision/check state or a truthful terminal STOP with clean repo and exact blocker/handoff. Speculative or in-flight updates are not authorized.
  - **Site-wide public-surface inventory:** NA-0540 classified 33 public-facing paths: `README.md`, `docs/README.md`, `docs/public/INDEX.md`, `docs/public/EXTERNAL_REVIEW_PACKAGE.md`, `docs/public/EXTERNAL_WEBSITE_IMPLEMENTATION_DIRECTIVE.md`, `docs/public/PUBLIC_ALLOWLIST_INVENTORY.md`, `docs/public/PUBLIC_ATTENTION_AND_VISIBILITY_STRATEGY.md`, `docs/public/PUBLIC_EXPORT_MANIFEST.md`, `docs/public/PUBLIC_RELEASE_RUNBOOK.md`, `docs/public/PUBLIC_WORKSPACE_AND_NAMING.md`, `docs/public/QSL_SERVER_ATTACHMENTS_PRODUCTION_BOUNDARY_PLAN.md`, `docs/public/RELEASE_READINESS_EVIDENCE_MAP.md`, `docs/public/SUITE2_TRIPLE_RATCHET_CLAIM_BOUNDARY.md`, `docs/public/WEBSITE_CLAIM_MATRIX.md`, `docs/public/WEBSITE_IMPLEMENTATION_HANDOFF.md`, `docs/public/WEBSITE_UPDATE_PLAN.md`, `docs/demo/ATTACHMENT_PUBLIC_DEMO_READINESS.md`, `docs/demo/CLEAN_HOST_REVIEWER_REPRODUCTION.md`, `docs/demo/CROSS_HOST_DEMO_STRESS_REPRODUCIBILITY.md`, `docs/demo/CROSS_HOST_PRIVATE_NETWORK_SOAK.md`, `docs/demo/CROSS_HOST_PUBLIC_DEMO_REPRODUCIBILITY.md`, `docs/demo/DEMO-PUBLIC-001_Metadata_Visibility.md`, `docs/demo/DEMO_ACCEPTANCE_CRITERIA.md`, `docs/demo/DEMO_ADVERSARIAL_STRESS_TESTING.md`, `docs/demo/DEMO_SOAK_REPEATED_RUN_STABILITY.md`, `docs/demo/DESKTOP_SIDECAR_ADVERSARIAL_STRESS.md`, `docs/demo/KT_NEGATIVE_PUBLIC_DEMO_READINESS.md`, `docs/demo/NATIVE_DESKTOP_PACKAGE_SCREENSHOT_READINESS.md`, `docs/demo/PUBLIC_DEMO_TOUCH_AND_FEEL_READINESS.md`, `CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md`, `CONTRIBUTING.md`, `SECURITY.md`, and `SUPPORT.md`. Ambiguous material path count was zero.
  - **Public-surface classification:** `README.md`, `docs/README.md`, and `docs/public/INDEX.md` are public front doors. `docs/public/EXTERNAL_REVIEW_PACKAGE.md`, `docs/public/QSL_SERVER_ATTACHMENTS_PRODUCTION_BOUNDARY_PLAN.md`, `docs/public/RELEASE_READINESS_EVIDENCE_MAP.md`, `docs/public/SUITE2_TRIPLE_RATCHET_CLAIM_BOUNDARY.md`, and the 13 `docs/demo/*.md` public demo/reviewer files are public evidence pages. `CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md`, `CONTRIBUTING.md`, `SECURITY.md`, `SUPPORT.md`, `docs/public/EXTERNAL_WEBSITE_IMPLEMENTATION_DIRECTIVE.md`, `docs/public/PUBLIC_ATTENTION_AND_VISIBILITY_STRATEGY.md`, `docs/public/WEBSITE_CLAIM_MATRIX.md`, `docs/public/WEBSITE_IMPLEMENTATION_HANDOFF.md`, and `docs/public/WEBSITE_UPDATE_PLAN.md` are public claim/reference pages. `docs/public/PUBLIC_ALLOWLIST_INVENTORY.md`, `docs/public/PUBLIC_EXPORT_MANIFEST.md`, `docs/public/PUBLIC_RELEASE_RUNBOOK.md`, and `docs/public/PUBLIC_WORKSPACE_AND_NAMING.md` are historical public records. No public progress/status page currently exists.
  - **Public accuracy baseline:** Classified public links resolved. Future factual correction candidates were recorded for stale current-main/public-safety/advisory/cargo-audit/recent-PR references in `docs/public/RELEASE_READINESS_EVIDENCE_MAP.md` and `docs/public/EXTERNAL_REVIEW_PACKAGE.md`. A claim-safety correction candidate was recorded for adding Progress-specific permitted/forbidden wording and same-entry qualifiers to `docs/public/WEBSITE_CLAIM_MATRIX.md`. Structural Progress architecture work was recorded for `README.md`, `docs/README.md`, `docs/public/INDEX.md`, new `docs/public/PROGRESS.md`, and new `docs/public/progress/2026-06-25.md`.
  - **Correction policies selected:** Same-day factual corrections and claim-safety corrections are authorized for NA-0541 on the exact path bundle only. Structural changes require separate authorization except the exact Progress architecture selected here.
  - **Public information architecture:** NA-0541 may add a README latest Progress panel, a `docs/public/INDEX.md` Current Progress section, a canonical `docs/public/PROGRESS.md` index, a dated `docs/public/progress/2026-06-25.md` entry, and a `docs/README.md` Progress navigation link. Repository docs remain the source of truth; website mirror work is deferred until an exact website source/deployment path is authorized.
  - **Exact NA-0541 path bundle:** NA-0541 may mutate only `CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md`, `CONTRIBUTING.md`, `README.md`, `SECURITY.md`, `SUPPORT.md`, `docs/README.md`, `docs/demo/ATTACHMENT_PUBLIC_DEMO_READINESS.md`, `docs/demo/CLEAN_HOST_REVIEWER_REPRODUCTION.md`, `docs/demo/CROSS_HOST_DEMO_STRESS_REPRODUCIBILITY.md`, `docs/demo/CROSS_HOST_PRIVATE_NETWORK_SOAK.md`, `docs/demo/CROSS_HOST_PUBLIC_DEMO_REPRODUCIBILITY.md`, `docs/demo/DEMO-PUBLIC-001_Metadata_Visibility.md`, `docs/demo/DEMO_ACCEPTANCE_CRITERIA.md`, `docs/demo/DEMO_ADVERSARIAL_STRESS_TESTING.md`, `docs/demo/DEMO_SOAK_REPEATED_RUN_STABILITY.md`, `docs/demo/DESKTOP_SIDECAR_ADVERSARIAL_STRESS.md`, `docs/demo/KT_NEGATIVE_PUBLIC_DEMO_READINESS.md`, `docs/demo/NATIVE_DESKTOP_PACKAGE_SCREENSHOT_READINESS.md`, `docs/demo/PUBLIC_DEMO_TOUCH_AND_FEEL_READINESS.md`, `docs/public/EXTERNAL_REVIEW_PACKAGE.md`, `docs/public/EXTERNAL_WEBSITE_IMPLEMENTATION_DIRECTIVE.md`, `docs/public/INDEX.md`, `docs/public/PROGRESS.md`, `docs/public/PUBLIC_ALLOWLIST_INVENTORY.md`, `docs/public/PUBLIC_ATTENTION_AND_VISIBILITY_STRATEGY.md`, `docs/public/PUBLIC_EXPORT_MANIFEST.md`, `docs/public/PUBLIC_RELEASE_RUNBOOK.md`, `docs/public/PUBLIC_WORKSPACE_AND_NAMING.md`, `docs/public/QSL_SERVER_ATTACHMENTS_PRODUCTION_BOUNDARY_PLAN.md`, `docs/public/RELEASE_READINESS_EVIDENCE_MAP.md`, `docs/public/SUITE2_TRIPLE_RATCHET_CLAIM_BOUNDARY.md`, `docs/public/WEBSITE_CLAIM_MATRIX.md`, `docs/public/WEBSITE_IMPLEMENTATION_HANDOFF.md`, `docs/public/WEBSITE_UPDATE_PLAN.md`, `docs/public/progress/2026-06-25.md`, `docs/governance/evidence/NA-0541_qsl_daily_public_progress_section_first_end_of_day_update_site_accuracy_implementation_harness.md`, `tests/NA-0541_qsl_daily_public_progress_section_first_end_of_day_update_site_accuracy_implementation_testplan.md`, `DECISIONS.md`, `TRACEABILITY.md`, and `docs/ops/ROLLING_OPERATIONS_JOURNAL.md`.
  - **No wildcard mutation authority:** No wildcard such as `docs/public/**` is authorized. NA-0541 may not add a path merely because it is discovered later. Public-facing issues outside the exact bundle must be recorded and deferred.
  - **First entry scope:** NA-0541 may publish the June 25, 2026 workday entry, using implementation-time verification, covering NA-0537, NA-0538, NA-0539, PR #1348 through #1352, D-1065 through D-1069 as applicable, README/docs/public evidence sync, and NA-0540 authorization if merged. Operator-local SSD maintenance context may be mentioned only as operator-local context, not protocol/security evidence.
  - **Evidence eligibility rules:** Eligible evidence includes merged PRs, accepted decisions, in-tree evidence/testplans, verified public-safety/advisories state, truthful terminal STOP responses, verified operator-local maintenance context clearly labeled as such, and verified factual/claim-safety corrections made on authorized public paths. Ineligible evidence includes unmerged branch work presented as complete, speculation, raw proof logs, private material, route-token/capability material, raw SSH config, `authorized_keys`, `known_hosts`, backup material, detailed private topology, incomplete checks represented as green, and structural redesign presented as factual correction.
  - **Claim policy:** Permitted wording must stay bounded, such as "QSL has bounded engineering evidence for..." and "These results are engineering evidence, not production readiness." Forbidden wording includes public-ready, production-ready, release-ready, replay-proof, downgrade-proof, vulnerability-free, bug-free, perfect-crypto, identity/trust/security-complete, and external-review-complete claims. Every latest-summary panel, dated entry, and corrected public section must preserve applicable no public-readiness, no production-readiness, no public-internet-readiness, no external-review-complete, no crypto-complete, no identity-complete, no trust-complete, no replay-proof, no downgrade-proof, no secret-material-complete, no side-channel-free, no vulnerability-free, no bug-free, and no perfect-crypto boundaries.
  - **Correction and retention policy:** Historical Progress entries must not be silently rewritten; factual corrections use a dated Correction section. Dated entries are retained in-tree. Raw proof roots/logs are never copied into public docs.
  - **Review checklist:** Future daily updates must verify actual current lane/handoff, merged or clearly identified PR state, decision IDs, evidence links, no raw logs, no private material, no topology or route-token leakage, no qsl-server/qsl-attachments implication, same-entry no-claim boundaries, latest links, authorized public path scans, stale status/reference/link/claim checks, verified corrections, deferred out-of-scope issues, link-check, claim scan, and public-safety.
  - **Manual process and automation:** Initial execution is manual and queue-driven. Codex may implement only under fresh qwork proof and an exact directive. Codex does not independently run qwork. No cron, systemd, GitHub Actions automation, or website mirror is authorized by NA-0540 or NA-0541; future automation requires a separate lane.
  - **SSD/shared-target governance:** SSD/shared-target governance remains deferred, not rejected, after operator-local cleanup and active qbuild SSD maintenance timer context.
  - **No implementation in NA-0540:** NA-0540 performs no public content implementation, no public correction implementation, no README/docs/public mutation, no local-ops mutation, no qwork/qstart/qresume execution, no qsl-backup execution, no qsl-server/qsl-attachments use, no qsc source/test/fuzz/Cargo mutation, no dependency/lockfile mutation, no workflow/script/helper mutation, no `public/` or `website/` creation, and no automation installation.
  - **Security invariants introduced/changed:**
    - Daily public Progress is stable-handoff-only.
    - Daily public Progress is site-accuracy-coupled; updating only the latest entry while leaving stale public surfaces is not allowed.
    - No wildcard public mutation authority is allowed.
    - Internal/governance/proof/test material is not promoted publicly merely because it is linked.
    - No public-readiness claim is made.
    - No production-readiness claim is made.
    - No public-internet-readiness claim is made.
    - No external-review-complete claim is made.
    - No crypto-complete claim is made.
    - No identity-complete claim is made.
    - No trust-complete claim is made.
    - No replay-proof claim is made.
    - No downgrade-proof claim is made.
    - No secret-material-complete claim is made.
    - No side-channel-free claim is made.
    - No vulnerability-free claim is made.
    - No bug-free claim is made.
    - No perfect-crypto claim is made.
    - Exactly one READY remains mandatory.
  - **Alternatives considered:**
    - README latest panel plus docs/public current panel plus Progress archive plus site-wide accuracy sweep (selected).
    - README and INDEX only with accuracy sweep (rejected because no durable dated archive).
    - PROGRESS.md only with accuracy sweep (rejected because front-page visibility is too weak).
    - Daily entry without site-wide accuracy sweep (rejected because it creates stale/contradictory public surfaces).
    - Immediate automated nightly publishing (rejected/deferred as premature).
    - Website mirror now (deferred until exact website source/deployment path is authorized).
    - No daily public progress (rejected because the operator prioritized public visibility).
  - **Implications for spec/impl/tests:**
    - `docs/governance/evidence/NA-0540_qsl_daily_public_progress_update_cadence_authorization_plan.md` records qwork proof, D452/D451 inheritance, surface discovery/classification, accuracy baseline, cadence, stable handoff, correction policies, public architecture, daily template, first-entry scope, exact future path bundle, red-team reviews, no-implementation boundary, SSD deferral, and recommendation.
    - `tests/NA-0540_qsl_daily_public_progress_update_cadence_authorization_testplan.md` records required markers, scope guard, queue/decision proof, no public implementation/correction assertions, no local-ops mutation assertions, no qsl-server/qsl-attachments assertions, no-claim assertions, and focused qsc runtime-test skip rationale.
    - `TRACEABILITY.md` maps NA-0540 to D-1070 and the selected NA-0541 implementation successor.
  - **Selected successor:** `NA-0541 -- QSL Daily Public Progress Section, First End-of-Day Update, and Site-Wide Accuracy Sweep Implementation Harness`.
  - **References:** NA-0540; NA-0541; D-1070; D-1069; D-1068; D452 response `/home/victor/work/qsl/codex/responses/NA0539_closeout_restore_na0540_20260625T214828Z_D452.md`; D451 response `/home/victor/work/qsl/codex/responses/NA0539_20260625T200840Z_D451.md`; D450 response `/home/victor/work/qsl/codex/responses/NA0538_closeout_restore_na0539_20260625T162258Z_D450.md`; D449 response `/home/victor/work/qsl/codex/responses/NA0538_20260625T150352Z_D449.md`; D446 response `/home/victor/work/qsl/codex/responses/NA0537_peer_label_recover_retry_20260625T030317Z_D446.md`; `docs/governance/evidence/NA-0540_qsl_daily_public_progress_update_cadence_authorization_plan.md`; `tests/NA-0540_qsl_daily_public_progress_update_cadence_authorization_testplan.md`; `TRACEABILITY.md`; `docs/ops/ROLLING_OPERATIONS_JOURNAL.md`

- **ID:** D-1071
  - **Title:** NA-0540 closeout and NA-0541 restoration
  - **Date:** 2026-06-25
  - **Status:** Accepted
  - **Goal IDs:** G1, G2, G3, G4, G5
  - **Decision:** D-1070 authorization evidence is accepted after PR #1353 merged as `ea38f150368e` and the post-merge `advisories` and `public-safety` checks completed success. NA-0540 is marked DONE. `NA-0541 -- QSL Daily Public Progress Section, First End-of-Day Update, and Site-Wide Accuracy Sweep Implementation Harness` is restored as the sole READY successor using the exact D-1070 path bundle and no wildcard mutation authority.
  - **Evidence consumed:** D-1070 records classification `DAILY_PUBLIC_PROGRESS_SITE_ACCURACY_IMPLEMENTATION_READY`, the daily end-of-day Progress cadence, the approximately 21:30 America/Chicago target, stable-handoff-only publication, site-wide public accuracy sweep, factual and claim-safety correction policies, structural-change boundary, public information architecture, June 25 first-entry scope, evidence eligibility, claim boundaries, retention policy, manual/queue-driven execution, automation deferral, SSD/shared-target governance deferral, no public implementation in NA-0540, no public correction implementation in NA-0540, no local-ops mutation, no qwork/qstart/qresume execution, no qsl-backup execution, no qsl-server/qsl-attachments use, and no public/production/security-completion claims. PR #1353 merged at `ea38f150368e`; post-merge public-safety and advisories completed success for that merge commit.
  - **Closeout result:** This closeout performs no NA-0541 implementation, no README/docs/public mutation, no public Progress content update, no public correction implementation, no local-ops script/timer/systemd mutation, no qwork/qstart/qresume execution by Codex, no qsl-backup execution, no qsc source/test/fuzz/Cargo mutation, no dependency/lockfile mutation, no workflow/script/helper mutation, no corpus/vector/input mutation, no formal/refimpl/service/public/backup mutation, no `public/` or `website/` path creation, and no qsl-server/qsl-attachments use.
  - **Restored successor scope:** The NA-0541 block in `NEXT_ACTIONS.md` repeats the exact D-1070 mutation path bundle and required validation markers. No placeholder, wildcard, or later-discovery path authority is introduced.
  - **Security invariants introduced/changed:**
    - NA-0540 is DONE.
    - NA-0541 is READY as the first implementation successor for the daily public Progress architecture and site-wide accuracy sweep.
    - Exactly one READY remains mandatory.
    - NA-0541 must use implementation-time repo, PR, decision, check, and handoff state.
    - NA-0541 may correct verified factual and claim-safety issues only inside the exact D-1070 path bundle.
    - Structural changes remain separately authorized unless they are the exact D-1070 Progress architecture.
    - No wildcard public mutation authority is introduced.
    - No internal/private material is promoted.
    - No public-readiness claim is made.
    - No production-readiness claim is made.
    - No public-internet-readiness claim is made.
    - No external-review-complete claim is made.
    - No crypto-complete claim is made.
    - No identity-complete claim is made.
    - No trust-complete claim is made.
    - No replay-proof claim is made.
    - No downgrade-proof claim is made.
    - No secret-material-complete claim is made.
    - No side-channel-free claim is made.
    - No vulnerability-free claim is made.
    - No bug-free claim is made.
    - No perfect-crypto claim is made.
  - **Alternatives considered:**
    - Defer closeout after PR #1353 (rejected because post-merge advisories and public-safety reached green inside the allowed short window).
    - Implement NA-0541 during this closeout (rejected because this closeout only restores the successor).
    - Restore a narrower successor than D-1070 selected (rejected because the future daily sweep must cover the exact public-facing surface classified by NA-0540).
    - Add wildcard `docs/public/**` authority (rejected because D-1070 requires individually named paths only).
  - **Implications for spec/impl/tests:**
    - `NEXT_ACTIONS.md` marks NA-0540 DONE and restores NA-0541 READY with the exact D-1070 path bundle.
    - `tests/NA-0540_closeout_restore_na0541_testplan.md` records PR #1353 merge proof, D-1070 acceptance, post-merge advisories/public-safety proof, exact path-bundle restoration, no NA-0541 implementation, no public-doc mutation, no local-ops mutation, no qsl-server/qsl-attachments use, no public/production/security-completion claims, and exactly-one-READY proof.
    - `TRACEABILITY.md` maps D-1070, D-1071, PR #1353, and the restored NA-0541 successor.
  - **Selected successor:** `NA-0541 -- QSL Daily Public Progress Section, First End-of-Day Update, and Site-Wide Accuracy Sweep Implementation Harness`.
  - **References:** NA-0540; NA-0541; D-1071; D-1070; PR #1353; merge `ea38f150368e`; QSL-DIR-2026-06-25-453; `NEXT_ACTIONS.md`; `TRACEABILITY.md`; `docs/ops/ROLLING_OPERATIONS_JOURNAL.md`; `tests/NA-0540_closeout_restore_na0541_testplan.md`; `docs/governance/evidence/NA-0540_qsl_daily_public_progress_update_cadence_authorization_plan.md`; `tests/NA-0540_qsl_daily_public_progress_update_cadence_authorization_testplan.md`

- **ID:** D-1072
  - **Title:** NA-0541 daily public Progress section, first end-of-day update, and site-wide accuracy sweep implementation
  - **Date:** 2026-06-25
  - **Status:** Accepted
  - **Goal IDs:** G1, G2, G3, G4, G5
  - **Decision:** NA-0541 consumes D453/D452/D451/D446 inheritance and implements the D-1070-authorized public Progress architecture and site-wide public accuracy sweep. Classification is `DAILY_PUBLIC_PROGRESS_SITE_ACCURACY_IMPLEMENTATION_PASS`. The June 25, 2026 dated Progress entry is published with publication timestamp `2026-06-25T18:46:42-0500 America/Chicago` and publication-time handoff wording: "At publication, NA-0541 is implementing the first public Progress entry and accuracy sweep; governance closeout to the next lane is pending."
  - **Exact changed paths:** `README.md`; `docs/README.md`; `docs/public/INDEX.md`; `docs/public/PROGRESS.md`; `docs/public/progress/2026-06-25.md`; `docs/public/EXTERNAL_REVIEW_PACKAGE.md`; `docs/public/RELEASE_READINESS_EVIDENCE_MAP.md`; `docs/public/WEBSITE_CLAIM_MATRIX.md`; `docs/governance/evidence/NA-0541_qsl_daily_public_progress_section_first_end_of_day_update_site_accuracy_implementation_harness.md`; `tests/NA-0541_qsl_daily_public_progress_section_first_end_of_day_update_site_accuracy_implementation_testplan.md`; `DECISIONS.md`; `TRACEABILITY.md`; `docs/ops/ROLLING_OPERATIONS_JOURNAL.md`.
  - **Public scan result:** All D-1070 public-facing paths were scanned. The Progress architecture was implemented in README, docs public index, the canonical Progress index, and the June 25 dated entry. Verified stale public references were corrected in `docs/public/EXTERNAL_REVIEW_PACKAGE.md`, `docs/public/RELEASE_READINESS_EVIDENCE_MAP.md`, and `docs/public/WEBSITE_CLAIM_MATRIX.md`. Progress-specific permitted/forbidden wording and qualifiers were added to `docs/public/WEBSITE_CLAIM_MATRIX.md`.
  - **Corrections and deferrals:** Factual corrections refreshed stale current-main, public-safety, advisory, dependency-health, and recent-PR references. Claim-safety corrections added Progress boundaries and no-claim qualifiers. No material out-of-scope factual or claim-safety correction blocked implementation. Historical demo-runbook path sanitization was documented as broader structural docs hygiene rather than a same-day factual or claim-safety correction.
  - **Security invariants introduced/changed:**
    - Progress entries are engineering evidence summaries and correction ledgers, not release certificates.
    - Public Progress does not promote internal/private material or raw proof logs.
    - No `public/` or `website/` path is created.
    - No automation, cron, systemd, timer, workflow, script, helper, dependency, lockfile, qsc source/test/fuzz/Cargo, local-ops, backup, qsl-server, qsl-attachments, remote action, qwork, or qsl-backup mutation is introduced.
    - qsl-server and qsl-attachments remain deferred and are not implied as integrated production services.
    - No public-readiness claim is made.
    - No production-readiness claim is made.
    - No public-internet-readiness claim is made.
    - No external-review-complete claim is made.
    - No crypto-complete claim is made.
    - No identity-complete claim is made.
    - No trust-complete claim is made.
    - No replay-proof claim is made.
    - No downgrade-proof claim is made.
    - No secret-material-complete claim is made.
    - No side-channel-free claim is made.
    - No vulnerability-free claim is made.
    - No bug-free claim is made.
    - No perfect-crypto claim is made.
    - Exactly one READY remains mandatory.
  - **Alternatives considered:**
    - Publish only the dated entry without a public index/panels (rejected because D-1070 selected the full Progress information architecture).
    - Correct only the three expected candidate files and skip the site-wide scan (rejected because D-1070 requires every authorized public path to be scanned).
    - Rewrite historical demo runbooks to remove all old local path examples (deferred because that is broader structural documentation hygiene and could damage reproduction context without a separate lane).
    - Defer Progress publication until external website deployment exists (rejected because D-1070 authorizes repository public docs only and explicitly defers website deployment).
  - **Implications for spec/impl/tests:**
    - `docs/public/PROGRESS.md` is the canonical public Progress index.
    - `docs/public/progress/2026-06-25.md` is the first dated public Progress entry.
    - `docs/governance/evidence/NA-0541_qsl_daily_public_progress_section_first_end_of_day_update_site_accuracy_implementation_harness.md` records qwork proof, inheritance, inventory, scan, corrections, Progress architecture, reviews, validation, SSD context, backup impact, and recommendation.
    - `tests/NA-0541_qsl_daily_public_progress_section_first_end_of_day_update_site_accuracy_implementation_testplan.md` records required markers and validation gates.
    - `TRACEABILITY.md` maps NA-0541 to D-1072 and the selected NA-0542 successor.
  - **Selected successor:** `NA-0542 -- QSL Local Ops SSD Hygiene / Shared Cargo Target Authorization Plan`.
  - **References:** NA-0541; NA-0540; D-1072; D-1071; D-1070; D-1069; D-1068; D453 response `/home/victor/work/qsl/codex/responses/NA0540_20260625T230241Z_D453.md`; D452 response `/home/victor/work/qsl/codex/responses/NA0539_closeout_restore_na0540_20260625T214828Z_D452.md`; D451 response `/home/victor/work/qsl/codex/responses/NA0539_20260625T200840Z_D451.md`; D446 response `/home/victor/work/qsl/codex/responses/NA0537_peer_label_recover_retry_20260625T030317Z_D446.md`; `docs/public/PROGRESS.md`; `docs/public/progress/2026-06-25.md`; `docs/public/WEBSITE_CLAIM_MATRIX.md`; `docs/governance/evidence/NA-0541_qsl_daily_public_progress_section_first_end_of_day_update_site_accuracy_implementation_harness.md`; `tests/NA-0541_qsl_daily_public_progress_section_first_end_of_day_update_site_accuracy_implementation_testplan.md`; `TRACEABILITY.md`; `docs/ops/ROLLING_OPERATIONS_JOURNAL.md`

- **ID:** D-1073
  - **Title:** NA-0541 closeout and NA-0542 restoration
  - **Date:** 2026-06-25
  - **Status:** Accepted
  - **Goal IDs:** G1, G2, G3, G4, G5
  - **Decision:** NA-0541 implementation evidence is accepted after PR #1355 merged as `7060fc40b57b` and post-merge `public-safety` and `advisories` completed success. NA-0541 is marked DONE. `NA-0542 -- QSL Local Ops SSD Hygiene / Shared Cargo Target Authorization Plan` is restored as the sole READY successor.
  - **Evidence consumed:** D-1072 records classification `DAILY_PUBLIC_PROGRESS_SITE_ACCURACY_IMPLEMENTATION_PASS`, the README/docs/public Progress architecture, the June 25, 2026 dated Progress entry, all D-1070 public-facing paths scanned, verified factual and claim-safety corrections, no material out-of-scope correction blocker, no internal/private material promoted, no raw proof logs, no public/website path creation, no automation, no local-ops mutation, no qsc source/test/fuzz/Cargo mutation, no dependency/lockfile mutation, no workflow/script/helper mutation, no qsl-server/qsl-attachments use, no remote action, no qwork/qsl-backup execution, and no public/production/security-completion claims.
  - **Restored successor scope:** NA-0542 is authorization/planning only. It may review the operator-installed qbuild SSD maintenance script/timer and qwork/qbuild configuration read-only, design cleanup/archive/retention/logging/pressure thresholds, design shared Cargo target policy, select exact later implementation paths, and choose a successor. It must not mutate local maintenance, systemd, qwork/qbuild, backup, qsc runtime/source, dependencies, workflows, public content, qsl-server, or qsl-attachments.
  - **Closeout result:** This closeout performs no NA-0542 implementation, no local-ops mutation, no public-content mutation, no qwork/qstart/qresume execution, no qsl-backup execution, no qsc source/test/fuzz/Cargo mutation, no dependency/lockfile mutation, no workflow/script/helper mutation, no remote action, and no qsl-server/qsl-attachments use.
  - **Security invariants introduced/changed:**
    - NA-0541 is DONE.
    - NA-0542 is READY as the sole successor.
    - Exactly one READY remains mandatory.
    - No NA-0542 implementation is performed by closeout.
    - No public-readiness claim is made.
    - No production-readiness claim is made.
    - No public-internet-readiness claim is made.
    - No external-review-complete claim is made.
    - No crypto-complete claim is made.
    - No identity-complete claim is made.
    - No trust-complete claim is made.
    - No replay-proof claim is made.
    - No downgrade-proof claim is made.
    - No vulnerability-free claim is made.
    - No bug-free claim is made.
    - No perfect-crypto claim is made.
  - **Alternatives considered:**
    - Stop after implementation merge (rejected because post-merge public-safety and advisories reached success and no material out-of-scope correction blocked closeout).
    - Implement NA-0542 during closeout (rejected because closeout only restores the successor).
    - Restore a different successor (rejected because D-1072 selected NA-0542 and the directive provided the exact successor block).
  - **Implications for spec/impl/tests:**
    - `NEXT_ACTIONS.md` marks NA-0541 DONE and restores NA-0542 READY.

    - `TRACEABILITY.md` maps D-1072, D-1073, PR #1355, and the restored NA-0542 successor.

- **ID:** D-1074
  - **Title:** NA-0542 local ops SSD hygiene and shared Cargo target authorization
  - **Date:** 2026-06-26
  - **Status:** Accepted
  - **Goal IDs:** G1, G2, G3, G4, G5
  - **Decision:** NA-0542 consumes D454/D453/D452 inheritance and operator cleanup context, inventories the installed qbuild SSD maintenance script/timer read-only, classifies the scheduled maintenance state, inventories qbuild storage/cache state, reviews current maintenance safety, discovers qwork/qbuild architecture read-only, selects local SSD retention/pressure/logging policy, selects an ordinary shared Cargo target design, preserves proof-root-isolated target exceptions, and authorizes the exact NA-0543 implementation successor. Classification is `LOCAL_OPS_SSD_HYGIENE_SHARED_TARGET_IMPLEMENTATION_READY`. The selected successor is `NA-0543 -- QSL Local Ops SSD Hygiene / Shared Cargo Target Implementation Harness`.
  - **Evidence consumed:** D454 recorded implementation PR #1355 merged at `7060fc40b57b`, closeout PR #1356 merged at `dedd73199619`, classification `DAILY_PUBLIC_PROGRESS_SITE_ACCURACY_IMPLEMENTATION_PASS`, public-safety and advisories success, first dated public Progress entry for June 25, 2026, and NA-0542 restored as sole READY. D453/D452 inheritance and the June 25 public Progress entry were reviewed. Operator cleanup context was consumed: root usage fell from 95% to about 37%, old per-lane Rust targets were removed, 1,133 old proof roots were archived to `/backup/qsl`, path-preserving symlinks were retained, and the nightly maintenance script/timer was installed as operator-owned local state.
  - **Installed maintenance inventory:** `/usr/local/sbin/qbuild-ssd-maintenance` is root:root mode 755, SHA-256 `53e9820f0ece`, default dry-run, service apply-mode ExecStart with 7-day target/proof-root ages and warn-percent 90, `/backup/qsl` mount guard, flock, active-process guard, proof-root archive to `/backup/qsl/qbuild-tmp-archive/YYYY/MM`, logs on `/backup/qsl`, and `/srv/qbuild/cache` exclusion. Service runs as root by default, has empty `SuccessExitStatus`, infinite timeout, and mount condition only. Timer is enabled/active, non-persistent, scheduled 03:30 America/Chicago with 20 minute randomized delay.
  - **Scheduled run classification:** `MAINTENANCE_NO_SCHEDULED_RUN_YET`. The timer has no last trigger and the service journal has no entries. The latest housekeeping log is an operator/manual apply run that archived 1,133 proof roots, deleted zero targets, and ended with `QBUILD_SSD_MAINTENANCE_OK`; it is not represented as a scheduled timer success.
  - **Storage/cache inventory:** `/srv/qbuild/work` is about 52G with 292 lane work directories. There are 28 remaining per-lane `qsl-protocol/target` directories totaling about 43GB. `/srv/qbuild/tmp` is about 65G with 150 live directories, 1,133 archived symlinks, and zero broken symlinks. `/srv/qbuild/cache` is about 21G; `/srv/qbuild/cache/targets/qsl-protocol` is about 17G. `/backup/qsl/qbuild-tmp-archive` is about 37G. Current disk usage is `/` 37% and `/backup/qsl` 33%.
  - **Current maintenance safety review:** The installed script is fail-closed enough for observed operator use but needs implementation hardening. Candidate age must use newest descendant mtime, not only top-level directory mtime. Active-process detection must avoid self-match and path-regex ambiguity and should consider lane/worktree recency plus dirty worktrees. Archive transactions must verify copy completeness before source removal and document `.moved.<timestamp>` recovery. systemd should treat intentional active-build skip distinctly from failure while keeping disk warnings non-success. Logs need machine-readable summaries, bounded retention, symlink verification, skipped paths, failed paths, and latest-result markers.
  - **qwork/qbuild architecture:** `qwork` resolves to `/home/victor/.local/bin/qwork`, a wrapper for `/srv/qbuild/tools/qwork.sh`; `qstart`, `qresume`, and `qbuild` were not resolvable in this shell. qwork/qbuild source is operator-owned local state, not tracked by this repository. `/srv/qbuild/tools/env_qbuild.sh` already defines `/srv/qbuild/cache/targets/<repo>` through `qbuild_target_dir`, but current qwork cannot export `CARGO_TARGET_DIR` into the parent shell that later launches Codex and does not create target symlinks or lane-local Cargo config.
  - **Shared Cargo target design:** Select option 1, qwork/qbuild-managed shared SSD target. Selected base is `/srv/qbuild/cache/targets/qsl-protocol`. Selected ordinary current partition is `/srv/qbuild/cache/targets/qsl-protocol/rustc-1.95.0-x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu/default`, tied to the observed cargo/rustc 1.95.0 host class. If NA-0543 observes a different toolchain class before implementation, it must STOP and request refreshed path authorization.
  - **Rejected options:** Lane target symlink is rejected because it confuses provenance and cleanup. Per-lane targets plus cleanup is rejected because it preserves large duplication. Global shell-profile `CARGO_TARGET_DIR` is rejected because it affects unrelated repositories. Shared target on platter is rejected because ordinary builds should remain on SSD. sccache-only is rejected because sccache is not installed and would not remove duplicated target artifacts by itself.
  - **Isolated-target exceptions:** Proof-root-isolated targets remain mandatory for binary provenance/hash evidence, remote binary staging/restaging, reproducibility-sensitive builds, sanitizer/Miri/fuzz/toolchain-specialized runs when collision risk exists, directives explicitly requiring isolated build artifacts, and any case where source-to-binary attribution would otherwise be ambiguous.
  - **Environment precedence:** A directive-provided `CARGO_TARGET_DIR` wins. A preexisting operator-provided `CARGO_TARGET_DIR` wins. The ordinary qwork/qbuild shared default applies only when `CARGO_TARGET_DIR` is unset. qwork must not overwrite explicit target overrides.
  - **Ownership/mode policy:** Parent `/srv/qbuild/cache/targets` may remain root:victor setgid as observed. Repo/class target directories should be victor:victor mode 2775. No global shell profile or committed Cargo config is authorized solely for local qbuild.
  - **Concurrency/locking policy:** One-lane-at-a-time governance and qwork lane locking remain primary. Future shared-target setup should use a repo/class advisory lock for setup/cleanup. Maintenance must skip when cargo/rustc/sccache/qwork/qstart/qresume activity is detected. Shared-target pressure cleanup requires active-process absence, exact path checks, and size reporting.
  - **Pressure thresholds:** Healthy below 80% root usage; warning at or above 80%; pressure at or above 85%; urgent at or above 90%; hard build/directive stop at or above 95%. Warning is report-only. Pressure and urgent cleanup require separately authorized bounded policy.
  - **Cleanup/retention/logging policy:** Normal nightly policy remains 7-day old per-lane target cleanup and 7-day old proof-root archive, `/backup/qsl` mounted, active build/qwork detection required, path-preserving archive links required, shared target excluded from routine age cleanup, and 90-day maintenance-log retention on `/backup/qsl`.
  - **Archive transaction policy:** Future implementation must preserve copy-before-cutover, verify destination mount, detect collisions, copy to archive, verify file count and byte count or rsync dry-diff before source removal, move source to `.moved.<timestamp>`, create and verify original-path symlink, and remove `.moved.<timestamp>` only after verification succeeds.
  - **Operator/Codex boundary:** Codex may mutate tracked files only. Operator-owned local actions include `/srv/qbuild/tools/env_qbuild.sh`, `/srv/qbuild/tools/qwork.sh`, `/home/victor/.local/bin/qwork`, `/usr/local/sbin/qbuild-ssd-maintenance`, `/etc/systemd/system/qbuild-ssd-maintenance.service`, `/etc/systemd/system/qbuild-ssd-maintenance.timer`, `/srv/qbuild/cache/targets/qsl-protocol/rustc-1.95.0-x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu/default`, `/backup/qsl/qbuild-tmp-archive/housekeeping-logs`, and `/backup/qsl/qbuild-local-ops-rollback/NA-0543/pre-change`. Codex must not silently perform operator-owned root/local actions.
  - **Exact future tracked paths:** NA-0543 may mutate only `docs/ops/DOC-OPS-005_qbuild_SSD_Hygiene_and_Shared_Cargo_Target_Runbook_v0.1.0_DRAFT.md`, `docs/ops/NA-0543_qbuild_operator_action_bundle.md`, `scripts/local_ops/qbuild-ssd-maintenance.sh`, `scripts/local_ops/qbuild-shared-target-env.sh`, `docs/governance/evidence/NA-0543_qsl_local_ops_ssd_hygiene_shared_cargo_target_implementation_harness.md`, `tests/NA-0543_qsl_local_ops_ssd_hygiene_shared_cargo_target_implementation_testplan.md`, `DECISIONS.md`, `TRACEABILITY.md`, and `docs/ops/ROLLING_OPERATIONS_JOURNAL.md`.
  - **Exact proof-review outputs for NA-0543:** `maintenance_installation/post_install_inventory.md`, `maintenance_installation/post_install_inventory.json`, `maintenance_run_review/post_install_run_review.md`, `maintenance_run_review/post_install_run_review.json`, `shared_target/post_operator_shared_target_verification.md`, `shared_target/post_operator_shared_target_verification.json`, `rollback/rollback_bundle_inventory.md`, `rollback/rollback_bundle_inventory.json`, `validation/na0543_scope_guard.txt`, and `validation/na0543_marker_proof.txt`.
  - **Rollback plan:** NA-0543 must create pre-change copies under `/backup/qsl/qbuild-local-ops-rollback/NA-0543/pre-change`. Rollback is operator-run only and restores `/usr/local/sbin/qbuild-ssd-maintenance`, the service unit, the timer unit, `/srv/qbuild/tools/env_qbuild.sh`, `/srv/qbuild/tools/qwork.sh`, and `/home/victor/.local/bin/qwork` from that exact rollback directory, followed by operator-run `sudo systemctl daemon-reload` and `sudo systemctl restart qbuild-ssd-maintenance.timer`. Rollback does not delete the shared target directory by default.
  - **No implementation in NA-0542:** NA-0542 performs no local maintenance mutation, no systemd mutation, no qwork/qbuild mutation, no qwork/qstart/qresume execution, no qsl-backup execution, no backup mutation, no maintenance dry-run or apply, no shared target creation, no target symlink creation, no deletion/move/archive/relink, no qsc source/test/fuzz/Cargo mutation, no dependency/lockfile mutation, no workflow mutation, no qsl-server/qsl-attachments use, no public content mutation, no remote command, and no public/production/security-completion claims.
  - **Security invariants introduced/changed:**
    - Local SSD hygiene remains fail-closed under uncertainty.
    - Shared targets are ordinary-build acceleration only, not provenance or reproducibility proof.
    - Explicit target overrides remain authoritative.
    - Proof-root-isolated targets remain mandatory for evidence-sensitive builds.
    - Operator-owned privileged/local mutations require an exact reviewed operator action bundle and later read-only proof review.
    - Exactly one READY remains mandatory.
    - No public-readiness claim is made.
    - No production-readiness claim is made.
    - No public-internet-readiness claim is made.
    - No external-review-complete claim is made.
    - No reproducibility-complete claim is made.
    - No backup/restore-complete claim is made.
    - No vulnerability-free claim is made.
    - No bug-free claim is made.
    - No perfect-build claim is made.
  - **Alternatives considered:**
    - qwork/qbuild-managed shared SSD target (selected because exact ownership and path boundaries are identifiable).
    - Operator-action authorization first, then proof-review only (rejected because tracked runbook/script-template paths can be identified now).
    - Shared-target implementation only and leave maintenance unchanged (rejected because current maintenance needs safety hardening).
    - Maintenance hardening only and defer shared target (rejected because qwork/env architecture is clear enough).
    - Policy-only closeout (rejected because exact paths and ownership are identifiable).
  - **Implications for spec/impl/tests:**
    - `docs/governance/evidence/NA-0542_qsl_local_ops_ssd_hygiene_shared_cargo_target_authorization_plan.md` records qwork proof, inheritance, maintenance inventory, scheduled-run classification, storage inventory, safety review, architecture discovery, shared-target selection, pressure policy, operator/Codex boundary, exact future paths, rollback plan, reviews, claim boundary, and recommendation.
    - `tests/NA-0542_qsl_local_ops_ssd_hygiene_shared_cargo_target_authorization_testplan.md` records required markers, scope guard, no-implementation assertions, no qwork/qsl-backup assertions, no qsc/source/dependency/workflow assertions, no qsl-server/qsl-attachments assertions, no public/production overclaim assertions, and focused qsc runtime-test skip rationale.
    - `TRACEABILITY.md` maps NA-0542 to D-1074 and the selected NA-0543 implementation successor.
  - **Selected successor:** `NA-0543 -- QSL Local Ops SSD Hygiene / Shared Cargo Target Implementation Harness`.
  - **References:** NA-0542; NA-0543; D-1074; D-1073; D-1072; D454 response `/home/victor/work/qsl/codex/responses/NA0541_20260626T001721Z_D454.md`; D453 response `/home/victor/work/qsl/codex/responses/NA0540_20260625T230241Z_D453.md`; D452 response `/home/victor/work/qsl/codex/responses/NA0539_closeout_restore_na0540_20260625T214828Z_D452.md`; `docs/public/PROGRESS.md`; `docs/public/progress/2026-06-25.md`; `docs/governance/evidence/NA-0542_qsl_local_ops_ssd_hygiene_shared_cargo_target_authorization_plan.md`; `tests/NA-0542_qsl_local_ops_ssd_hygiene_shared_cargo_target_authorization_testplan.md`; `TRACEABILITY.md`; `docs/ops/ROLLING_OPERATIONS_JOURNAL.md`

Codex may generate tracked review artifacts only; operator executes privileged/local actions; Codex verifies later read-only.

- **ID:** D-1076
  - **Title:** NA-0543 local ops SSD hygiene and shared Cargo target implementation artifacts
  - **Date:** 2026-06-26
  - **Status:** Accepted
  - **Goal IDs:** G1, G2, G3, G4, G5
  - **Decision:** NA-0543 consumes D455/D454/D453 inheritance plus D-1074/D-1075 and implements the D-1074-authorized tracked local-ops artifacts: canonical qbuild SSD maintenance template, shared Cargo target helper, runbook, dry-run-first operator action bundle, fixture/static validation plan, proof-root proposed qwork/env/wrapper integration files, proof-root proposed systemd units, rollback manifests, evidence, traceability, and rolling journal updates. The result classification is `LOCAL_OPS_SSD_HYGIENE_SHARED_TARGET_OPERATOR_ACTION_BUNDLE_READY`, selecting `NA-0544 -- QSL Local Ops SSD Hygiene / Shared Cargo Target Operator Action Proof Review Harness` as the expected successor after merge and green checks.
  - **Evidence consumed:** D455 recorded PR #1357 merged at `987229c8dc0e`, closeout PR #1358 merged at `8635151e3fdd`, D-1074 authorization accepted, D-1075 restoring NA-0543 READY, scheduled maintenance state `MAINTENANCE_NO_SCHEDULED_RUN_YET`, installed maintenance inventory, qwork/qbuild architecture, exact tracked/operator path bundles, ordinary shared target `/srv/qbuild/cache/targets/qsl-protocol/rustc-1.95.0-x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu/default`, explicit-target precedence, isolated-target exceptions, pressure thresholds, rollback policy, and no implementation in NA-0542.
  - **Latest scheduled maintenance classification:** NA-0543 rechecked installed maintenance read-only. The timer was enabled/active with next run listed, `LastTriggerUSec` remained empty, the service had no execution timestamps, the service journal had no entries, and the latest housekeeping log remained the manual apply run. Classification remained `MAINTENANCE_NO_SCHEDULED_RUN_YET`.
  - **Exact tracked paths changed:** `docs/ops/DOC-OPS-005_qbuild_SSD_Hygiene_and_Shared_Cargo_Target_Runbook_v0.1.0_DRAFT.md`; `docs/ops/NA-0543_qbuild_operator_action_bundle.md`; `scripts/local_ops/qbuild-ssd-maintenance.sh`; `scripts/local_ops/qbuild-shared-target-env.sh`; `docs/governance/evidence/NA-0543_qsl_local_ops_ssd_hygiene_shared_cargo_target_implementation_harness.md`; `tests/NA-0543_qsl_local_ops_ssd_hygiene_shared_cargo_target_implementation_testplan.md`; `DECISIONS.md`; `TRACEABILITY.md`; `docs/ops/ROLLING_OPERATIONS_JOURNAL.md`.
  - **Canonical maintenance script:** `scripts/local_ops/qbuild-ssd-maintenance.sh` defaults to dry-run, requires explicit `--apply`, requires root for live apply, requires `/backup/qsl` mount outside fixture mode, uses nonblocking flock, validates roots/candidates, rejects dangerous destructive inputs, excludes `/srv/qbuild/cache`, uses newest-descendant mtime candidate policy, distinguishes active-build safe skip exit `3`, uses copy/verify/finalize/move/symlink archive transactions, emits human and JSON logs, applies bounded log retention, and supports proof-root-only fixture mode.
  - **Shared-target helper:** `scripts/local_ops/qbuild-shared-target-env.sh` computes or preserves target selection for qsl-protocol, validates repo/toolchain/build-class components, emits shell/JSON/qwork-proof output, preserves explicit `CARGO_TARGET_DIR`, supports directive isolated targets, rejects unrelated repos by default, and creates directories only in explicit prepare mode.
  - **Proposed qwork integration mechanism:** Proof-root proposed copies update env/qwork/wrapper logic to use file-backed propagation. qwork writes `cargo_target_mode`, `cargo_target_dir`, `cargo_target_source`, `cargo_target_build_class`, `cargo_target_toolchain_key`, `explicit_target_preserved`, and `shared_target_ready` into startup proof files and writes a shell-safe environment file under the lane `.qwork` directory. This survives the qwork child-process boundary and does not rely on magical parent-shell export.
  - **Proposed systemd hardening:** Proof-root proposed units add `/backup/qsl` mount condition, exact installed script `ExecStart`, safe-skip/reclaimed `SuccessExitStatus`, bounded timeout, background scheduling, `NoNewPrivileges`, `PrivateTmp`, filesystem protection, explicit writable paths, and no network requirement.
  - **Operator bundle:** `docs/ops/NA-0543_qbuild_operator_action_bundle.md` begins with `DO NOT RUN UNTIL DIRECTOR REVIEW`, is dry-run-first, gives one-step-at-a-time operator commands, names every root-owned action, requires rollback capture under `/backup/qsl/qbuild-local-ops-rollback/NA-0543/pre-change`, lists proof outputs, and includes rollback commands. Codex did not execute the bundle.
  - **Rollback bundle:** Proof-root rollback manifests and the tracked operator bundle specify pre-change capture and restoration of `/usr/local/sbin/qbuild-ssd-maintenance`, the service unit, the timer unit, `/srv/qbuild/tools/env_qbuild.sh`, `/srv/qbuild/tools/qwork.sh`, and `/home/victor/.local/bin/qwork`; rollback does not delete the shared target by default.
  - **Security invariants introduced/changed:**
    - Local SSD maintenance remains fail-closed under ambiguous roots, mounts, active builds, archive collisions, copy verification failure, and missing proof fields.
    - Routine cleanup never deletes `/srv/qbuild/cache` or the shared target.
    - Shared targets are ordinary-build acceleration only, not provenance or reproducibility proof.
    - Explicit target overrides remain authoritative.
    - Proof-root-isolated targets remain mandatory for binary provenance/hash evidence, remote binary staging/restaging, reproducibility-sensitive builds, sanitizer/Miri/fuzz/toolchain-specialized runs where collision risk exists, directives requiring isolated artifacts, and source-to-binary attribution ambiguity.
    - Operator-owned privileged/local mutation remains operator-run only and requires later read-only proof review.
    - Exactly one READY remains mandatory.
    - No public-readiness claim is made.
    - No production-readiness claim is made.
    - No public-internet-readiness claim is made.
    - No external-review-complete claim is made.
    - No reproducibility-complete claim is made.
    - No backup/restore-complete claim is made.
    - No vulnerability-free claim is made.
    - No bug-free claim is made.
    - No perfect-build claim is made.
  - **Operator-owned paths:** `/srv/qbuild/tools/env_qbuild.sh`; `/srv/qbuild/tools/qwork.sh`; `/home/victor/.local/bin/qwork`; `/usr/local/sbin/qbuild-ssd-maintenance`; `/etc/systemd/system/qbuild-ssd-maintenance.service`; `/etc/systemd/system/qbuild-ssd-maintenance.timer`; `/srv/qbuild/cache/targets/qsl-protocol/rustc-1.95.0-x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu/default`; `/backup/qsl/qbuild-tmp-archive/housekeeping-logs`; `/backup/qsl/qbuild-local-ops-rollback/NA-0543/pre-change`.
  - **No operator action executed:** NA-0543 performs no installed/local path mutation, no systemd mutation, no shared-target creation or mutation, no qwork/qstart/qresume execution, no qsl-backup execution, no backup mutation, no maintenance live dry-run/apply, no qsc source/test/fuzz/Cargo mutation, no dependency/lockfile mutation, no workflow mutation, no qsl-server/qsl-attachments use, and no remote command.
  - **Alternatives considered:**
    - Install directly from Codex (rejected because D-1074 keeps privileged/local mutation operator-owned).
    - Use shell profiles or global Cargo config for propagation (rejected because it affects unrelated repos).
    - Use lane target symlinks (rejected because provenance and cleanup ambiguity are unacceptable).
    - Treat shared target as reproducibility proof (rejected; isolated targets remain required for evidence-sensitive work).

- **ID:** D-1077
  - **Title:** NA-0543 closeout and NA-0544 restoration
  - **Date:** 2026-06-26
  - **Status:** Accepted
  - **Goal IDs:** G1, G2, G3, G4, G5
  - **Decision:** NA-0543 closeout is accepted after PR #1359 merged at `717b38ac7d3d92033390a8827cde276fbdfcdf7b`, D-1076 was accepted once, and fresh closeout proof verified the D-1076 implementation classification `LOCAL_OPS_SSD_HYGIENE_SHARED_TARGET_OPERATOR_ACTION_BUNDLE_READY`. NA-0543 is marked DONE. `NA-0544 -- QSL Local Ops SSD Hygiene / Shared Cargo Target Operator Action Proof Review Harness` is restored as exactly one READY successor. No NA-0544 implementation is performed.
  - **Fresh proof consumed:** The closeout consumed fresh qwork proof written at `2026-06-26T18:15:26Z`, verified startup values, clean pre-fetch worktree/index/untracked state, live HEAD/origin-main match, root disk below the hard-stop threshold, queue state READY NA-0543, NA-0542 DONE, NA-0541 DONE, D-1074 once, D-1075 once, D-1076 once, D-1077 absent before patch, D-1078 absent, and duplicate decision count zero.
  - **Current required-check classification:** Current main required-check classification completed for `717b38ac7d3d`. `public-safety` completed success. `advisories` completed success. Every branch-protection required check was green or conclusively satisfied through GitHub API proof. `goal-lint` and `CodeQL` were required PR contexts satisfied by PR #1359 status rollup, while current main push checks exposed the expected direct check-run set and a successful status rollup. No failed branch-protection-required check exists.
  - **Forward-audit non-required failures:** Fresh check-run evidence classified `remote-handshake`, `remote-relay`, and `relay-ui-integration` as failed non-required check-runs, not listed in branch-protection required contexts and not materially relevant to this closeout-only governance scope. They are recorded as forward-audit evidence, not ignored.
  - **Operator bundle and rollback proof:** D-1076 operator bundle and rollback bundle are ready. The operator bundle begins with `DO NOT RUN UNTIL DIRECTOR REVIEW`, is dry-run-first, names the root/local action boundary, lists proof outputs, and includes rollback instructions. Rollback requirements remain operator-run only.
  - **Boundary proof:** This closeout performs no operator action bundle execution, no installed/local path mutation, no systemd mutation, no qwork/qstart/qresume execution by Codex, no qsl-backup execution, no backup mutation, no shared-target creation or mutation, no maintenance live dry-run/apply, no qsc source/test/fuzz/Cargo mutation, no dependency/lockfile mutation, no workflow mutation, no remote action, and no qsl-server/qsl-attachments work.
  - **Claim boundary:** No public-readiness claim is made. No production-readiness claim is made. No public-internet-readiness claim is made. No external-review-complete claim is made. No reproducibility-complete claim is made. No backup/restore-complete claim is made. No vulnerability-free claim is made. No bug-free claim is made. No perfect-build claim is made.
  - **Security invariants introduced/changed:**
    - Exactly one READY remains mandatory.
    - NA-0544 is read-only proof review until a Director-authorized operator action phase has completed or been intentionally declined.
    - Operator-owned privileged/local mutation remains outside Codex closeout authority.
    - Failed non-required remote/relay checks must remain forward-audit evidence and cannot be silently ignored if a future lane touches remote/relay/runtime scope.
  - **Alternatives considered:**
    - Start NA-0544 during closeout (rejected because this directive restores the successor only).
    - Treat non-required failed remote/relay checks as ignored (rejected; they are explicitly recorded as forward-audit evidence).
    - Execute or verify operator-owned local actions during closeout (rejected because NA-0544 is the read-only proof review successor after operator action or intentional decline).
  - **Implications for spec/impl/tests:**
    - `NEXT_ACTIONS.md` marks NA-0543 DONE and restores NA-0544 READY as the sole successor.
    - `tests/NA-0543_closeout_restore_na0544_testplan.md` records merge proof, D-1076 acceptance, fresh qwork proof, required-check classification, public-safety/advisories proof, no failed required checks, forward-audit handling of non-required failures, operator/rollback readiness, D-1077 restoration, no NA-0544 implementation, and closeout boundary markers.
    - `TRACEABILITY.md` maps PR #1359, D-1076, D-1077, implementation artifacts, closeout testplan, and NA-0544 restoration.
    - `docs/ops/ROLLING_OPERATIONS_JOURNAL.md` records the closeout proof and NA-0544 restoration.
  - **Selected successor:** `NA-0544 -- QSL Local Ops SSD Hygiene / Shared Cargo Target Operator Action Proof Review Harness`.
  - **References:** NA-0543; NA-0544; D-1077; D-1076; D-1075; D-1074; PR #1359; merge `717b38ac7d3d92033390a8827cde276fbdfcdf7b`; `NEXT_ACTIONS.md`; `TRACEABILITY.md`; `docs/ops/ROLLING_OPERATIONS_JOURNAL.md`; `tests/NA-0543_closeout_restore_na0544_testplan.md`; `docs/ops/NA-0543_qbuild_operator_action_bundle.md`; `docs/ops/DOC-OPS-005_qbuild_SSD_Hygiene_and_Shared_Cargo_Target_Runbook_v0.1.0_DRAFT.md`; `docs/governance/evidence/NA-0543_qsl_local_ops_ssd_hygiene_shared_cargo_target_implementation_harness.md`; `tests/NA-0543_qsl_local_ops_ssd_hygiene_shared_cargo_target_implementation_testplan.md`

- **ID:** D-1078
  - **Title:** NA-0544 local ops SSD hygiene and shared Cargo target operator action proof review
  - **Date:** 2026-06-26
  - **Status:** Accepted
  - **Goal IDs:** G1, G2, G3, G4, G5
  - **Decision:** NA-0544 accepts the read-only proof review of the human operator action phase authorized by D-1076 and restored by D-1077. The operator action bundle was executed by the human operator, not Codex. Fresh qwork proof from `2026-06-27T00:53:48Z` was verified. Rollback inventory, installed canonical maintenance script, installed service/timer units, installed qwork/env/wrapper files, shared target directories, qwork target-selection proof, explicit-target preservation proof, unrelated-repository rejection proof, post-install dry-run proof, timer state, and operator/Codex boundaries all passed. The result classification is `LOCAL_OPS_SSD_HYGIENE_SHARED_TARGET_OPERATOR_ACTION_PROOF_REVIEW_PASS`.
  - **Evidence consumed:** D-1076 and D-1077 were consumed. D-1076 recorded `LOCAL_OPS_SSD_HYGIENE_SHARED_TARGET_OPERATOR_ACTION_BUNDLE_READY`; D-1077 marked NA-0543 DONE and restored NA-0544 as the sole READY successor. The operator bundle begins with `DO NOT RUN UNTIL DIRECTOR REVIEW` and requires rollback capture, install proof, shared-target proof, qwork proof, explicit-target proof, unrelated-repository rejection proof, dry-run proof, timer proof, disk proof, and no-qsl-backup boundary proof.
  - **Fresh qwork proof:** Verified startup `.kv`, JSON, and cargo-target env files under `/srv/qbuild/work/NA-0544/.qwork`. Required values included lane NA-0544, repo `qsl-protocol`, path `/srv/qbuild/work/NA-0544/qsl-protocol`, HEAD/origin-main/main `b4a64f78efe7`, clean worktree/index/untracked state, READY_COUNT 1, READY NA-0544, `qwork_version_or_sha=175b0ea1d5b9abc07bdab66e9b92446e2a3d533018468e94a95c26f8698f86cf`, `cargo_target_mode=shared`, the ordinary shared target path, `explicit_target_preserved=no`, and `shared_target_ready=yes`.
  - **Installed hashes:** Installed script `/usr/local/sbin/qbuild-ssd-maintenance` matched `84f20f9e3189b8994f378ac8f8a2cde5a99f899496a8b393b7b15800a1149b0c`. Installed service matched `7841add332a4ee37bd53b29e20b5d698c9e3f6014bec65ff98b6fc723b01c089`. Installed timer matched `d50064ce5878a2910bd27c86dfb2631bf3bc94c5e7aee30c564704c94b323102`. Installed env script matched `adf2c6dc8d719ca5b3a27260e8868ad7fde4d32ae98d19fdf8c5f10c3e30020d`. Installed qwork script matched `175b0ea1d5b9abc07bdab66e9b92446e2a3d533018468e94a95c26f8698f86cf`. Installed qwork wrapper matched `97f27ced9daa2444742ba4a881f580c25b5cef1a670d83cb77668c7c4c791ee9` and is a regular mode-755 file, not a symlink.
  - **Rollback hashes:** Rollback inventory under `/backup/qsl/qbuild-local-ops-rollback/NA-0543/pre-change` matched: maintenance script `53e9820f0ece7329864fb5716b9f08fbe64af1f38a775c505a62685422b845c5`; service `d4f37b7ed0eee160ee0593fdddc03b747785d965d5ed0931c6a53a5c84db3d8c`; timer `d50064ce5878a2910bd27c86dfb2631bf3bc94c5e7aee30c564704c94b323102`; env script `621c7d97c685627ef3f921c89161aed57c63a540daa5d408ef9b1956c4ece294`; qwork script `438b81623d3a461213974af1ea3219105aa8c14c988cf2de835f1df240eff742`; old wrapper `9b5d1eb57fac554c0f5ad0d9dc168df86aa3e847565ad6bc3120155dd333e65b`.
  - **Shared target proof:** `/srv/qbuild/cache/targets/qsl-protocol/rustc-1.95.0-x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu/default` and its parent directories exist as real directories, not symlinks, owned `victor:victor` with mode `2775`. qwork proof target dir matches that default directory.
  - **Target-selection proof:** Default helper proof reports shared mode, source `qwork-default`, build class `default`, toolchain key `rustc-1.95.0-x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu`, explicit target not preserved, and shared target ready. Explicit-target helper proof reports explicit mode, source `preexisting-env`, `explicit_target_preserved=yes`, and `shared_target_ready=not-applicable`. Unrelated-repository helper proof exits `2`, emits the expected unauthorized ordinary shared target rejection phrase, and has empty stdout.
  - **Dry-run and timer proof:** Post-install dry-run log/JSON at `/backup/qsl/qbuild-tmp-archive/housekeeping-logs/qbuild-ssd-maintenance_20260627T004638Z.*` reports version `NA-0543-qbuild-ssd-maintenance-v0.1.0`, mode dry-run, classification `DRY_RUN_CANDIDATES`, exit code 0, target candidate count 7, proof candidate count 20, zero broken symlinks, zero deleted, zero archived, zero failed, and zero reclaimed bytes. Latest maintenance state is `MAINTENANCE_CANONICAL_DRY_RUN_PASS`. The timer is enabled and active; service state is inactive/dead with previous `Result=success` and `ExecMainStatus=0`. No scheduled canonical apply after install is visible yet; this is a monitoring follow-up, not a failure.
  - **Boundary proof:** Codex did not run maintenance apply or a live maintenance dry-run, did not run qwork/qstart/qresume, did not run qsl-backup, did not mutate backup state, did not perform sudo/admin action, did not perform systemd mutation, did not mutate qsc source/test/fuzz/Cargo paths, did not mutate dependencies or lockfiles, did not mutate workflows, did not perform remote action, did not use qsl-server or qsl-attachments, and did not make public-readiness, production-readiness, reproducibility-complete, backup/restore-complete, vulnerability-free, bug-free, or perfect-build claims.
  - **Security invariants introduced/changed:**
    - Operator-owned privileged/local state remains operator-run and Codex-review-only unless a later directive explicitly authorizes otherwise.
    - Shared targets remain ordinary-build acceleration only, not provenance or reproducibility proof.
    - Explicit target overrides remain authoritative.
    - Unrelated repositories fail closed for ordinary shared target selection.
    - Dry-run success is not the same as scheduled apply success.
    - Exactly one READY remains mandatory.
  - **Alternatives considered:**
    - Treat operator transcript alone as proof (rejected; current filesystem/log/proof facts were verified read-only).
    - Run qwork or maintenance again from Codex to reproduce proof (rejected; NA-0544 forbids Codex operator actions).
    - Wait for a future scheduled canonical apply before accepting proof review (rejected; timer enabled/active plus canonical dry-run pass is sufficient, with scheduled canonical apply noted as monitoring follow-up).
  - **Implications for spec/impl/tests:**
    - `docs/governance/evidence/NA-0544_qsl_local_ops_ssd_hygiene_shared_cargo_target_operator_action_proof_review.md` records the proof review and claim boundaries.
    - `tests/NA-0544_qsl_local_ops_ssd_hygiene_shared_cargo_target_operator_action_proof_review_testplan.md` records required proof markers and validation gates.
    - `TRACEABILITY.md` maps NA-0544 to D-1078, qwork proof, rollback proof, installed hash proof, target-selection proof, dry-run proof, timer proof, and successor selection.
    - `docs/ops/ROLLING_OPERATIONS_JOURNAL.md` records qwork proof timestamp, main SHA, installed hashes, latest maintenance state, successor selection, no-claim boundary, and Codex/operator boundary.
  - **Selected successor:** `NA-0545 -- QSL Remote/Relay Non-Required CI Failure Forward-Audit Authorization Plan`.
  - **References:** NA-0544; NA-0545; D-1078; D-1077; D-1076; D-1075; D-1074; `docs/ops/NA-0543_qbuild_operator_action_bundle.md`; `docs/ops/DOC-OPS-005_qbuild_SSD_Hygiene_and_Shared_Cargo_Target_Runbook_v0.1.0_DRAFT.md`; `docs/governance/evidence/NA-0544_qsl_local_ops_ssd_hygiene_shared_cargo_target_operator_action_proof_review.md`; `tests/NA-0544_qsl_local_ops_ssd_hygiene_shared_cargo_target_operator_action_proof_review_testplan.md`; `TRACEABILITY.md`; `docs/ops/ROLLING_OPERATIONS_JOURNAL.md`

- **ID:** D-1079

No observed failed required check remained; main merge commit missing PR-only direct contexts was recorded separately and not treated as failure.
  - **Restored successor:** NA-0545 is authorization and triage only for non-required remote/relay CI failures `remote-handshake`, `remote-relay`, and `relay-ui-integration`. It may collect fresh read-only GitHub workflow/check/job/log metadata, inspect current workflow definitions and referenced scripts read-only, classify whether failures still reproduce or are stale/flaky, and select a future exact implementation lane if needed.
  - **Boundary proof:** This closeout performs no NA-0545 implementation, no Codex operator action, no qwork/qstart/qresume execution by Codex, no qsl-backup execution, no backup mutation, no qsc source/dependency/workflow mutation, no remote action, and no qsl-server/qsl-attachments work.

No reproducibility-complete claim is made. No backup/restore-complete claim is made. No vulnerability-free claim is made. No bug-free claim is made. No perfect-build claim is made.
  - **Security invariants introduced/changed:**
    - Exactly one READY remains mandatory.
    - NA-0545 is read-only authorization and triage unless a later exact implementation scope is selected.
    - Failed non-required remote/relay checks remain forward-audit evidence and cannot be silently ignored.
    - Operator/local state remains outside Codex closeout authority.
  - **Alternatives considered:**

- **ID:** D-1080
  - **Title:** NA-0545 remote/relay non-required CI failure forward-audit authorization
  - **Date:** 2026-06-27
  - **Status:** Accepted
  - **Goal IDs:** G1, G2, G3, G4, G5
  - **Decision:** NA-0545 accepts the read-only forward audit for the three non-required remote/relay failures forwarded from NA-0543. D-1078 and D-1079 were consumed. Fresh NA-0545 qwork proof from `2026-06-27T01:53:59Z` was verified from `.kv`, JSON, and cargo-target env files. Current main HEAD and origin/main were verified at `594704571c36`. public-safety completed success. advisories completed success. No failed required check was observed. Branch-protection required contexts were classified, including PR-only `goal-lint` and `CodeQL` satisfaction through PR #1362 rollup. Prior failed run IDs `28222737830`, `28221877145`, and `28221488004` were consumed. Current main has no attached target check-runs for `remote-handshake`, `remote-relay`, or `relay-ui-integration`; relevant workflow/script/test paths are unchanged from the failing `717b38ac7d3d` merge. The overall result classification is `REMOTE_RELAY_FORWARD_AUDIT_REPRODUCTION_AUTHORIZATION_READY`.
  - **Historical target status:** `remote-handshake` run `28222737830` failed in workflow `remote-handshake-tests` on scheduled main at `717b38ac7d3d`; bounded log summary shows a qsc vault-init failure before handshake assertions. Classification: `REMOTE_HANDSHAKE_RUNTIME_SCOPE_LIKELY`. `remote-relay` run `28221877145` failed in workflow `remote-relay-tests`; bounded log summary shows qsc contact-store validation errors and failed happy-path count expectations. Classification: `REMOTE_RELAY_RUNTIME_SCOPE_LIKELY`. `relay-ui-integration` run `28221488004` failed in workflow `relay-ui-integration`; bounded log summary shows local relay health HTTP 404 before ignored UI tests ran. Classification: `RELAY_UI_WORKFLOW_SCOPE_LIKELY`.
  - **Workflow and script inventory:** Current workflow files were identified read-only as `.github/workflows/remote-handshake-tests.yml`, `.github/workflows/remote-relay-tests.yml`, and `.github/workflows/relay-ui-integration.yml`. Referenced in-repo scripts/tests were identified read-only as `scripts/demo/qsc_remote_handshake_smoke.sh`, `scripts/demo/qsc_remote_relay_smoke.sh`, `qsl/qsl-client/qsc/tests/relay_ui_integration.rs`, and `qsl/qsl-client/qsc/tests/common/mod.rs`. The relay UI workflow depends on a fresh qsl-server clone/build during CI; NA-0545 did not clone or run qsl-server.
  - **Log visibility and private-material handling:** Historical failed-step logs were visible through read-only GitHub log access. Only bounded proof-root summaries were saved. No raw logs were copied into repository docs. Saved log summaries passed private-material scan: no unmasked private keys, GitHub tokens, bearer tokens, relay tokens, or authorization header values were found. Masked secret placeholders remained proof-root-only.
  - **Selected successor:** Option B, `NA-0546 -- QSL Remote/Relay Non-Required CI Failure Bounded Reproduction Authorization Plan`. Option A was rejected because exact implementation ownership cannot be selected safely from read-only evidence. Option C was rejected because the target failures are not proven stale/no-fix-required.
  - **Boundary proof:** No implementation mutation occurred. No workflow mutation occurred. No dependency/lockfile mutation occurred. No qsc source/test/fuzz/Cargo mutation occurred. No qsl-server/qsl-attachments use occurred. No remote command occurred. No qwork/qstart/qresume execution by Codex occurred. No qsl-backup execution occurred. No backup mutation occurred. No operator/local system mutation occurred. No public-site mutation occurred. No Cloudflare configuration mutation occurred. No private material was published. No raw logs were copied into repository docs.
  - **Claim boundary:** No public-readiness claim was made. No production-readiness claim was made. No public-internet-readiness claim was made. No external-review-complete claim was made. No reproducibility-complete claim was made. No backup/restore-complete claim was made. No vulnerability-free claim was made. No bug-free claim was made. No perfect-build or perfect-crypto claim was made.
  - **Security invariants introduced/changed:**
    - Exactly one READY remains mandatory.
    - Non-required remote/relay failures must be reproduced or explicitly bounded before any implementation lane selects mutation paths.
    - Read-only log summaries may support classification, but raw logs and endpoint strings are not repository evidence.
    - qsl-server and qsl-attachments remain outside NA-0545 mutation scope.
  - **Alternatives considered:**
    - Select exact implementation successor (rejected because exact future ownership/path bundle is not safely determined).
    - Treat the failures as stale and select public-site verification (rejected because the target workflows did not rerun on current main and relevant target paths are unchanged).
    - Rerun workflows during NA-0545 (rejected because NA-0545 is authorization and triage only).
  - **Implications for spec/impl/tests:**
    - `docs/governance/evidence/NA-0545_remote_relay_non_required_ci_failure_forward_audit_authorization_plan.md` records qwork proof, inheritance, required-check classification, historical run audit, workflow/script inventory, log visibility, classifications, boundaries, and successor selection.
    - `tests/NA-0545_remote_relay_non_required_ci_failure_forward_audit_authorization_testplan.md` records required markers and validation gates.
    - `TRACEABILITY.md` maps NA-0545 to D-1080, the target run IDs, workflow inventory, testplan markers, and selected successor.
    - `docs/ops/ROLLING_OPERATIONS_JOURNAL.md` records qwork proof timestamp, current main SHA, target classifications, selected successor, and no-mutation/no-claim boundaries.

Fresh qwork proof from `2026-06-27T03:21:51Z` was verified from `.kv`, JSON, and cargo-target env files. Current main HEAD and origin/main were verified at `0e4560febcc459add01083e43c24c19d3b19c813`. public-safety completed success. advisories completed success. No failed required check was observed. Branch-protection required contexts were classified, including PR #1364 proof for PR-only `goal-lint` and `CodeQL` contexts. The inherited target failures are `remote-handshake` run `28222737830`, `remote-relay` run `28221877145`, and `relay-ui-integration` run `28221488004`.
  - **Authorization design:** Workflow trigger inventory completed for `.github/workflows/remote-handshake-tests.yml`, `.github/workflows/remote-relay-tests.yml`, and `.github/workflows/relay-ui-integration.yml`. Referenced command inventory completed for `scripts/demo/qsc_remote_handshake_smoke.sh`, `scripts/demo/qsc_remote_relay_smoke.sh`, `qsl/qsl-client/qsc/tests/relay_ui_integration.rs`, and `qsl/qsl-client/qsc/tests/common/mod.rs`. Current-main dispatch authorization design completed and authorizes, for a later lane only, `gh workflow run remote-handshake-tests.yml --ref main`, `gh workflow run remote-relay-tests.yml --ref main -f scenario=happy-path -f seed=1`, and `gh workflow run relay-ui-integration.yml --ref main`. Local reproduction design completed and does not authorize local reproduction.
  - **Boundary classification:** qsl-server/qsl-attachments boundary was classified. remote-handshake and remote-relay have no qsl-server/qsl-attachments involvement. relay-ui-integration has workflow-transitive qsl-server involvement only; future use is limited to GitHub Actions black-box rerun/dispatch of the existing workflow, with no local Codex qsl-server command, clone, build, mutation, or qsl-attachments mutation. Log capture policy, redaction policy, and private-material scan policy were selected. Future NA-0547 classifications were selected.

No perfect-build or perfect-crypto claim was made. Exactly one READY remains mandatory.

- **ID:** D-1084
  - **Title:** NA-0547 remote/relay non-required CI failure bounded reproduction and log capture
  - **Date:** 2026-06-27
  - **Status:** Accepted
  - **Goal IDs:** G1, G2, G3, G4, G5
  - **Decision:** NA-0547 accepts the D-1082-authorized bounded reproduction and log-capture execution. D-1082 and D-1083 were consumed. Fresh qwork proof from `2026-06-27T07:04:10Z` was verified from `.kv`, JSON, and cargo-target env files. Current main HEAD and origin/main were verified at `c37fa6111e46`. public-safety completed success. advisories completed success. No failed required check was observed. Branch-protection required contexts were classified, including PR #1366 rollup proof for PR-only `goal-lint` and aggregate `CodeQL`. The target remote/relay workflows failed on current main as non-required reproduction targets.
  - **Historical rerun proof:** Exact D-1082 historical rerun commands were executed: `gh run rerun 28222737830 --failed`, `gh run rerun 28221877145 --failed`, and `gh run rerun 28221488004 --failed`. Historical rerun evidence was captured for run/job/log metadata. remote-handshake rerun attempt 2 failed in step `Run remote handshake smoke (happy-path seed=1)` and was classified `HISTORICAL_REPRODUCED`. remote-relay rerun attempt 2 failed in step `Run remote relay smoke (manual/nightly)` and was classified `HISTORICAL_REPRODUCED`. relay-ui-integration rerun attempt 2 failed in step `Start local relay and run ignored relay UI integration tests` and was classified `HISTORICAL_REPRODUCED`.
  - **Current-main dispatch proof:** Exact D-1082 current-main dispatch commands were executed: `gh workflow run remote-handshake-tests.yml --ref main`, `gh workflow run remote-relay-tests.yml --ref main -f scenario=happy-path -f seed=1`, and `gh workflow run relay-ui-integration.yml --ref main`. New dispatch runs were uniquely identified as `28298341119`, `28298371731`, and `28298405239`. Current-main dispatch evidence was captured for run/job/check/log metadata. remote-handshake run `28298341119` failed in step `Run remote handshake smoke (happy-path seed=1)`. remote-relay run `28298371731` failed in step `Run remote relay smoke (manual/nightly)`. relay-ui-integration run `28298405239` failed in step `Start local relay and run ignored relay UI integration tests`.
  - **Log and private-material proof:** Raw logs are stored proof-root-only under `/srv/qbuild/tmp/NA0547_remote_relay_reproduction_log_capture_20260627T183232Z/`. Raw logs were reviewed and retained proof-root-only because they contain masked secret/authorization fields and public CI token-like identifiers. Redacted historical and current-main extracts passed private-material scan. Repository docs contain only bounded summaries. No private material was published. No raw logs were copied into repository docs.
  - **Per-target classification:** remote-handshake is `REMOTE_HANDSHAKE_REPRODUCED_CURRENT`; remote-relay is `REMOTE_RELAY_REPRODUCED_CURRENT`; relay-ui-integration is `RELAY_UI_REPRODUCED_CURRENT`.
  - **Overall result classification:** `REMOTE_RELAY_REPRODUCTION_PARTIAL_MORE_EVIDENCE_REQUIRED`. All three targets reproduced on current main, but exact future implementation ownership and mutation path bundle cannot be selected safely from summary evidence alone because the remote smoke failures involve secret-backed relay boundaries and qsc runtime/harness behavior, while relay UI includes workflow-transitive qsl-server behavior.
  - **Selected successor:** `NA-0548 -- QSL Remote/Relay Non-Required CI Failure Follow-Up Evidence Authorization Plan`. The successor is an authorization lane for exact additional GitHub Actions metadata/log capture or rerun permissions, exact log redaction boundaries, and exact stop conditions before any implementation lane. It authorizes no workflow/runtime/dependency/qsc/qsl-server/qsl-attachments mutation.
  - **Boundary proof:** No local reproduction executed. No workflow mutation occurred. No runtime/source mutation occurred. No dependency/lockfile mutation occurred. No qsc source/test/fuzz/Cargo mutation occurred. No qsl-server/qsl-attachments source mutation occurred. No local qsl-server/qsl-attachments command execution occurred. No remote command outside exact GitHub Actions API actions authorized by D-1082 occurred. No qwork/qstart/qresume execution by Codex occurred. No qsl-backup execution occurred. No backup mutation occurred. No public-site mutation occurred. No Cloudflare mutation occurred. Exactly one READY remains mandatory.
  - **Claim boundary:** No public-readiness claim was made. No production-readiness claim was made. No public-internet-readiness claim was made. No external-review-complete claim was made. No reproducibility-complete claim was made. No backup/restore-complete claim was made. No vulnerability-free claim was made. No bug-free claim was made. No perfect-build claim was made. No perfect-crypto claim was made.
  - **Recovered failure:** A read-only preflight metadata command used unsupported `gh workflow view --json` flags. Classification: recoverable command-shape issue. Corrective action: use allowed read-only GitHub REST workflow metadata endpoints. Final result: workflow metadata captured and command gate passed before any rerun or dispatch.
  - **Implications for spec/impl/tests:**
    - `docs/governance/evidence/NA-0547_remote_relay_non_required_ci_failure_bounded_reproduction_log_capture_harness.md` records qwork proof, inheritance, required-check classification, command gate, historical reruns, current-main dispatches, log/private-material review, classifications, successor, and boundaries.
